===== DOC START split=test idx=0 id=story_qa_000041 =====
The cat was fluffy. This was a big characteristic. <definition>Characteristic means something that makes a thing special.</definition> He sat by the fish bowl. An eyeball looked back at him! The fish swam in circles. "I will possibly catch you!" said the cat. He tapped the glass.

<question>What kind of cat was he?</question>
<answer>The cat was fluffy.</answer>

<question>What does characteristic mean?</question>
<answer>Characteristic means something that makes a thing special.</answer>

<question>Where did the cat sit?</question>
<answer>The cat sat by the fish bowl.</answer>

<question>What looked back at the cat?</question>
<answer>An eyeball looked back at him.</answer>

<question>What did the fish do?</question>
<answer>The fish swam in circles.</answer>

<question>What did the cat say he might do?</question>
<answer>He said, "I will possibly catch you!"</answer>

<question>What did the cat do to the glass?</question>
<answer>He tapped the glass.</answer>
===== DOC END =====

===== DOC START split=test idx=1 id=story_qa_000003 =====
The pig lived in a pen. One day, the pig wanted to be free. He saw a hole in the fence. He could go to the other side! He wiggled under the fence. It was a tight fit.

Now he was out! He ran to the mud. He loved to roll in the mud. He was happy to be free. <definition>Free means not being stuck in one place.</definition>

The pig saw other pigs. They were also in the mud. He was not the only one. They all liked to be in the mud together. It was a good day.

<question>Where did the pig live?</question>
<answer>The pig lived in a pen.</answer>

<question>What did the pig want to be?</question>
<answer>The pig wanted to be free.</answer>

<question>What did the pig see in the fence?</question>
<answer>The pig saw a hole.</answer>

<question>How did the pig get out?</question>
<answer>He wiggled under the fence.</answer>

<question>Where did the pig run to?</question>
<answer>He ran to the mud.</answer>

<question>What did the pig love to do in the mud?</question>
<answer>He loved to roll in the mud.</answer>

<question>Was the pig happy to be free?</question>
<answer>Yes, the pig was happy.</answer>

<question>What does free mean?</question>
<answer>Free means not being stuck in one place.</answer>

<question>Who else was in the mud?</question>
<answer>Other pigs were in the mud.</answer>

<question>Did the pigs like being in the mud together?</question>
<answer>Yes, they all liked it.</answer>
===== DOC END =====

===== DOC START split=test idx=4 id=story_qa_000211 =====
Tom wanted to make a new form of music. He played the saxophone in his room. His house is in the northwestern part of town. One day, Tom played his saxophone by the window. The sound floated in the air. A bird heard it and came in to listen. The bird loved the music. Tom was happy. He wanted to share his form of music with his friends. He played in the yard in the morning. The music was loud and fun. Everyone in the neighborhood could hear him in. Tom loved playing his saxophone. It made him smile. The bird stayed in his tree, listening all day long. It was a good day for music and friends.

<question>Where did Tom play his saxophone first?</question>
<answer>Tom played his saxophone in his room.</answer>

<question>Where is Tom's house?</question>
<answer>Tom's house is in the northwestern part of town.</answer>

<question>What did Tom do one day by the window?</question>
<answer>He played his saxophone by the window.</answer>

<question>What heard the music and came to listen?</question>
<answer>A bird heard the music and came to listen.</answer>

<question>Did the bird like the music?</question>
<answer>Yes, the bird loved the music.</answer>

<question>How did Tom feel when the bird listened?</question>
<answer>Tom was happy.</answer>

<question>What did Tom do in the yard?</question>
<answer>He played his saxophone in the yard.</answer>

<question>Was the music loud?</question>
<answer>Yes, the music was loud.</answer>

<question>Did everyone in the neighborhood hear Tom?</question>
<answer>Yes, everyone in the neighborhood could hear him.</answer>

<question>What made Tom smile?</question>
<answer>Playing his saxophone made Tom smile.</answer>

<question>Where did the bird stay?</question>
<answer>The bird stayed in the tree.</answer>

<question>Did the bird listen all day?</question>
<answer>Yes, the bird listened all day long.</answer>

<question>Was it a good day for music and friends?</question>
<answer>Yes, it was a good day for music and friends.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=5 id=story_qa_000369 =====
The little pig lived in a pen. He liked his pen, especially when it rained. The mud was his favorite. He liked to roll in it. He did not like bath time. <definition>Not means the opposite of is.</definition> Mama Pig would say, "Time to get clean!" The little pig would run away. He would hide behind the hay. Mama Pig would always find him. She would carry him to the tub. He did not like the soap. But he loved playing in the bubbles. Bath time was not so bad after all! He even splashed Mama Pig!

<question>Where did the little pig live?</question>
<answer>The little pig lived in a pen.</answer>

<question>What did the pig like?</question>
<answer>The pig liked the mud.</answer>

<answer>He liked to roll in it.</answer>

<answer>He did not like bath time.</answer>

<answer>Mama Pig would say time to get clean.</answer>

<answer>He would hide behind the hay.</answer>

<question>Who would find him?</question>
<answer>Mama Pig would find him.</answer>

<question>Where would Mama Pig carry him?</question>
<answer>She would carry him to the tub.</answer>

<answer>He did not like the soap.</answer>

<question>What did he love in the tub?</question>
<answer>He loved playing in the bubbles.</answer>

<answer>He splashed Mama Pig.</answer>

They made many blue cars. A little girl named Rose visited the factory. She saw all the cars. She picked a red car. She smiled. "I love my car!" she said.

<answer>The toy factory made many cars.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=7 id=story_qa_000615 =====
Tommy liked to watch TV. He loved a series about animals. One day, he asked his mom, "Can I watch the new show?" She smiled and said, "Yes, but you must do your affairs first." Tommy did his chores quickly. 

<definition>Affairs means tasks or things you need to do.</definition> Then he sat down to watch. The series was fun. 

Tommy wondered if he could be a hero like the animals on TV. He thought, "Can I be brave too?" His mom said, "You can be brave when you try new things." Tommy smiled. He knew he could be a hero in his own way. 

He watched more episodes and learned many things. He was happy. He knew that being kind and brave was important. Tommy finished his day feeling good. He was ready for tomorrow's fun adventures.

<question>What did Tommy like to watch?</question>
<answer>Tommy liked to watch TV.</answer>

<question>What kind of series did Tommy love?</question>
<answer>The series was about animals.</answer>

<answer>He asked, "Can I watch the new show?"</answer>

<answer>She said, "Yes, but you must do your affairs first."</answer>

<answer>He sat down to watch TV.</answer>

<answer>The cat wanted to talk.</answer>

<answer>The dog said, "No talk!"</answer>

In her mind, she imagined her shoes taking her on fun adventures. Some days, she would walk in the park and look at the trees. Are you ready to find some new shoes too?

Maybe she would try the slide later. For now, she would swing high! Higher and higher! She felt like she could touch the sky.

===== DOC START split=test idx=11 id=story_qa_000691 =====
Tim liked science. He liked to learn about bugs. One day, he was remembering all the bug names. <definition>Remembering means thinking about something from the past.</definition> He said them in order. First ants, then bees. Suddenly, a big grasshopper jumped by! It landed on Tim's nose! Tim giggled. The grasshopper tickled. It jumped off and flew away. Tim kept naming bugs. He liked bugs a lot. He wanted to learn more about bugs in science class.

<question>What did Tim like to learn about?</question>
<answer>Tim liked to learn about bugs.</answer>

<question>What was Tim remembering?</question>
<answer>He was remembering bug names.</answer>

<answer>Remembering means thinking about something from the past.</answer>

<answer>He said ants first.</answer>

<question>What bug did Tim say after ants?</question>
<answer>He said bees after ants.</answer>

<answer>A big grasshopper jumped by.</answer>

<answer>It landed on Tim's nose.</answer>

<answer>Tim giggled.</answer>

<question>What did the grasshopper do after landing on Tim's nose?</question>
<answer>It tickled him and then flew away.</answer>

<question>What does Tim want to learn more about?</question>
<answer>He wants to learn more about bugs in science class.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=12 id=story_qa_000783 =====

Tommy thought about life. He thought, "Life is like a big adventure." Then, she showed him a textured rock. <definition>Textured means it feels rough or bumpy when you touch it.</definition> Tommy ran his fingers over it. It felt bumpy and fun. 

He learned that facts help us understand life. He liked learning new things every day. Tommy decided he wanted to learn more about the world and all its textured rocks.

===== DOC START split=test idx=13 id=story_qa_000776 =====
All the birds were singing in the trees. Their songs were loud and happy. Over the hill, a short squirrel ran fast. He had a distinct way of jumping from branch to branch. The squirrel loved to listen to the singing birds. One bird had a very short song, but it was still very sweet. The animals all stopped to hear the music. They smiled and clapped their paws and wings. The singing made everyone feel happy. The squirrel wanted to sing too, but he was too short. So, he just listened and danced on his tiptoes. All the animals loved the singing day. It was a fun and happy time for all.

<question>What were all the birds doing?</question>
<answer>The birds were singing in the trees.</answer>

<question>Were the bird songs loud or quiet?</question>
<answer>The songs were loud and happy.</answer>

<question>Who ran over the hill?</question>
<answer>The squirrel ran over the hill.</answer>

<question>How did the squirrel jump from branch to branch?</question>
<answer>He had a distinct way of jumping.</answer>

<question>Did the squirrel like to listen to the birds sing?</question>
<answer>Yes, the squirrel loved to listen to the singing birds.</answer>

<question>What was special about one bird's song?</question>
<answer>The bird's song was very short but sweet.</answer>

<question>What did the animals do when they heard the music?</question>
<answer>They stopped, smiled, and clapped their paws and wings.</answer>

<question>Did the singing make everyone feel happy?</question>
<answer>Yes, the singing made everyone feel happy.</answer>

<question>Did the squirrel want to sing?</question>
<answer>Yes, the squirrel wanted to sing too.</answer>

<question>Why didn't the squirrel sing?</question>
<answer>He was too short to sing.</answer>

<question>What did the squirrel do instead?</question>
<answer>He listened and danced on his tiptoes.</answer>

<question>Was the singing day fun?</question>
<answer>Yes, it was a fun and happy day for all.</answer>
===== DOC END =====

===== DOC START split=test idx=14 id=story_qa_000925 =====
Tim had a red truck. He used it to haul rocks. The truck was old, but Tim loved it. One day, Tim saw a big hill. "I can climb that hill!" he said. He put a rock in his truck. Then he drove up, up, up the hill. The truck went slow. It went very slow. But the truck made it! Tim was so happy. He took the rock out. He <definition>used means you did something with it</definition>. Now he used the rock to make a little house for his toy frog. The frog liked his new house. Tim can play with his truck and his frog.

<question>What color was Tim's truck?</question>
<answer>Tim's truck was red.</answer>

<question>What did Tim haul in his truck?</question>
<answer>He hauled rocks.</answer>

<question>Was the truck new or old?</question>
<answer>The truck was old.</answer>

<question>What did Tim want to climb?</question>
<answer>Tim wanted to climb a big hill.</answer>

<question>Did the truck make it up the hill?</question>
<answer>Yes, the truck made it.</answer>

<question>How did the truck go up the hill?</question>
<answer>The truck went slow.</answer>

<question>What did Tim do with the rock after?</question>
<answer>He made a house for his frog.</answer>

<question>Who liked the new house?</question>
<answer>The frog liked the new house.</answer>

<question>Who can Tim play with now?</question>
<answer>Tim can play with his truck and his frog.</answer>

<question>What does used mean?</question>
<answer>Used means you did something with it.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=16 id=story_qa_001106 =====
The bug was small. It was a ladybug. Ladybugs are a type of bug. <definition>Genus means a group of similar living things.</definition> Its color was red. It had black spots. Someone made a tiny house for the ladybug. It was made of leaves. The leaves were marked with dirt. The ladybug liked its house. It liked the soft leaves. It liked the dirt marks. The ladybug was happy. It was especially happy when it rained. The rain made the leaves wet. The ladybug drank the water. It was a good day to be a ladybug.

<question>What kind of bug was it?</question>
<answer>It was a ladybug.</answer>

<question>What color was the ladybug?</question>
<answer>It was red.</answer>

<question>What color were the spots?</question>
<answer>The spots were black.</answer>

<question>What was the house made of?</question>
<answer>The house was made of leaves.</answer>

<question>What was on the leaves?</question>
<answer>There was dirt on the leaves.</answer>

<question>Did the ladybug like its house?</question>
<answer>Yes, the ladybug liked its house.</answer>

<question>What did the ladybug like about the house?</question>
<answer>It liked the soft leaves and dirt marks.</answer>

<question>When was the ladybug especially happy?</question>
<answer>It was happy when it rained.</answer>

<question>What did the ladybug drink?</question>
<answer>It drank the water.</answer>

<question>What does genus mean?</question>
<answer>Genus means a group of similar living things.</answer>
===== DOC END =====

===== DOC START split=test idx=17 id=story_qa_001153 =====
Lily had a little plant. It sat by the window in her room. The plant was small. It needed water. Lily gave it water every day. One day, Lily saw something strange. There were thin cords growing from the plant. <definition>Cords are like thin ropes.</definition> "Wow!" said Lily. The cords reached up high. They reached down low. They even tickled her nose! Lily giggled. She got some small toys. She tied them to the cords. Now her plant had tiny toys hanging from it. It was the best plant in the whole world. Lily smiled at her plant. Her plant made her happy.

<question>Who had a little plant?</question>
<answer>Lily had a little plant.</answer>

<question>Where was the plant?</question>
<answer>The plant was by the window.</answer>

<question>What did the plant need?</question>
<answer>The plant needed water.</answer>

<question>What did Lily give the plant?</question>
<answer>Lily gave the plant water.</answer>

<question>What did Lily see one day?</question>
<answer>Lily saw something strange.</answer>

<question>What were growing from the plant?</question>
<answer>Thin cords were growing from the plant.</answer>

<question>What are cords?</question>
<answer>Cords are like thin ropes.</answer>

<question>Where did the cords reach?</question>
<answer>The cords reached up high and down low.</answer>

<question>What did the cords do to Lily?</question>
<answer>The cords tickled her nose.</answer>

<question>What did Lily do when the cords tickled her?</question>
<answer>Lily giggled.</answer>

<question>What did Lily tie to the cords?</question>
<answer>Lily tied small toys to the cords.</answer>

<question>What did Lily think about her plant?</question>
<answer>She thought it was the best plant in the world.</answer>

<question>How did Lily feel about her plant?</question>
<answer>Her plant made her happy.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=18 id=story_qa_001187 =====
In the jungle, the animals felt happy. But one monkey caused trouble. He showed insubordination, not listening to the rules. <definition>Insubordination means not following what you are told to do.</definition> The atmosphere turned quiet and sad. The animals hoped he would learn to be kind. Soon, everyone was friends again, and the jungle was bright and cheerful.

<question>Where did the animals live?</question>
<answer>The animals lived in the jungle.</answer>

<question>How did the animals feel at first?</question>
<answer>The animals felt happy.</answer>

<question>Who caused trouble?</question>
<answer>A monkey caused trouble.</answer>

<question>What did the monkey show?</question>
<answer>The monkey showed insubordination.</answer>

<question>What does insubordination mean?</question>
<answer>Insubordination means not following what you are told to do.</answer>

<answer>Everyone was friends again.</answer>

The pig saw a fire! It was near the barn. He ran to get help. He shouted, "Fire! Fire!" The farmer heard him. The farmer saved all the animals. He saved the horses and the cows. He saved the chickens, too. The pig was very happy. He was brave, especially for a little pig. The farmer gave the pig extra apples. The pig was a hero! He ate his apples by the barn.

===== DOC START split=test idx=22 id=story_qa_001328 =====
Tom was a small boy. One day, he saw a bird in the tree. The bird was in a small nest. Tom wanted to see it close. He climbed up carefully.

But the bird did not like that. It made an uncomplimentary sound. <definition>Uncomplimentary means saying something mean or not nice.</definition> The bird did not want Tom in its nest.

Tom stepped back. He looked at the nest and the bird. He was happy to see it from below. He knew the bird wanted to stay in its small nest. 

Tom smiled. He learned that some things are best in their own place.

<question>Who is the boy in the story?</question>
<answer>The boy is Tom.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=37 id=story_qa_002138 =====
Lily loved to paint. She liked to paint cats and dogs. She liked to paint flowers too. Lily was always fashioning new things. <definition>Fashioning means making something new.</definition> She was making a new picture every day. Her art teacher told her to paint an apple. That was the orthodox way. <definition>Orthodox means the normal or expected way to do something.</definition> But Lily did not want to paint an apple. She wanted to paint a rainbow cat! Her mom said, "Your art has great value." <definition>Value means how much something is worth.</definition> So Lily painted her rainbow cat. It was the best painting ever!

<question>What did Lily love to do?</question>
<answer>Lily loved to paint.</answer>

<question>What did Lily like to paint?</question>
<answer>She liked to paint cats and dogs.</answer>

<question>Did Lily like to paint flowers?</question>
<answer>Yes, she liked to paint flowers too.</answer>

<question>What does fashioning mean?</question>
<answer>Fashioning means making something new.</answer>

A big red truck was next to him. "I like traffic," said the truck. "I like to look at all the other cars."

The blue car did not agree. "That is silly," he said.

Suddenly, the traffic started to move. The blue car zoomed ahead. He forgot all about the red truck. Zoom! He was free! He was happy again. What an adventure!

Pam was not happy. Trees do not do much. She stood on the stage. The goose walked by. The goose said hello to the tree. Pam was quiet. She was a tree. The goose kept talking. Pam started nodding. <definition>Nodding means to move your head up and down.</definition>

The teacher yelled, "Trees do not nod!" Pam stopped nodding. It was hard to not act like a person. But Pam was a good tree after that. The end.

One day, a distinguished general walked by. <definition>Distinguished means important and respected.</definition> He was in the military. <definition>The military is the army, navy, and air force.</definition> He saw the little plant. "You will be a great tree," he said. "You just need sunshine!"

The general moved a big rock. Now the sun shone right on the plant. The plant felt happy. It would be big soon! It would be the best tree in the whole world!

===== DOC START split=test idx=65 id=story_qa_003313 =====
The cat sat on a mat. The mat was in the sun. The sun was warm. The cat liked to nap there.

One day, Lily made a picture. It was an abstract painting. <definition>Abstract means the picture does not look like real things.</definition> It had blobs of red and blue. It had lines of green and yellow.

Lily showed the cat her art. "What do you think?" she asked.

The cat blinked. The painting was overfamiliar. <definition>Overfamiliar means you have seen it so many times that you don't really notice it anymore.</definition> Lily always made art like this.

The cat yawned. He liked the sun better. He went back to sleep on the mat. Lily giggled. Cats like naps more than art, she thought. She went back inside to make more colorful blobs in her abstract art.

<question>Where did the cat sit?</question>
<answer>The cat sat on a mat.</answer>

His momma bear smiled. "Be careful," she said. "Being careful is important." <definition>Being means what something is, or how it is acting.</definition>

The little bear ran to the big hill. He rolled all the way down. He ran back up. He rolled down again. He wanted to roll all day! It was the best day to be a bear.

The big bus had a flat tire. Oh no! That was not good. The bus could not go. The driver looked sad. He needed to fix it. This was <definition>part means a piece of something.</definition> part of his job.

“I can help!” said Tim. Tim was only one small boy. <definition>One means the number 1.</definition> But he was strong.

“How?” asked the driver.

“I can hold the light!” said Tim.

The driver smiled. He got out his tools. He worked hard by the tire. <definition>By means near to.</definition> Tim held the light. He held it high. The driver took the tire off.

The driver worked fast. He put on a new tire. He worked as fast as he could. <definition>As means like or similar to.</definition> Soon, the bus was ready.

"Thank you, Tim!" said the driver. "You helped a lot!" Tim smiled. He was happy to help.

<answer>The bus had a flat tire.</answer>

The sun went down. It got dark. <definition>Dark means with little or no light.</definition> A little frog sat on a rock. He wanted to go home. But his home was far away. It was a long way to go. He hopped off the rock.

The path was bumpy. It was also very unsteady. <definition>Unsteady means not firm or stable.</definition> The frog felt like he might fall. He took a deep breath. He hopped again.

A firefly flew by. It blinked its light on and off. “Hello!” said the frog. “Can you help me?”

The firefly smiled. "I can light your way!"

The firefly flew in front of the frog. The frog followed the light. The path was still unsteady. But the light helped. Soon, the frog was home. He was happy to be on his lily pad. He thanked the firefly and went to sleep. It was a long day for the little frog.

<answer>The sun went down.</answer>

<answer>The frog was sitting on a rock.</answer>

<answer>The frog wanted to go home.</answer>

<answer>The frog's home was far away.</answer>

<answer>Dark means with little or no light.</answer>

<answer>The path was bumpy and unsteady.</answer>

<question>What does unsteady mean?</question>
<answer>Unsteady means not firm or stable.</answer>

<question>Who flew by?</question>
<answer>A firefly flew by.</answer>

<answer>The frog said hello and asked for help.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=73 id=story_qa_003800 =====
Pip was a little pig. He lived by a big farm. Pip had a brother. His brother was very annoying. <definition>Annoying means to bother or irritate someone.</definition> His brother liked to pull Pip's tail.

One day, Pip's mom cooked a big meal. <definition>Cooked means to prepare food by heating it.</definition> She cooked yummy apples. She cooked sweet potatoes. Pip's brother tried to take Pip's apple.

"No!" said Pip. He ran away. He ran by the big barn. He ate his apple. It was so good. His brother could not get it. Pip smiled. Being annoying was not nice. Pip was happy he had his apple all by himself. He ate it all up.

<question>Who was Pip?</question>
<answer>Pip was a little pig.</answer>

<question>Where did Pip live?</question>
<answer>Pip lived by a big farm.</answer>

Timmy had an idea. He could try reducing the size of the robot. <definition>Reducing means making something smaller.</definition> Maybe a smaller robot would be easier to move. He took the robot apart. He took off its head. He took off its arms. He took off its legs. Now there was just the body.

Timmy put the body back together. It was still stiff! He could not get the robot to dance at all. He did not have any luck. Timmy sighed. "Maybe I will play with it as is," he said.

===== DOC START split=test idx=84 id=story_qa_004328 =====

"What game will I do?" said Tim. He looked at the ring toss. He looked at the bean bag game. He had to state his choice. <definition>State means to say something clearly.</definition>

He saw a big dunking booth. The sign said, "Dunk the Principal!" Tim loved his principal. But he wanted to try!

Tim paid for his turn. He got three balls. He threw one. It missed. He threw another. It missed again. The last ball went in! SPLASH! The principal fell in the water. Everyone laughed.

The principal was exposed to the sun now. <definition>Exposed means not covered or protected.</definition> He smiled and waved. Tim got a prize. It was a small bouncy ball. He was happy.

<answer>Today was the school fair.</answer>

"Let's play tag!" said the bunny.

The pig smiled. "I like tag!"

The bunny tagged the pig. "You are it!" she said. The pig chased the bunny. He was not sad anymore. Playing with the bunny was as fun as eating mud! <definition>As means in the same way.</definition> They played all day.

===== DOC START split=test idx=89 id=story_qa_004513 =====
The sun was hot. Lily wanted to play. She looked around her yard. Was it true that fairies lived there? <definition>True means that something is real or correct.</definition>

She saw someone hiding. It was a small cat. Its name was Patches. Patches had white fur and black spots.

Lily picked up Patches. She held him up high. He tried to climb to her upper arm. <definition>Upper means higher or on top.</definition>

"Silly cat," Lily said. She put Patches on the ground.

Patches ran to a tree. He started to climb. He climbed very fast. He went up, up, up. He was in the upper branches.

Lily smiled. "He thinks he is a fairy!" she said. She knew it was not true, but it was fun to pretend. She wanted to play with Patches all day.

<question>What was the weather like?</question>
<answer>The sun was hot.</answer>

<question>What did Lily want to do?</question>
<answer>Lily wanted to play.</answer>

<question>What did Lily wonder about her yard?</question>
<answer>She wondered if fairies lived there.</answer>

<question>What does true mean?</question>
<answer>True means that something is real or correct.</answer>

<question>Who was hiding in the yard?</question>
<answer>A small cat was hiding.</answer>

<question>What was the cat's name?</question>
<answer>The cat's name was Patches.</answer>

The kids played in the park. They played by the big tree. They played on the swings. But Timmy wanted to play a different game. <definition>Different means not the same.</definition>

He saw someone near the pond. It was a girl with a red hat. She was throwing rocks into the water. Timmy walked over to her.

"Hi!" said Timmy. "What are you doing?"

"I am seeing how many times the rocks skip on the water," she said. "I want to see the surface of the water." <definition>Surface means the top part of something.</definition>

Timmy watched a rock skip five times! "Wow!" he said. "Can I try?" The girl smiled and said, "Yes!" Timmy picked up a rock and threw it. It only skipped one time. They both laughed and played together all afternoon.

===== DOC START split=test idx=93 id=story_qa_004615 =====
The little red hen lived by a big farm. She liked to eat seeds. One day, she found a big seed. She wanted to plant it. But she needed help.

She asked the pig, "Will you help me plant this seed?" The pig said, "No, not I!"

She asked the cat, "Will you help me plant this seed?" The cat said, "No, not I!"

She asked the man. <definition>A man is an adult who is not a woman.</definition> "Will you help me plant this seed?" The man said, "No, not I!"

So, the little red hen planted the seed all by herself. Soon, the seed grew into wheat. The hen needed help to cut the wheat.

She asked the pig, the cat, and the man to help. They all said no. So, she cut it by herself.

Then, she wanted to make bread. "Will you give me a hand?" she asked. <definition>Give means to hand something over.</definition> The pig, cat, and man all said no.

The little red hen made the bread all by herself. She cooked it. It smelled yummy! She ate it all up.

<question>Where did the little red hen live?</question>
<answer>The little red hen lived by a big farm.</answer>

<question>What did the little red hen like to eat?</question>
<answer>She liked to eat seeds.</answer>

<answer>She found a big seed.</answer>

<answer>She asked the pig, the cat, and the man.</answer>

<answer>No, the cat did not help her.</answer>

<answer>No, the man did not help her.</answer>

<answer>The little red hen planted the seed.</answer>

A British scientist came by. <definition>British means from the country of Great Britain.</definition> She wanted to study the rock. Suddenly, the bus stopped. "What happened?" asked Sue. The driver said, "Something is wrong with the bus. It won't move."

A tow truck came. It had a big hook. It drug <definition>Drug means to pull something heavy.</definition> the bus to a repair shop.

The kids had to wait inside. They played games. They sang songs. They hoped the bus would start soon.

Finally, the driver said, "The bus is fixed!" The kids cheered. The bumpy ride would cease. <definition>Cease means to stop.</definition> They all ran back to their seats. The bus drove away, smooth as could be.

<answer>Many seats.</answer>

The frog jumped! He wanted to catch the bug. He jumped high in the air. Splash! He fell in the river.

The water was cold. He did not like the cold water. He swam to a lily pad. He sat on the lily pad. He saw the bug again. The bug flew away.

The frog was sad. Then he saw a fly. The fly was black. It landed on the lily pad. The frog stuck out his tongue. He ate the fly. Yum!

The frog was happy again. He liked living in the river. He liked to eat bugs. He was a happy, green frog.

Timmy liked to make up games. He said the toys were in a pretend town. The town needed laws. <definition>Legislative means having the power to make laws.</definition> Timmy said the red car was in charge. The car made a rule. The rule was that everyone had to be nice.

One day, a toy truck was not nice. The truck took a toy block from a small bear. The red car drove over. He told the truck, "Be nice! That is the law!" The truck gave the block back. Timmy was happy the red car used its power for good.

<definition>Fleshy means soft and juicy.</definition>

A bird sat on the branch.  
The apple looked tasty!

Finally, they saw a small door in a big tree. The tiny key fit perfectly! The door opened. Inside, they saw more coins and a little note. The note said, "Thanks for trading and being kind." Tom and Lily clapped. They were happy. They learned that being nice and sharing can make fun adventures. They ran away happily, ready for more fun in the park.

They looked under the bench. They looked by the tree. They looked near the slide. Still no car.

Then, someone saw the car. <definition>Someone means a person.</definition> It was under a bush! The boy picked it up. He gave it to Lily.

Lily smiled. "Thank you!" she said.

The boy smiled back. "You are welcome!"

Lily and the boy played with the car. They had fun. It was a good day. Being kind made them both happy.

===== DOC START split=test idx=142 id=story_qa_006904 =====
The class went on a trip. They went to see a farm. The farm made yummy food that preserves fruit. <definition>Preserves means to keep something safe and good for a long time.</definition> They saw apples and pears.

Someone asked, "Can we try some?" The farmer smiled. "Yes!" he said. He had little jars of jam. He distributed the jam to all the kids. <definition>Distributed means to give something out to a group of people.</definition>

Each kid got a spoon. They tasted the jam. It was sweet! They ate it at a picnic table. The sun was warm. Everyone was happy. It was a great day.

<question>Where did the class go?</question>
<answer>The class went to a farm.</answer>

<question>What did the farm make?</question>

Tom whispered, "That bug is cool." Lily nodded and said, "We are friends, and we help each other." They smiled and watched the intersecting roads again. The sun shone down. Tom was happy he had Lily. They learned that even small things, like a tiny cut or a bug, can be part of a big, fun day. They knew that words and friendship make everything better.

===== DOC START split=test idx=182 id=story_qa_008160 =====
Tom and Lily wanted to build a big tower. They took blocks and stacked them in a line. "If we combine our blocks," said Tom, "we can make a taller tower." Lily nodded. They pushed their blocks together. The tower grew tall and strong. 

<definition>United means working together as one.</definition> When they worked united, they could do more. Tom said, "We are capable of making a very tall tower." Lily smiled. She knew they could do it. 

They used a small scale called a gram to check how heavy the blocks were. Gram measures how light or heavy things are. The blocks were light, but when they put many together, the tower became heavy. 

Soon, the tower was so tall it almost touched the sky. They cheered. "We did it!" they yelled. Their teamwork made their dream come true. They learned that working united and combining their ideas made big things possible.

<question>What did Tom and Lily want to build?</question>
<answer>They wanted to build a big tower.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=183 id=story_qa_008344 =====
The sun was hot. A little frog sat on a rock. He lived in a big green forest. He liked to hop on the rocks. One day, he found a strange thing. It was a big rock with a cleft in it. <definition>A cleft is a split or crack in something.</definition>

"Wow!" said the frog. He jumped in the cleft. It was cool and dark in there. He felt safe.

A bird flew by. "Silly frog!" she sang. "You should worship the sun!" <definition>Worship means to really, really like something.</definition>

The frog popped his head out. "I like the sun," he said. "But I LOVE the cool, dark cleft!" He jumped back in. It was the best place of all. He was happy in his little rock home.

===== DOC START split=test idx=195 id=story_qa_008983 =====
Tommy had a toy robot. The robot was loud and beeped annoyingly. Tommy used the robot to play, but it was very noisy.  
<definition>Annoyingly means in a way that makes you a little upset or tired.</definition>  

Tommy wanted to show his friend how the robot worked. He pressed a button in a funny manner.  
He moved his hand quickly, then slowly. The robot beeped and spun around. Tommy’s friend laughed.  

But the robot made a loud noise again. Tommy frowned. He didn’t like the annoying sound.  
He decided to turn it off. He used his hands carefully and in a calm manner.  
<definition>Manner means the way you do something.</definition>  

Now the robot was quiet. Tommy and his friend played happily. They had fun without the annoying noise.  
Tommy learned that sometimes, it’s better to use things in a gentle manner. That way, everyone stays happy.

<question>What toy did Tommy have?</question>
<answer>Tommy had a toy robot.</answer>

<question>What kind of noise did the robot make?</question>
<answer>The robot made a loud and annoying beep.</answer>

<answer>Annoyingly means in a way that makes you a little upset or tired.</answer>

<answer>Tommy wanted to show his friend.</answer>

<answer>Tommy did not like the noise.</answer>

<question>What did Tommy decide to do with the robot?</question>
<answer>Tommy decided to turn it off.</answer>

<question>What does manner mean?</question>
<answer>Manner means the way you do something.</answer>

<answer>Tommy used his hands carefully and in a calm manner.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=215 id=story_qa_010167 =====

One day, the frog saw a little bird. The bird was sitting by the pond. The bird looked sad. "What is wrong?" asked the frog.

"I am thirsty," said the bird. "But I am afraid of the water."

The frog smiled. "The water is fun! I like it, especially." <definition>Especially means more than other things.</definition> He jumped in the water. He splashed and played. The bird watched him.

The frog hopped out. He brought water to the bird. The bird drank the water. The bird felt much better. Now the bird liked the frog, especially. They both played by the pond.

<answer>The frog lived by a big pond.</answer>

She spent the afternoon in the shade of the tree. At sunset, she went inside. She was tired but happy. She loved her garden and the bird so much. Every day, she played in her garden and watched the birds. It was a perfect day in her special place.

===== DOC START split=test idx=225 id=story_qa_010592 =====
Tom had a big toy boat. He wanted to make it go fast. He pushed it by the water. The boat started to move. <definition>Propel means to push or drive forward.</definition> Tom pushed it again. The boat jumped ahead. It was fun to watch. 

Tom’s friend, Lily, saw the boat. She wanted to help. She blew on the boat gently. The wind helped propel the boat even faster. It zoomed across the pond. They clapped and cheered. 

Tom said, “Let’s push it by the rocks!” Lily pushed hard. The boat went past the rocks and into the grass. It stopped there. Tom said, “We did it! Our boat can propel itself fast.” 

They laughed and played all afternoon. The boat was happy to be pushed by their hands. They knew next time, they could make it go even faster. The boat was their little race car by the water.

The farmer could not find him. The pig giggled. He liked to trick the farmer. He knew the farmer would not be happy. But the pig liked to play. He would act silly again tomorrow.

===== DOC START split=test idx=240 id=story_qa_011379 =====
Spot was a dog. He was a good dog. Spot was trained. <definition>Trained means Spot learned to do tricks.</definition> He could sit. He could stay. He could even roll over!

One day, a bird flew into the yard. Spot saw the bird. He wanted to chase it! But his owner said, "Stay, Spot!"

Spot tried to stay. But the bird was so close! He felt a little bit irrational. <definition>Irrational means Spot was acting without thinking.</definition> It didn't make sense to stay!

Spot barked. He wiggled. He really, really wanted to chase that bird.

His owner laughed. "Okay, Spot," she said. "Go get it!"

Spot ran and ran. But the bird flew away. Spot wagged his tail. Even though he didn't catch the bird, he was happy he got to run! He was a very good, and very fast, dog.

<question>What kind of animal was Spot?</question>
<answer>Spot was a dog.</answer>

<answer>Yes, Spot was a good dog.</answer>

<answer>His owner told him to stay.</answer>

<answer>Irrational means Spot was acting without thinking.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=259 id=story_qa_011998 =====
Lily loved to paint. She liked to paint flowers. She liked to paint trees. One day, Lily wanted to paint something new. She wanted to paint something special.

<definition>Cultural means relating to the habits, traditions, and beliefs of a group of people.</definition> She asked her mom about cultural things. Lily's mom showed her a book. The book had pictures of art from all over the world. Lily saw a particular painting she liked. <definition>Particular means special or specific.</definition> It was a painting of a dragon.

"I want to paint a dragon!" Lily said. She got out her paints. She mixed red and yellow to make orange. She mixed blue and yellow to make green. Lily painted the dragon as best as she could. She painted it in bright colors.

Someone knocked on the door. It was Lily's grandma! "Wow," said Grandma. "That is a wonderful dragon! You are a great artist." Lily smiled. She loved to paint.

<question>What does Lily love to do?</question>
<answer>Lily loves to paint.</answer>

<question>What does Lily like to paint?</question>
<answer>She likes to paint flowers and trees.</answer>

<question>What did Lily want to do one day?</question>
<answer>She wanted to paint something new and special.</answer>

<question>Who showed Lily a book about cultural things?</question>
<answer>Her mom showed her the book.</answer>

<question>What was in the book?</question>
<answer>The book had pictures of art from all over the world.</answer>

<question>What kind of painting did Lily see and like?</question>
<answer>She saw a painting of a dragon.</answer>

<question>What did Lily want to paint?</question>
<answer>She wanted to paint a dragon.</answer>

<question>What colors did Lily mix to make orange?</question>
<answer>She mixed red and yellow.</answer>

<question>What colors did Lily mix to make green?</question>
<answer>She mixed blue and yellow.</answer>

<question>How did Lily paint her dragon?</question>
<answer>She painted it in bright colors.</answer>

<question>Who knocked on the door?</question>
<answer>It was Lily's grandma.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=273 id=story_qa_012959 =====
The little pig was sad. He sat by the big oak tree. He wanted to play! He saw a blue bird. "Can you play with me?" ask the pig.

The bird said, "I am busy, pig." The pig sighed. He felt as gloomy as a rainy day.

Then, a frog hopped by. The pig knew a funny verse. <definition>A verse is a part of a poem or song.</definition> He said the verse to the frog. The frog laughed!

"That was funny!" said the frog. "I like to play, especially in the mud!" The pig smiled. He liked mud too. The pig and the frog played all day. The pig was not sad anymore. He had a new friend!

<answer>He was sitting by the big oak tree.</answer>

<answer>The bird said, "I am busy, pig."</answer>

<answer>The pig felt sad and gloomy.</answer>

<answer>The frog hopped by.</answer>

<answer>The pig told a funny verse to the frog.</answer>

<answer>The frog laughed.</answer>

Mama Bear cut the fabric. She sewed it with a needle. She sewed it with thread. Soon, the shirt was done.

"Here you go!" she said.

The little bear put on the shirt. It fit him just right. It covered his whole body. He was not cold anymore. He ran outside to play by the big tree. He was so happy. He loved his new shirt. It was the best one ever.

Dot got a cold pack. She said, "This will help your nose. It will cool down the hot part."

Pat put the cold pack on his nose. "Oink! That feels good."

The cold pack helped. The red went away. The inflammation was gone! Pat was happy. He could use his nose again. He and Dot played all day.

The little robot beeped. He lived in a big wasteland. <definition>A wasteland is a place that is empty and cannot grow things.</definition> It was hot and dusty. He was all alone. He used to have friends. <definition>Used means something you had before.</definition> But they went away.

One day, he saw something. It was a small green plant! It grew in the dust. The robot was happy. He wanted to help it grow.

He got water by the old well. He gave the plant a drink. He cleared away the rocks. <definition>Clear means to take things away.</definition> He wanted the sun to shine. The plant grew bigger.

Soon, more plants grew by the first one. The wasteland was not empty anymore. It was turning green! The robot smiled. He was not alone anymore. He helped make the wasteland clear of rocks and full of plants. He was happy to have a garden.

<question>Where did the robot live?</question>
<answer>The robot lived in a big wasteland.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=283 id=story_qa_013651 =====
Patty the pig loved cake. She loved all kinds of cake! Chocolate cake was her favorite. One day, Patty met a bird named Poe. Poe was very smart. He liked to think about big ideas. <definition>Philosophy is when you think about big ideas like what is real or what is good.</definition> Poe said, "What is the best cake?"

Patty thought hard. That was a big question. It was like a kind of philosophy. She liked chocolate cake best, but maybe vanilla cake was better? She asked Poe, "What do you think?"

Poe smiled. "The best cake is the one you share!" Patty smiled too. She got a big chocolate cake. She shared it with Poe. They both agreed. Sharing cake was the best!

<question>What animal is Patty?</question>
<answer>Patty is a pig.</answer>

<question>What does Patty love?</question>
<answer>Patty loves cake.</answer>

<question>What kind of cake is Patty's favorite?</question>
<answer>Chocolate cake is her favorite.</answer>

<question>What is the bird's name?</question>
<answer>The bird's name is Poe.</answer>

<question>What was Poe good at?</question>
<answer>Poe was very smart.</answer>

<question>What is philosophy?</question>
<answer>Philosophy is when you think about big ideas.</answer>

<question>What question did Poe ask Patty?</question>
<answer>He asked what is the best cake.</answer>

<question>What kind of cake did Patty share?</question>
<answer>She shared chocolate cake.</answer>

<question>Who did Patty share the cake with?</question>
<answer>She shared it with Poe.</answer>

<question>What did they think was the best cake?</question>
<answer>They thought sharing cake was the best.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=286 id=story_qa_013649 =====
The kids were playing in the park. It was a sunny day. They liked being outside. Being outside made them happy. Alex was not playing. He was having a mental block. <definition>Mental means something in your mind, like a thought.</definition> He could not think of a game.

"Let's play tag!" said Mia. Alex still felt stuck. He walked to the central part of the park. <definition>Central means in the middle of something.</definition> There was a big tree there. He sat down under it. He closed his eyes. He took a deep breath.

Suddenly, he had an idea! "Let's play hide-and-seek!" he shouted. Mia and the other kids cheered. Alex was happy to be playing again. His mental block was gone. Now everyone was having fun.

<answer>The kids were playing in the park.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=289 id=story_qa_013781 =====
One day, Tim saw a small fire in the yard. The fire was not big, but it made him worried. He ran to get help. The fire was on a pile of leaves. <definition>On means something is resting or sitting above something else.</definition> The leaves were dry and easy to burn. Tim knew he had to stop the fire before it grew. He grabbed a hose and turned it on. Water splashed on the fire. The fire went out quickly. Tim was glad. He looked at the small pile of ashes. The fire was gone, and everything was safe. Tim learned to be careful with fire. Fire can be fun to roast marshmallows, but only when grown-ups are watching. Now, Tim keeps a close eye on any small fire. He knows it can become a big problem if not put out. After the fire was gone, Tim felt happy. He went inside and told his mom about what happened. She smiled and said, “Good job, Tim!” The yard was safe again.

<question>What did Tim see in the yard?</question>
<answer>Tim saw a small fire.</answer>

<question>Did the fire make Tim happy or worried?</question>
<answer>The fire made Tim worried.</answer>

<question>Where was the fire?</question>
<answer>The fire was on a pile of leaves.</answer>

<question>What does on mean?</question>
<answer>On means something is resting or sitting above something else.</answer>

<question>What did Tim grab to put out the fire?</question>
<answer>Tim grabbed a hose.</answer>

<question>Did the fire go out quickly?</question>
<answer>Yes, the fire went out quickly.</answer>

<question>How did Tim feel after the fire was out?</question>
<answer>Tim felt glad.</answer>

<question>What did Tim see when he looked at the pile?</question>
<answer>Tim saw a small pile of ashes.</answer>

<question>What did Tim learn to be after the fire?</question>
<answer>Tim learned to be careful with fire.</answer>

<question>Who did Tim tell about the fire?</question>
<answer>Tim told his mom about the fire.</answer>

<answer>She said good job.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=290 id=story_qa_013792 =====
The pig was pink. He liked to play in the mud. It was his favorite thing. One day, he saw a big bug. The bug was on a rock. The pig wanted to play with the bug. He walked to the rock.

"Hello, Bug!" said the pig. The bug did not say hello. The pig was sad. He tried to climb on the rock. The rock was too big. He could not get on top. He looked in the mud. He saw a stick. He picked up the stick. He put the stick on the rock. Now he could climb!

He climbed on the stick. Then he climbed on the rock. Now he was next to the bug. The bug flew away. The pig was sad again. But then he saw a puddle in the mud. He jumped in the puddle! Splish, splash! He was happy again. <definition>A puddle is a small pool of water on the ground.</definition>

<question>What color was the pig?</question>
<answer>The pig was pink.</answer>

<question>What did the pig like to play in?</question>
<answer>The pig liked to play in the mud.</answer>

<answer>The pig saw a big bug.</answer>

<answer>No, the bug did not say hello.</answer>

<answer>No, the rock was too big.</answer>

<answer>He used the stick to climb on top.</answer>

A scientist came to the beach. She had beakers and test tubes. She studied the water. She studied the sand. She did a chemical test. <definition>Chemical means something made by scientists in a lab.</definition>

The crab watched her. Maybe she could help him get on a ship. He waved his claw. The scientist smiled. She put the crab in a bucket. "Now for a new home!" she said. The bucket went on a ship through the window! The crab was so happy.

In a small forest, there lives a tiny frog. The frog is a happy inhabitant of the woods. Every day, he jumps in the green grass and sings a loud song. The frog will hop a long way in the morning. He loves to explore where he can live and play. 

One day, a bird asked the frog, “Will you come to my nest?” The frog said, “Yes, I will visit you in your home.” The bird lives high in a tree, which is a good place for an inhabitant like her. 

The frog is glad he lives in the forest. He knows he will have fun with his friends. Every inhabitant in the woods will enjoy the day. The forest is a happy place to be!

From that day, they used good manners every day. They played happily together. Lina was glad they learned something important. She knew that some kids just need a little help to be kind. She showed them how to do it. Now, all the children played in a happy way, with some sharing and some helping. They all liked to be kind. And they loved to have fun together.

The little spider lived by a big tree. She was very small. She made webs all day. Her webs were very strong. They looked threadlike. <definition>Threadlike means very thin, like a piece of string.</definition> A big bug flew by. It got stuck in the web. The spider ran over to it. "Hello!" she said. The bug wiggled. The spider giggled. She helped the bug get free. "Thank you!" said the bug. It flew away quickly. The spider went back to making her threadlike webs. She liked living by the big tree. It was a good place to be.

<question>Where did the little spider live?</question>
<answer>The spider lived by a big tree.</answer>

<question>Was the spider big or small?</question>
<answer>The spider was very small.</answer>

<answer>She made webs all day.</answer>

The other pigs liked the plan. They started to gather sticks. Building a house is hard work. The pigs had to strive to do their best. <definition>Strive means to try very hard.</definition>

One pig carried a big stick. Another pig carried a small stick. Soon they had many sticks. They worked and worked. At last, the house was done. The pigs were happy to rest by their new house. It was the best house by the big tree!

The other cars were fast, too. A red car went past him. Then a green car. Oh no! He had to move even faster. He pushed on the gas. The little blue car went as fast as he could. He could see the finish line!

He tried to go faster, but the red car was in front. The green car was right behind him. He went faster and faster. He won! The little blue car was the winner! He was so happy.

===== DOC START split=test idx=309 id=story_qa_014823 =====
Lina loved to visit her favorite place in the park. It was a small hill with big trees. She and her friends play there every afternoon. They run and laugh and jump around. 

One day, they played a game called "Hide and Seek." They hid behind trees and under bushes. When it was almost the ending of the game, everyone tried to find the last friend. 

Lina counted to ten and then looked around. She saw her friend Tim hiding behind a big tree. She found him and shouted, "I found you!" Tim smiled and clapped his hands. 

After the game, they sat on the grass. They talked about their fun day. They all said they like this place very much. 

<definition>The ending is the last part of something, like a story or a game.</definition> The day was fun, and they promised to come back soon. They knew that their favorite place is special to all of them.

<answer>She found her friend Tim.</answer>

<answer>She said, "I found you!"</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=310 id=story_qa_014856 =====
The pig was very small. He lived in a pen by a big red barn. He liked to play in the mud. He would roll and roll. He got very dirty!

One day, the farmer gave him a toy. It was a ball. The pig pushed the ball with his nose. He ran as fast as he could. He liked his new toy very much.

The pig saw a puddle. It was full of mud. He wanted to play in it. But he also wanted to play with his ball. What should he do?

He looked at the ball. He looked at the mud. He had an idea! He would play with the ball in the mud. That would be very fun. He jumped in. He pushed the ball. Now the ball was muddy too!

The pig was as happy as could be. <definition>As happy as can be means he was really, really happy!</definition> He loved his ball and he loved the mud. It was a very good day for the pig.

<question>Where did the pig live?</question>
<answer>The pig lived in a pen by a big red barn.</answer>

<question>What did the pig like to do in the mud?</question>
<answer>The pig liked to roll and get very dirty.</answer>

<question>What did the farmer give the pig?</question>
<answer>The farmer gave the pig a ball, which is a toy.</answer>

<question>What did the pig do with the ball?</question>
<answer>The pig pushed the ball with his nose and ran fast.</answer>

<question>What did the pig see that was full of mud?</question>
<answer>The pig saw a puddle that was full of mud.</answer>

<question>What did the pig want to do, the mud or the ball?</question>
<answer>The pig wanted to play in the mud and with his ball.</answer>

<question>What idea did the pig have?</question>
<answer>He decided to play with the ball in the mud.</answer>

<question>What happened when the pig played in the mud?</question>
<answer>The ball got muddy too.</answer>

<question>How did the pig feel at the end?</question>
<answer>The pig was as happy as could be.</answer>

<question>What did the pig love?</question>
<answer>The pig loved his ball and the mud.</answer>

<question>Was it a good day for the pig?</question>
<answer>Yes, it was a very good day for the pig.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=311 id=story_qa_014863 =====
The sun was up. Lily went to play outside. She saw her friends. They were in a group. <definition>A group is when you have some people together.</definition>

"Let's play tag!" said Tom.

The group ran fast. Lily tagged Ben. Then Ben tagged Maya. Maya tagged Tom.

They played for a long time. The sun got hotter. Lily felt tired. The other kids did too.

"I'm thirsty," said Lily.

"Me too!" said Ben.

They all went inside. They drank juice. They rested for a bit.

Lily felt better. The juice helped appreciably. <definition>Appreciably means that you can notice the difference.</definition> She felt much better than before. The whole group went outside to play again. Now they played hide and seek. It was so much fun!

<question>What time of day was it?</question>
<answer>The sun was up.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=317 id=story_qa_015219 =====

"Hello!" says the pig. "I like your hat."

The frog smiles. "Thank you!"

The pig wants to write a story. He wants to write a story about the frog. He needs a type writer. <definition>A type writer is a machine that prints letters on paper when you push buttons.</definition> He runs home. He gets his type writer. He walks back to the pond.

He sits by the pond. He starts to type. He types about the frog and his hat. He types a long story. The frog is happy. The pig is happy too. They are good friends.

Sometimes, Pip got tired. <definition>Tired means you do not have any energy.</definition> When he was tired, he would land on a branch. He would take a little nap. He liked to nap in the sun. The sun felt warm on his feathers.

One day, Pip was racing a butterfly. The butterfly was very fast. Pip tried to fly faster. He flapped his wings. He went very, very fast. But then, Pip got too tired. He landed on a big red flower. He took a nap. The butterfly won the race. But Pip did not mind. He liked his nap more.

===== DOC START split=test idx=324 id=story_qa_015605 =====
Pip was a small frog. He had one big dream. He wanted to jump to the top of the lily pad tower!

The tower was very tall. All the big frogs could do it. Pip tried and tried. He jumped, but he only got halfway. He felt sad.

Mama Frog saw Pip. "What's wrong?" she asked.

"I can't jump high enough!" said Pip. "I feel the pressure." <definition>Pressure is when you feel like you have to do something.</definition>

Mama Frog smiled. "Don't worry, Pip. Just keep trying. Don't put so much pressure on yourself."

Pip took a deep breath. He jumped again. This time, he jumped even higher! He still didn't make it. But he was closer.

He kept jumping. And jumping. Finally, he made it to the top! He was so happy. He learned that even with pressure, he could do anything.

<question>What kind of animal was Pip?</question>
<answer>Pip was a frog.</answer>

<question>Was Pip big or small?</question>
<answer>Pip was small.</answer>

<question>What did Pip want to do?</question>
<answer>Pip wanted to jump to the top of the lily pad tower.</answer>

<answer>The big frogs could.</answer>

<answer>Pip jumped halfway.</answer>

<answer>Pip felt sad.</answer>

<answer>Mama Frog asked Pip.</answer>

<answer>Pressure is when you feel like you have to do something.</answer>

<answer>Mama Frog told Pip to keep trying.</answer>

Tom said, "Let’s pretend the enemy is coming." His troops stood ready. They acted brave and strong. Tom felt happy because he was a good leader. His toy troops looked ready for a big adventure. 

He smiled and said, "We are ready now. We will win the game!" Tom knew that with his helper and good position, his troops would do great. It was a fun day of pretend play.

One day, she wanted to know more about the language people speak in faraway places. She found tiny pictures on the globe that showed flags and words. She asked her mom, “What language do they speak by the big red dot?” Her mom said, “That is the country of China. They speak Chinese.” Lina smiled. She loved learning new words and understanding the world. 

Her globe plaything helped her see the big world outside her house. She dreamed of visiting far places one day and learning many languages. Every night, she kept her globe by her bed, ready for new adventures. Lina knew that her small globe was a special plaything that helped her dream big.

===== DOC START split=test idx=334 id=story_qa_016281 =====
The little bear loved to play. He liked being outside. He liked being by the big tree.

One sunny day, he found a ball. The ball was red and bouncy. He wanted to be a good player. He bounced the ball in the air. Then he bounced it on the ground.

"Look at me!" he said to a bird. The bird watched him. The bear bounced the ball too hard. It bounced away. It bounced in the pond. Oh no!

The bear was sad. He did not like the ball being in the pond. He would be more careful next time. <definition>Being means what something is.</definition>

<question>What did the little bear love to do?</question>
<answer>The little bear loved to play.</answer>

<question>Where did the bear like to be?</question>
<answer>He liked being outside.</answer>

He put Patches on his lap and petted her. "It's okay, Patches," he said. "But no more knocking over blocks!" Patches purred and licked Timmy's hand. They watched the train go round and round.

Lily watched the bug closely. She knew about something called granulocytic. <definition>Granulocytic means a type of tiny cell in your body that helps fight sickness.</definition> She remembered that granulocytic cells are part of your body's way to stay healthy and strong. 

Tom felt better after resting. He and Lily smiled. They learned that even in new places, they could find interesting things. They decided to walk back home, happy and ready for their next adventure.

===== DOC START split=test idx=352 id=story_qa_017056 =====
Tom was in a special place. It was a big building with a sign that said "American." He saw soldiers from the military. They wore uniforms and hats. 

One soldier was locking a door. Tom asked, "What are you locking?" The soldier smiled. <definition>The military is a group of people who protect a country.</definition> "We keep things safe. This place has important stuff."

Tom looked around. He saw a flag waving high. It was the American flag. Tom felt happy. He liked this place because it was full of brave soldiers. 

The soldier patted Tom on the head. "Thank you for visiting," he said. Tom waved goodbye and went home, thinking about the brave soldiers in the military.

<question>Where was Tom?</question>
<answer>Tom was in a big building.</answer>

<question>What did the sign say?</question>
<answer>The sign said American.</answer>

<question>Who did Tom see in the building?</question>
<answer>Tom saw soldiers.</answer>

<question>What did the soldiers wear?</question>
<answer>They wore uniforms and hats.</answer>

<question>What was one soldier doing?</question>
<answer>He was locking a door.</answer>

<question>What did Tom ask the soldier?</question>
<answer>Tom asked what he was locking.</answer>

<question>Why did the soldier say they lock the door?</question>
<answer>To keep things safe.</answer>

<question>What is the military?</question>
<answer>The military is a group of people who protect a country.</answer>

<question>What did Tom see waving high?</question>
<answer>He saw the American flag.</answer>

<question>How did Tom feel?</question>
<answer>Tom felt happy.</answer>

<question>Why did Tom like the place?</question>
<answer>It was full of brave soldiers.</answer>

<question>What did the soldier do to Tom?</question>
<answer>The soldier patted Tom on the head.</answer>

<question>What did the soldier say to Tom?</question>
<answer>He said thank you for visiting.</answer>

<question>What did Tom do before he left?</question>
<answer>Tom waved goodbye.</answer>

<question>What did Tom think about when he went home?</question>
<answer>He thought about the brave soldiers.</answer>
===== DOC END =====

Children and animals came to see the pretty blooms and remember how spring makes everything new. They smiled and played, happy in the bright, blooming garden.

===== DOC START split=test idx=354 id=story_qa_017084 =====
Tom loved to watch cars. One day, he saw a shiny automobile. It was big and fast. Tom wanted to ride in it. But the driver was doing something immoral. <definition>Immoral means not doing what is right or good.</definition> The driver was not following the rules. Tom told his mom. She smiled and said, "It is best to be kind and honest." Tom learned that doing what is right is more important than a fast car. He decided to always be good. The automobile drove away, but Tom felt happy inside. He knew he made a good choice.

<question>What did Tom love to watch?</question>
<answer>Tom loved to watch cars.</answer>

They saw fish and shells. Lina wore a tropical hat. It had bright colors. She loved the warm weather.  

Her friend Tim said, "Texas is a big place. It is not tropical, but it has a lot of sun."  

They played and talked about being social. <definition>Social means liking to be with others and making friends.</definition>  

They had a great day at the sea. They learned new things and had fun. It was a perfect day with friends by the water.

It just floated on. The girl laughed. Clouds cannot talk! She drew a dog shaped like the cloud in her book.

Grandpa had some candy. He gave Tim a red candy. Then he gave Tim a blue candy. "Thank you, Grandpa!" Tim said. He ate the red candy. It was yummy! He saved the blue candy for later. Grandpa smiled at Tim. He put the cigar in his pocket. Then they went to play in the yard. It was a fun day.

The little pig liked books. He liked to read all day. His favorite book was about snacks. He read about the consumption of cookies. <definition>Consumption means eating or drinking something.</definition> He wanted to eat all the cookies!

One day, the pigs included him in their mud play. He tried it. He liked it! Now he liked mud and books.

===== DOC START split=test idx=366 id=story_qa_017766 =====
The snow fell. It was a cold day. <definition>Cold means having a low temperature.</definition> Lily wanted to play. She put on her hat. She put on her coat. She put on her mittens. "I will go outside!" she said.

Lily saw her friend, Tom. Tom was sad. "It is too cold to play," he said.

"No, it is not! Come with me!" said Lily. <definition>Come means to move or travel towards someone.</definition>

They went to the park. The slide was covered in snow. "We can slide!" said Lily.

Tom smiled. He liked to slide. They climbed up the steps. Down they went! They went down as fast as they could. <definition>As means in the same way.</definition>

"This is fun!" said Tom.

Lily and Tom played all day. They were not cold anymore. They were happy. They liked playing in the snow.

<answer>Snow fell from the sky.</answer>

<answer>It was a cold day.</answer>

<answer>Cold means having a low temperature.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=367 id=story_qa_017775 =====
The frog sat by a big log. He wanted a snack. He saw a fly. "I can estimate how far away it is," he thought. <definition>Estimate means to guess how many or how far something is.</definition> He stuck out his tongue. But the fly flew away!

The frog was sad. He hopped to a bush. The ground was moist. <definition>Moist means a little wet.</definition> He saw a worm! He stuck out his tongue again. This time he got it!

That was his last worm. <definition>Last means the one that comes at the end.</definition> He was happy by being full. <definition>Being means existing or what someone is.</definition> He took a nap by the moist bush.

<question>Where did the frog sit?</question>
<answer>The frog sat by a big log.</answer>

<answer>He wanted a snack.</answer>

<question>What did the frog see first?</question>
<answer>He saw a fly.</answer>

<answer>Estimate means to guess how far something is.</answer>

<question>Did the frog catch the fly?</question>
<answer>No, the fly flew away.</answer>

<answer>He hopped to a bush.</answer>

<question>What was the ground like?</question>
<answer>The ground was moist.</answer>

<question>What does moist mean?</question>
<answer>Moist means a little wet.</answer>

<question>What did the frog see at the bush?</question>
<answer>He saw a worm.</answer>

<question>Did the frog catch the worm?</question>
<answer>Yes, he got it.</answer>

<question>What does last mean?</question>
<answer>Last means the one that comes at the end.</answer>

<answer>Being means existing or what someone is.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=368 id=story_qa_017778 =====
Tom had a big toy car. One day, he saw his friend Lily with a shiny ball. Tom wanted the ball. He thought, "Should I trade my car for the ball?"  
<definition>Considered means to think carefully about something.</definition>  
Lily smiled. She said, "I want your car."  
Tom thought again. "Maybe I should keep my car," he said. But then he remembered he loved the shiny ball.  
"Okay," Tom said. "Let’s trade."  
They swapped toys. Tom got the ball. Lily got the car.  
They both cheered. It was a fun trade.  
Later, Tom played with his new ball. It was so shiny and round.  
Lily drove Tom’s car around. She was happy.  
They laughed and played all afternoon.  
Tom learned that a good trade can make everyone happy.  
Sometimes, it’s nice to trade things with friends.  
That way, everyone gets something they like better.  
Tom and Lily became good trade friends.  
They played happily together every day.

<question>What toy did Tom have?</question>
<answer>Tom had a big toy car.</answer>

<question>What toy did Lily have?</question>
<answer>Lily had a shiny ball.</answer>

<question>What did Tom want?</question>
<answer>Tom wanted the ball.</answer>

<question>What does considered mean?</question>
<answer>Considered means to think carefully about something.</answer>

<question>What did Lily say she wanted?</question>
<answer>She said she wanted the car.</answer>

<question>Did Tom trade his car for the ball?</question>
<answer>Yes, Tom traded his car for the ball.</answer>

<question>Who got the ball?</question>
<answer>Tom got the ball.</answer>

<question>Who got the car?</question>
<answer>Lily got the car.</answer>

<question>Were they both happy?</question>
<answer>Yes, they were both happy.</answer>

<question>What did Tom learn?</question>
<answer>Tom learned that a good trade can make everyone happy.</answer>

<question>What kind of friends did Tom and Lily become?</question>
<answer>They became good trade friends.</answer>

<question>Did they play happily together every day?</question>
<answer>Yes, they played happily together every day.</answer>
===== DOC END =====

===== DOC START split=test idx=369 id=story_qa_017741 =====
One day, Tim went to the park. He loved to play on the slides and swing. He was happy. Suddenly, unexpectedly, a big dog ran into the park. The dog looked friendly but loud. Tim felt a strange emotion. It was a mix of surprise and a little fear. <definition>Emotion is how you feel inside, like happy, sad, or scared.</definition> He took a step back. The dog wagged its tail and came close. Tim felt brave now. He reached out his hand and petted the dog. The dog licked his face. Tim smiled big. He was glad the dog was friendly. After that day, Tim learned that sometimes surprises can make us feel many emotions. He was happy he was brave. The dog became his new friend. They played together all afternoon. Tim’s heart felt warm and funny inside. He knew that surprises can be good, even if they are unexpected. Sometimes, unexpected things help us learn how to be brave and kind. And that day, Tim had a very special adventure.

<question>Where did Tim go one day?</question>
<answer>Tim went to the park.</answer>

<question>What did Tim love to play on?</question>
<answer>He loved to play on the slides and swing.</answer>

<question>Who ran into the park?</question>
<answer>A big dog ran into the park.</answer>

<question>What did the dog look like?</question>
<answer>The dog looked friendly but loud.</answer>

<question>How did Tim feel when he saw the dog?</question>
<answer>He felt a mix of surprise and a little fear.</answer>

<question>What does emotion mean?</question>
<answer>Emotion is how you feel inside, like happy, sad, or scared.</answer>

<question>What did Tim do when the dog came close?</question>
<answer>He reached out his hand and petted the dog.</answer>

<question>What did the dog do to Tim's face?</question>
<answer>The dog licked his face.</answer>

<question>How did Tim feel after petting the dog?</question>
<answer>Tim smiled big.</answer>

<question>What did Tim learn that day?</question>
<answer>Tim learned that sometimes surprises can make us feel many emotions.</answer>

<question>Who became Tim's new friend?</question>
<answer>The dog became his new friend.</answer>

<question>What did Tim and the dog do together?</question>
<answer>They played together all afternoon.</answer>

<question>How did Tim's heart feel inside?</question>
<answer>Tim's heart felt warm and funny inside.</answer>

<question>What can unexpected things help us learn?</question>
<answer>Unexpected things help us learn how to be brave and kind.</answer>

<question>What did Tim have that day?</question>
<answer>Tim had a very special adventure.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=370 id=story_qa_017841 =====
Tom was a happy person. He loved to play outside. One day, he saw a round ball. The ball was big and red. Tom wanted to kick it over the fence. But the fence was very tall. He could not do it directly. 

<definition>Indirectly means not straight or right at something. It means doing something in a roundabout way.</definition> 

Tom thought hard. He found a stick. He pushed the ball with the stick, over the fence. The ball rolled down the hill. It went over a small pond. Tom ran after it. The ball went over a rock and then over a bush. 

Finally, the ball stopped near a person. That person picked up the ball and smiled. Tom was happy. He had played a fun game, indirectly and over many things.

<question>Who is the story about?</question>
<answer>The story is about Tom.</answer>

<question>Was Tom happy or sad?</question>
<answer>Tom was happy.</answer>

<question>What did Tom love to do?</question>
<answer>He loved to play outside.</answer>

<question>What did Tom see one day?</question>
<answer>Tom saw a round ball.</answer>

<question>What color was the ball?</question>
<answer>The ball was red.</answer>

<question>What did Tom want to do with the ball?</question>
<answer>Tom wanted to kick it over the fence.</answer>

<question>Was the fence short or tall?</question>
<answer>The fence was tall.</answer>

<question>Could Tom kick the ball over the fence at first?</question>
<answer>No, he could not.</answer>

<question>What does indirectly mean?</question>
<answer>Indirectly means not straight or right at something.</answer>

<question>What did Tom find to help him?</question>
<answer>He found a stick.</answer>

<question>What did Tom do with the stick?</question>
<answer>He pushed the ball with the stick.</answer>

<question>Where did the ball roll after it went over the fence?</question>
<answer>It rolled down the hill.</answer>

<question>What did the ball go over?</question>
<answer>It went over a small pond, a rock, and a bush.</answer>

<question>Who picked up the ball at the end?</question>
<answer>A person picked up the ball.</answer>

<question>Was Tom happy at the end?</question>
<answer>Yes, Tom was happy.</answer>
===== DOC END =====

===== DOC START split=test idx=372 id=story_qa_017909 =====
The little dog, Pat, loved to play. He loved to play in the grass. He loved to play in the mud! Sometimes, Pat played in the house. But Mom did not like that.

One day, Pat was in the yard. He saw a bird. The bird was very small. The bird hopped near the big tree. Pat wanted to play with the bird.

But the bird's mom was watchful. <definition>Watchful means to watch very carefully.</definition> She sat on a branch. She watched Pat. She wanted to keep her baby safe.

Pat barked at the bird. The bird flew away. Pat was sad. He wanted a friend. Then, he saw a ball. He chased the ball instead. Playing with the ball was fun, too!

<question>What did Pat love to do?</question>
<answer>Pat loved to play.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=376 id=story_qa_018184 =====
The sun was hot. A little ladybug sat on a leaf. A person walked by. <definition>A person is a boy, a girl, or a grown-up.</definition> The person did not see the ladybug. The person liked to sing. He sang a funny song. Ding! The ladybug heard a sound. A raindrop struck the leaf. <definition>Struck means hit.</definition> Then another raindrop struck. Plink, plank, plunk! More raindrops fell. The ladybug ran under the leaf. The person opened his umbrella. Now the ladybug and the person were both safe from the rain. They were happy. The sun came out again.

<answer>The ladybug was sitting on a leaf.</answer>

<answer>Struck means hit.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=377 id=story_qa_018150 =====
The little bear wanted to go on an adventure. He looked up. He looked down. He packed a snack. He told his mom, "I am going north!" <definition>North is the direction that is at the top of a map.</definition>

The bear walked and walked. He saw a bird. The bird flew down from a tree. "Hello!" said the bear. "Where are you going?"

"I am going to find all the berries!" said the bird. <definition>All means every one of something.</definition> "Want to come?"

The bear said, "Yes! That sounds fun." So the bear and bird went to find berries. They ate all the berries they could find. The bear and bird were happy. The sun went down. The bear went home.

<question>What did the little bear want to do?</question>
<answer>The little bear wanted to go on an adventure.</answer>

<question>What did the bear pack?</question>
<answer>The bear packed a snack.</answer>

<answer>The bear went north.</answer>

<answer>North is the direction that is at the top of a map.</answer>

<question>Who did the bear see?</question>
<answer>The bear saw a bird.</answer>

<question>What did the bird want to find?</question>
<answer>The bird wanted to find all the berries.</answer>

<question>What does all mean?</question>
<answer>All means every one of something.</answer>

<question>Did the bear go with the bird?</question>
<answer>Yes, the bear went with the bird.</answer>

<question>What did the bear and bird eat?</question>
<answer>They ate all the berries.</answer>

<question>Was the bear happy?</question>
<answer>Yes, the bear was happy.</answer>

<question>When did the bear go home?</question>
<answer>The bear went home when the sun went down.</answer>
