===== DOC START split=test idx=378 id=story_qa_018304 =====
Lina had a big box. Inside the box, she kept her toys. She liked to put her toys in order. First, her teddy bear. Next, her blocks. She loved to do this in the morning.  

One day, Lina’s mom said, "It’s time to clean up." Lina knew what that meant. She put her toys in order again. She did it in quick time.  

<definition>In time means doing something before it is too late or before a certain moment.</definition> Lina finished just in time. She was happy. Then she sat down to play with her toys.  

Lina thought, “It’s fun to put things in order. It makes me happy.” She looked at her tidy box. Everything was neat and in the right place.  

From that day, Lina always put her toys in order. She did it in her own good time. She loved to keep her toys neat and clean.  

And every day, she learned that doing things in order makes everything better.

<question>What did Lina have?</question>
<answer>Lina had a big box.</answer>

<question>What did Lina keep in the box?</question>
<answer>She kept her toys in the box.</answer>

<question>What did Lina like to do with her toys?</question>
<answer>She liked to put her toys in order.</answer>

<question>What did she put in the box first?</question>
<answer>First, her teddy bear.</answer>

<question>What did she put in the box next?</question>
<answer>Next, her blocks.</answer>

<question>When did she love to do this?</question>
<answer>She loved to do this in the morning.</answer>

<question>What did Lina’s mom say?</question>
<answer>It's time to clean up.</answer>

<question>Did Lina know what to do?</question>
<answer>Yes, she did.</answer>

<question>Did she do it slowly or quickly?</question>
<answer>She did it in quick time.</answer>

<question>What does "in time" mean?</question>
<answer>In time means doing something before it is too late.</answer>

<question>Was Lina happy when she finished?</question>
<answer>Yes, she was happy.</answer>

<question>What did Lina think was fun?</question>
<answer>She thought it was fun to put things in order.</answer>

<question>What did she learn to do every day?</question>
<answer>She learned to put her toys in order.</answer>

<question>What did she learn about doing things in order?</question>
<answer>She learned it makes everything better.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=380 id=story_qa_018310 =====
In a big park, there were many trees. Some trees were tall, and some were short. The birds in the trees sang different songs.  
<definition>Different means not the same. Things that are different are not alike.</definition>  
A small boy named Tim loved to explore. He looked in the bushes and under rocks.  
One day, Tim found a tiny, different bug. It was bright blue and had big wings.  
He put the bug in his hand and smiled. "You are in my hand now," he said.  
The bug chirped a little and then flew away. Tim watched it go.  
He was happy to find something different.  
From that day, Tim wanted to find different things in the park every day.  
He knew that in the world, many things are different, and that makes it special.

<question>Where were the trees?</question>
<answer>The trees were in a big park.</answer>

<question>Were all the trees the same height?</question>
<answer>No, some trees were tall and some were short.</answer>

<question>Who sang in the trees?</question>
<answer>The birds sang in the trees.</answer>

<question>What does different mean?</question>
<answer>Different means not the same.</answer>

<question>What did Tim love to do?</question>
<answer>Tim loved to explore.</answer>

<question>What did Tim look in?</question>
<answer>He looked in the bushes.</answer>

<question>What did Tim look under?</question>
<answer>He looked under rocks.</answer>

<question>What did Tim find one day?</question>
<answer>Tim found a tiny, different bug.</answer>

<question>What color was the bug?</question>
<answer>The bug was bright blue.</answer>

<question>Did the bug have wings?</question>
<answer>Yes, the bug had big wings.</answer>

<question>Where did Tim put the bug?</question>
<answer>He put the bug in his hand.</answer>

<question>What did the bug do?</question>
<answer>The bug chirped and then flew away.</answer>

<question>How did Tim feel about finding the bug?</question>
<answer>He was happy.</answer>

<question>What did Tim want to do every day after that?</question>
<answer>Tim wanted to find different things in the park every day.</answer>

<question>What does Tim think makes the world special?</question>
<answer>Tim thinks that many things are different, and that makes it special.</answer>

One day, Pat saw a man. <definition>A man is an adult who is not a woman.</definition> The man was wearing a big hat. The man was also digging! But he was digging for worms. Pat barked. He wanted to help. The man smiled. He gave Pat a worm. Pat did not eat it. He buried it. Now Pat and the man were both planting. Black dog, white flowers, and a nice man. It was a good day.

===== DOC START split=test idx=383 id=story_qa_018462 =====
Timmy was a little robot. He was a happy robot. He lived in a big, blue house. Timmy was not a person. He was a robot!

One day, Timmy played outside. He tripped on a rock. He fell and scratched his arm. Some red liquid came out. "Oh no! Is that blood?" he asked. <definition>Blood is the red liquid that flows through people and animals.</definition> But Timmy did not have blood. He was a robot!

Instead of blood, Timmy had oil. He needed oil for his electrical parts. <definition>Electrical means something that has to do with electricity.</definition> The oil helped him move. It helped him be a good robot.

Timmy’s mom came to help. She cleaned his scratch. She said, "You are a good robot, Timmy! You are a good being." <definition>Being means a creature or a person.</definition> Timmy smiled. He was happy to be a robot being. He went back to playing in the yard.

<question>Where did Timmy live?</question>
<answer>Timmy lived in a big, blue house.</answer>

<question>Was Timmy a person?</question>
<answer>No, he was a robot.</answer>

<question>What happened to Timmy when he played outside?</question>
<answer>He tripped on a rock and fell.</answer>

<question>What came out of Timmy’s arm?</question>
<answer>Red liquid came out.</answer>

<question>Did Timmy have blood?</question>
<answer>No, he did not have blood. He was a robot.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy have instead of blood?</question>
<answer>He had oil.</answer>

<question>Why did Timmy need oil?</question>
<answer>Oil helped him move and work.</answer>

<question>What does electrical mean?</question>
<answer>Electrical means something that has to do with electricity.</answer>

<question>Who helped Timmy after he fell?</question>
<answer>His mom helped him.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy’s mom do?</question>
<answer>She cleaned his scratch.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy’s mom say to him?</question>
<answer>She said, "You are a good robot, Timmy!"</answer>

<question>How did Timmy feel?</question>
<answer>He was happy.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy do after his mom helped him?</question>
<answer>He went back to playing in the yard.</answer>
===== DOC END =====

===== DOC START split=test idx=384 id=story_qa_018554 =====
Emma read a short book by her friend. She liked the story, but she didn’t understand the context. <definition>Context means the words or situation around a story that help you understand it.</definition> Emma asked her friend to explain. Her friend smiled and said, “Sometimes, stories need a little more words to make sense.” Emma nodded. Now she knew why the story was short. She was happy to learn something new and read more stories by her friend.

<question>Who wrote the book Emma read?</question>
<answer>Her friend wrote the book.</answer>

<question>Did Emma like the story?</question>

His friend, Dot the dog, saw the rock. Dot had a stick. "Let's exchange!" said Dot. <definition>Exchange means to trade one thing for another.</definition> Pip looked at the stick. He looked at his rock. He liked the rock more. But Dot looked sad.

Pip smiled. He gave Dot the rock. Dot gave Pip the stick. Now they both had something new. They played together in the sun. Pip learned it is fun to exchange, even if you like your own thing a lot. It's nice to be kind to friends.

After a while, the bug crawled away. Tommy smiled and closed his notebook. “I love to study bugs,” he said.  
He ran inside to tell his mom about his new bug friend. “Next time, I will find more bugs,” he said happily.  
Tommy knew that studying bugs was fun and made him smile.

Lina wanted to help. She showed kindness and sat quietly near the bunny. <definition>Kindness means being nice and caring.</definition> The bunny snuggled close to her. Lina smiled because she knew that kindness can make a big difference. She looked around at all the beautiful nature around her. She was happy to find a new friend. From that day, Lina visited the opening often. She loved helping her new bunny friend and enjoying the beauty of nature.

Soon, the pizza man came. He had a big box. It smelled so good! Timmy’s dad opened the box. It was pepperoni pizza! "Yay!" shouted Timmy. Pizza night was the best, even if it was on Tuesday. He loved being in his family.

===== DOC START split=test idx=394 id=story_qa_019059 =====
Pip was a little frog. Pip loved to swim in the pond. The pond was full of water. One day, Pip found a letter. It was floating on a lily pad. The letter was for his friend, Tad.

Pip hopped to Tad's house. "I have a letter for you!" he said. Tad was happy. "Thank you, Pip!" said Tad. He opened the letter. It was from his grandma.

The letter said, "Come visit me! You can come on Monday, or you can come on Tuesday. Either day is fine." <definition>Either means one or the other.</definition>

Tad looked at Pip. "Should I go on Monday or Tuesday?" he asked.

Pip thought and thought. "Go on Monday!" Pip said. "We can play in the water as soon as you get back on Tuesday!"

Tad smiled. "That is a great idea!" said Tad. He packed his bag. He was as happy as could be. He gave Pip a big hug. "See you soon!"

<question>What kind of animal was Pip?</question>
<answer>Pip was a little frog.</answer>

<question>Where did Pip love to swim?</question>
<answer>Pip loved to swim in the pond.</answer>

<question>What did Pip find floating on a lily pad?</question>
<answer>Pip found a letter.</answer>

<question>Who was the letter for?</question>
<answer>The letter was for Tad.</answer>

<answer>Pip went to Tad's house.</answer>

<answer>Tad said, "Thank you, Pip!"</answer>

<question>Who wrote the letter?</question>
<answer>It was from Tad's grandma.</answer>

<question>What did the letter say?</question>
<answer>The letter said, "Come visit me! You can come on Monday, or you can come on Tuesday."</answer>

<question>What does "either" mean?</question>
<answer>It means one or the other.</answer>

<question>What day did Pip tell Tad to go?</question>
<answer>Pip told Tad to go on Monday.</answer>

<question>What did Pip say about Tuesday?</question>
<answer>Pip said they could play in the water on Tuesday.</answer>

<question>What did Tad do after he decided?</question>
<answer>Tad packed his bag.</answer>

Lily smiled. "Yes! I used it to play fetch. It's by the tree."

The dog wagged his tail. He ran to the tree. There it was! He grabbed his ball. Now he could play again. The dog and Lily played all day.

One day, a girl came to clean the tank. "This is a fish republic!" she said. <definition>A republic is a place where everyone gets a say.</definition> "The fish are the leaders." She took out the green algae. Now the tank was clear. The fish could see everything! He saw his friend waving at him. The fish was happy in his clean, clear tank.

She took her small shovel and dug in the dirt. She was careful so she wouldn’t hurt her plants. 

Lily’s mom showed her a new kind of technology. It was a special tool that helped find weeds. It was on a small computer. Lily’s mom said, “This can help us see weeds in the garden very well.” Lily was excited to learn how it works.

While Lily was working, her cat sat on a rock nearby. The cat watched her and purred. Lily smiled. She was happy to take care of her garden and learn new things. ===== DOC START split=test idx=419 id=story_qa_020079 =====
Tom found a box in the park. Inside, he saw a strange shape. It was round and shiny. "What is this?" Tom asked. He touched the shape gently. It was cold. <definition>Shape means the form or outline of something.</definition> The shape was in the box. Tom wanted to know more. He looked around and saw a small hole in the box. He carefully put his hand in the hole. The shape moved a little. Tom smiled. He was happy to find something new. Now he knew a shape can be many things. Sometimes, shapes are in boxes, waiting to be found. Tom decided to keep the shape safe and play with it tomorrow. He ran home, excited to tell everyone about his new discovery.

<question>What did Tom find in the park?</question>
<answer>Tom found a box in the park.</answer>

<answer>Inside, he saw a strange shape.</answer>

<answer>It was round and shiny.</answer>

One day, a wise owl saw him. The owl smiled. "I can help you!" she hooted. She directed the bird to a big tree. <definition>Directed means showed the way.</definition> "Go there," she said. "You will find fun."

The bird flew to the tree. He saw other birds singing. They had a band! A bird with a trumpet saw him. "Can you sing?" he asked. The little bird had never sung before. No one had ever spoken to him about singing. <definition>Spoken means talked.</definition> He took a deep breath. He sang a little song. It was good! The band asked him to join. Now his life was full of music.

Suddenly, the handle turned! The box opened slowly. Inside, Tom found a shiny star. He smiled. The star looked special. Tom thought about how Jesus is a Christian way to be kind and caring.

He took the star home. Now, every night, he looked at it and felt happy. Tom knew that being a Christian meant helping others and being patient, especially when things are hard.

===== DOC START split=test idx=435 id=story_qa_020647 =====
Tom and Lily loved building with blocks. They were combining red blocks and blue blocks to make a tall tower. As the tower got higher, the blocks at the top started tapering, getting smaller and pointier. <definition>Tapering means becoming thinner or narrower at one end.</definition> Lily said, "Let’s join the blocks at the top so the tower stays steady." They carefully placed the last block on top, and the tower stood tall. They smiled and clapped. The tower was very fun to build. The blocks fit together nicely. Combining the blocks made a big, strong tower. Tapering helped the tower look neat. When they finished, they looked at their work and felt happy. They loved building and playing with their blocks. It was a fun day with their tall, tapering, and joining tower!

<question>What did Tom and Lily love to build?</question>
<answer>They loved building with blocks.</answer>

<question>What colors of blocks did they use?</question>
<answer>They used red and blue blocks.</answer>

<question>What did the tower do as it got higher?</question>
<answer>The top blocks got smaller and pointier.</answer>

<question>What does tapering mean?</question>
<answer>Tapering means becoming thinner or narrower at one end.</answer>

<question>What did Lily say to do at the top of the tower?</question>
<answer>She said, "Let’s join the blocks at the top."</answer>

<question>Why did they join the blocks at the top?</question>
<answer>To make the tower stay steady.</answer>

<question>What did they do after placing the last block?</question>
<answer>The tower stood tall and they smiled.</answer>

<question>How did the tower look because of tapering?</question>
<answer>The tower looked neat.</answer>

<question>Did they have fun building the tower?</question>
<answer>Yes, they had fun.</answer>

<question>Did the blocks fit together well?</question>
<answer>Yes, the blocks fit together nicely.</answer>

<question>How did Tom and Lily feel after building the tower?</question>
<answer>They felt happy.</answer>

<question>What did they love to do with their blocks?</question>
<answer>They loved building and playing.</answer>

<question>Was it a fun day for Tom and Lily?</question>
<answer>Yes, it was a fun day.</answer>

Suddenly, she heard a noise. It was a bird sitting on a metal pole. The bird made a loud chirp. Lina looked around and saw many tropical flowers and trees. She thought, "This island is full of surprises." 

Lina decided to collect her metal spoon and take it home. She knew it was special because it was from a tropical place. She was happy to have a tiny piece of the island with her. And she dreamed of more adventures next time.

Every day, Lily checked on her bug. She hoped her theory was right. She knew she had found something fun and new. The bug was special, and Lily was happy she found it in her garden.

Mom said, "This is good material for a cape!" She helped him put it on.

<definition>Intramuscularly means getting a shot in your muscle, like in your arm.</definition>  
Tommy was a little scared. The nurse said, "The shot will go in here, in your arm." She showed him where.  
He sat in the chair quietly. The nurse cleaned his arm. Then, she gently put the needle in, <definition>intramuscularly</definition>.  
Tommy looked between his legs. "All done!" she said.  
He smiled and hugged his toy. Now, he knew shots help keep him healthy in and out of his body.

The ant had a lot of power. <definition>Power is being strong and able to do things.</definition> It could lift things much bigger than itself.

One day, the ants found an archaic map. <definition>Archaic means very, very old.</definition> The map was old and brown. It showed where the humans kept their cookies. The ants needed a legislative plan. <definition>Legislative means having to do with making rules or laws.</definition> They needed to decide how to get the cookies.

The ants made a plan. They would march to the house. They would climb the table. They would take the cookies back to the hill. It was a good plan. The ants worked together. They used their power to get the cookies. All the ants were happy.

The bird's foot made a mark. It was spreading sand everywhere. <definition>Spreading means to open out or cover a large area.</definition> The ants were safe in their hill. They ate the crumb. It was a good day for every one of them.

Lina smiled. She thought, “Now I know how animals are grouped by genus. I will learn more!” She played with her cat and the cord all afternoon. The cat loved to chase the moving cord. Lina was happy to learn new things about animals and laws.

<definition>Hardship means a difficult time or problem.</definition>  
The sun was shining, and the snow was sparkling. Tom felt happy, even as it was cold. He knew that in the warm house, he would be cozy soon.  
The thermal hat kept his head warm. In the end, Tom was glad he wore his warm clothes. He learned that even in hardship, you can find joy.

She wanted to find more bugs and learn about the genus they belong to. She knew she would have lots of fun. Every bug she saw was a new friend to her.

===== DOC START split=test idx=472 id=story_qa_022293 =====
The little bird, Pip, had a bad attitude. <definition>Attitude is the way you feel and think about something.</definition> He did not like to fly. All the other birds loved to fly. They soared with their wings. Pip sat on the branch.

"Flying is silly!" he said.

A wise old owl watched him. "It is good to be kind," she said. "It is good to act kindly." <definition>Act means to do something.</definition>

Pip puffed out his chest. He did not want to act kindly. He wanted to be grumpy.

The owl hooted softly. "Every being can be kind." <definition>Being means a person or animal.</definition> "Even you, Pip."

Pip looked at his wings. He thought about being kind. He took a deep breath. He flapped his wings. He flew! It was fun! Pip smiled. His attitude changed. Flying was not silly after all.

</question>

The little bird's name was Pip.

===== DOC START split=test idx=490 id=story_qa_023468 =====
The pig was sad. He had one small balloon. It was red. He wanted to play. But no one was around.

Then he saw the cat. The cat was sitting by a tree. The pig ran to the cat. "Will you play with me?" he asked.

The cat looked up. She saw the balloon. "Yes!" said the cat. "Let's play catch."

The pig smiled. He threw the balloon to the cat. The cat hit it with her paw. It went up high.

They played for a long time. They laughed as they played. <definition>As means at the same time.</definition> The pig was not sad anymore. He had a friend. Playing catch was fun. The red balloon floated in the air. It was a good day for the pig and the cat. They liked to play together.

<question>
Why was the pig sad?
</question>
<answer>
He was sad because no one was around to play.

===== DOC START split=test idx=491 id=story_qa_023539 =====
The little bear wanted honey. He wanted it right now! But Mama Bear said, "Not yet." It was not honey time.

"We need a plan," she said. "A method!" <definition>A method is a way of doing something.</definition>

Mama Bear told him to be mindful. <definition>Mindful means to pay attention to what you are doing.</definition> "Think about each step," she said.

First, they walked to the bee tree. Then, they put on their bee suits. Next, they used smoke to calm the bees. Finally, they got the honey!

The little bear was happy. He ate honey in the sun. He was very mindful of how good it tasted. Mama Bear smiled. Their method worked. It was honey time, at last!

<question>What did the little bear want?</question>
<answer>The little bear wanted honey.</answer>

<question>Did Mama Bear give him honey right away?</question>
<answer>No, Mama Bear did not give him honey right away.</answer>

<question>What did Mama Bear say they needed?</question>
<answer>Mama Bear said they needed a plan.</answer>

<question>What is a method?</question>
<answer>A method is a way of doing something.</answer>

<question>What did Mama Bear tell the little bear to be?</question>
<answer>Mama Bear told him to be mindful.</answer>

<question>What does mindful mean?</question>
<answer>Mindful means to pay attention to what you are doing.</answer>

<question>Where did they walk first?</question>
<answer>They walked to the bee tree.</answer>

<question>What did they put on?</question>
<answer>They put on their bee suits.</answer>

<question>What did they use to calm the bees?</question>
<answer>They used smoke to calm the bees.</answer>

<question>What did the little bear do with the honey?</question>
<answer>The little bear ate the honey.</answer>

<question>Where did he eat the honey?</question>

The little frog lived by a big rock. He loved to catch bugs. One day, he saw a funny bug. It was big and brown. It looked leathery. <definition>Leathery means something that looks and feels like leather, which is animal skin.</definition> The frog tried to catch it, but it flew away.

"Hello!" said a small, round ladybug. "That was a beetle!"

"Oh," said the frog. "Are you a beetle?"

"No! I am greek!" said the ladybug. <definition>Greek is something from Greece, a country far away.</definition> "I came here by the wind." She smiled. "Want to play?" The frog smiled back. "Yes!"

The palinuridae swam closer. It had long feelers. The crab hid in his shell. He peeked out with one eye. The palinuridae tickled his shell. The crab wiggled.

The palinuridae wanted to play. It poked the crab again. The crab came out of his shell. He made a funny face. The palinuridae laughed. Then they swam off together in the sea. They were friends.

Timmy loved pizza. He ate his slice fast. But, he wanted more! He looked at his friend, Lily. Lily was eating very slowly.

"Are you going to eat that?" Timmy asked. Lily shook her head. "I am full," she said. Timmy smiled. "Can I have it?" he asked. Lily gave Timmy her pizza.

Now Timmy had two slices! He felt happy. He ate the second slice even faster. Pizza was his favorite. He wished they could divide the pizza again! Maybe tomorrow they would get more pizza. He hoped so!

===== DOC START split=test idx=505 id=story_qa_024325 =====
The pig lived by a big red barn. He liked to play with his friends. One day, the pig wanted to act in a play. <definition>Act means to pretend to be someone else.</definition> He asked his friends, "Will you act in a play with me?" The cow said yes. The horse said yes too. "I will write the play," said the pig. The play was about a trip to the moon. The pig was the astronaut. The cow was the moon. The horse was the rocket. They had fun. The end.

<question>Where did the pig live?</question>
<answer>The pig lived by a big red barn.</answer>

<question>Who did the pig like to play with?</question>
<answer>He liked to play with his friends.</answer>

<answer>The pig wanted to act in a play.</answer>

<answer>Act means to pretend to be someone else.</answer>

<answer>The cow and the horse said yes.</answer>

<question>Who wrote the play?</question>
<answer>The pig wrote the play.</answer>

<answer>The play was about a trip to the moon.</answer>

Timmy was a small bear. He liked to play all day. He liked physical games the best. <definition>Physical means using your body.</definition> He would run and jump. He would climb trees.

One day, Timmy's mom said, "Timmy, it's time to learn about bearing." <definition>Bearing is knowing where you are and where you are going.</definition> Timmy asked, "What is bearing?"

Mom said, "It's how you find your way in the woods." She showed Timmy how to look at the sun. She showed him how to look at the trees. "The moss grows on the north side," she said.

Timmy practiced his bearing. He walked around the yard. He pointed north. He pointed south. He had so much fun learning. Now Timmy could play and never get lost!

<answer>Timmy is a bear.</answer>

<answer>Physical means using your body.</answer>

<answer>He would run and jump and climb trees.</answer>

<answer>Bearing is knowing where you are and where you are going.</answer>

<answer>Moss grows on the north side.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=523 id=story_qa_025182 =====
The little bear Cubby loved to play. He loved to play as a superhero. He made a mask from a paper plate. He put on his cape. His cape was a red towel.

Cubby gave a speech. It was a very impassioned speech. <definition>Impassioned means to show a lot of feeling.</definition> "I will save the forest!" he said. He ran into the woods.

First, he saw a squirrel. The squirrel was stuck. It was stuck in a bush. Cubby pulled the bush open. The squirrel ran away.

Next, he saw a bird. The bird had fallen from its nest. Cubby put the bird back in its nest.

Cubby felt good. He was happy he helped. He was a good superhero. He went home for lunch. He told his mom about his day. She smiled at him.

<question>What is the bear's name?</question>
<answer>The bear's name is Cubby.</answer>

<answer>Cubby liked to play as a superhero.</answer>

<answer>Cubby made a mask from a paper plate.</answer>

<question>What was Cubby's cape?</question>
<answer>Cubby's cape was a red towel.</answer>

<answer>Cubby said he would save the forest.</answer>

<answer>Impassioned means to show a lot of feeling.</answer>

<question>Who did Cubby see first?</question>

The town decided to fix the park. They got a new slide. They got new swings. Now the park was fun for everyone. The kids played all day. They were very happy.

A wise owl was living nearby. <definition>Living means to be alive.</definition> He heard the ants and snails argue. He said, "Everyone gets a turn! You all get equal time to play the games you want."

The ants and snails thought about this. Then, the snails got to pick a game. They picked hide-and-seek in the garden. The ants had to pay attention. <definition>Attention means to watch or listen carefully.</definition> The snails were very good at hiding! Soon, everyone was having fun. Playing together was better than arguing.

"Hello," the note said. "I need help!"

Timmy looked at the snail. The snail was acting in a strange manner. <definition>Manner means the way someone or something acts.</definition> It was moving very fast. It was a brisk pace for a snail! <definition>Brisk means quick and energetic.</definition>

Timmy picked up the snail. "What is wrong?" he asked. The snail pointed to its empty food bowl. "Oh!" said Timmy. He gave the snail a big piece of lettuce. The snail was happy.

He asked his mom, "Is this real?" His mom looked at the sign. "No, dear. That sign is not real. Someone is being silly." <definition>Being means existing or happening.</definition>

"They are acting fraudulently." <definition>Fraudulently means doing something dishonest to trick people.</definition> "We don't want any honey from them!" <definition>Any means even one.</definition> The bear nodded. "Can I ride in the truck?" asked the cub. Mama Bear said, "Not today. We need to pick more berries first."

The cub picked and picked. He filled his bowl. He still wanted more! Mama Bear showed him a secret spot. It had even more berries! The cub's eyes grew big. He picked and picked until his tummy was full. He was happy. He did not want any more berries.

Tim had a toy car. It was his favorite. Tim wanted to find a good place for his car. He looked all around the house.

First, he placed it on the table. But Mom said, "No, Tim! Not there."

Next, he placed it on the chair. But Dad said, "No, Tim! Not there."

Then, he placed it on the floor. But his sister said, "No, Tim! I will trip!"

Tim was sad. Where could he place his car? He needed a good place. <definition>Place means a spot or area.</definition> He thought and thought.

Suddenly, Tim smiled. He ran to his room. He placed the car on his bed! It was the perfect place. Now his car was safe and sound. Tim was happy. He gave his car a hug. "You are safe in your place," he said.

<question>What toy did Tim have?</question>
<answer>Tim had a toy car.</answer>

<question>Was the toy car his favorite?</question>
<answer>Yes, the toy car was his favorite.</answer>

<question>Where did Tim first put the car?</question>
<answer>Tim first put the car on the table.</answer>

<question>Did his mom like the car on the table?</question>
<answer>No, his mom did not like the car on the table.</answer>

<question>Where did Tim put the car next?</question>
<answer>Tim put the car on the chair next.</answer>

<question>Did his dad like the car on the chair?</question>
<answer>No, his dad did not like the car on the chair.</answer>

<question>Where did Tim put the car after the chair?</question>
<answer>Tim put the car on the floor after the chair.</answer>

<question>Who said Tim could not put the car on the floor?</question>
<answer>His sister said Tim could not put the car on the floor.</answer>

<question>What does place mean?</question>
<answer>Place means a spot or area.</answer>

<question>Where did Tim finally put the car?</question>
<answer>Tim finally put the car on his bed.</answer>

<question>Was Tim happy with the final place?</question>
<answer>Yes, Tim was happy with the final place.</answer>

<question>What did Tim do to his car at the end?</question>
<answer>Tim gave his car a hug.</answer>
===== DOC END =====

===== DOC START split=test idx=560 id=story_qa_027346 =====
Lily had long brown hair. She liked to brush it. One day, Lily planted a seed. She wanted to see a plant grow. <definition>Growth means getting bigger.</definition> She gave it water. She gave it sun. She waited and waited. Nothing happened.

Lily felt sad. She looked at her hair. It was long. It kept growing! The plant was not growing as fast as her hair. <definition>As means at the same time.</definition>

Then, Lily saw a tiny sprout. It was green! The plant was growing. Lily smiled. She was so happy. She would keep taking care of the plant. She knew it would grow big and tall, just like her hair! She watered it every day. She talked to it. The plant liked Lily as much as Lily liked the plant.

<question>What color was Lily's hair?</question>
<answer>Lily's hair was brown.</answer>

<question>What did Lily like to do with her hair?</question>
<answer>She liked to brush it.</answer>

<answer>Lily planted a seed.</answer>

<answer>She wanted to see a plant grow.</answer>

<answer>Growth means getting bigger.</answer>

<answer>She gave it water and sun.</answer>

<answer>Lily felt sad.</answer>

<answer>Lily's hair was growing fast.</answer>

The big red dog, Pat, loved to play. He loved to run fast! Pat ran in the park. He saw a pile of leaves. He ran right through it! This was causing leaves to fly everywhere. <definition>Causing means making something happen.</definition>

A little girl sat on a bench. She was reading a book. The leaves landed all on her! She giggled. Pat wagged his tail. He did not mean to do that.

Pat picked up a stick. He dropped it on the ground. Then he picked up other things. <definition>Things are objects that you can see and touch.</definition> He picked up a ball. He picked up a pinecone.

"Woof!" said Pat. He wanted to play fetch. The little girl threw the ball. Pat ran to get it. Playing fetch was so much fun! He was not causing any trouble now. He was just a good dog playing on a nice day.

The pig was in a pen. He did not like the pen. It was small. He wanted to play. He wanted to run. He saw a hole in the fence. It was a big hole. "I can get out!" said the pig.

He pushed and pushed. He was stuck! The hole was not big enough. The pig was sad. He did not want to stay in the pen. He wanted to play with his friends.

A cow walked by. The cow saw the pig. "Are you stuck?" asked the cow. The pig nodded. He was stuck tight. The cow pulled the fence. She pulled very hard. The hole got bigger. The pig wiggled free! He was not stuck anymore. He ran to play.

<answer>The pig was in a pen.</answer>

<answer>He got stuck.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=577 id=story_qa_028616 =====
The little blue car was fast. It zoomed down the road. Timmy was in the car. He liked to go fast. He liked to sing as he drove.

Suddenly, Timmy saw a sign. It noted that the road was closed. <definition>Noted means that something was written down or pointed out.</definition> "Oh no!" said Timmy. He had to find another way.

He saw a small passage through the trees. <definition>A passage is a way to get from one place to another.</definition> It looked bumpy. Timmy was not sure. Then, he saw a person walking towards him. <definition>A person is another word for a man, woman, or child.</definition>

"Is this passage safe?" Timmy asked.

The person smiled. "Yes," she said. "But go slow as you drive. It is very bumpy!"

Timmy thanked her. He drove very slowly. The car bounced up and down. It was a fun ride after all!

<question>What color was the car?</question>
<answer>The car was blue.</answer>

<question>Was the car fast or slow?</question>
<answer>The car was fast.</answer>

<answer>Timmy was in the car.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy like to do as he drove?</question>
<answer>He liked to sing.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy see?</question>
<answer>Timmy saw a sign.</answer>

<answer>The sign said the road was closed.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=578 id=story_qa_028666 =====
The little seed sat in the dirt. It was small and brown. It wanted to grow. <definition>Grow means to get bigger.</definition> The sun shined bright. The rain fell down. The seed started to grow a little root.

Then, it grew a little sprout. A sprout is like a baby plant. The sprout pushed up, up, up. It grew towards the sun. It needed the sun to grow big and strong.

Soon, the sprout had leaves. The leaves were green and soft. As the days went by, the plant got taller. It grew flowers. The flowers were as bright as the sun. Bees came to visit the flowers. The plant was happy to grow big and strong. It gave the bees sweet food.

<question>Where did the little seed sit?</question>
<answer>The seed sat in the dirt.</answer>

<question>What color was the seed?</question>
<answer>The seed was brown.</answer>

<question>What did the seed want to do?</question>
<answer>The seed wanted to grow.</answer>

<question>What does grow mean?</question>
<answer>Grow means to get bigger.</answer>

<question>What shined bright?</question>
<answer>The sun shined bright.</answer>

<question>What fell down?</question>
<answer>The rain fell down.</answer>

<question>What did the seed grow first?</question>

===== DOC START split=test idx=580 id=story_qa_028788 =====
The farm was big. A small dog lived there. He liked to bark. He barked at the cows. He barked at the chickens. He even barked at the wind!

One day, he heard a noise. It came from the neighboring farm. <definition>Neighboring means close by.</definition> The dog was scared, but he had courage. <definition>Courage means being brave.</definition> He ran to the fence.

He saw a cat. The cat was stuck by a big tree. The cat looked sad, as if it might cry. The dog knew he had to help.

Long ago some people were kept in slavery. <definition>Slavery means being owned by another person and made to work without pay.</definition> The dog knew that wasn't right. He would free this cat!

He barked and barked. A farmer came. The farmer helped the cat. The cat was free! The dog wagged his tail. He was happy he helped.

<question>Where did the small dog live?</question>
<answer>The small dog lived on a big farm.</answer>

<answer>He liked to bark.</answer>

<answer>Courage means being brave.</answer>

"Those are cruciferae," Mom said. <definition>Cruciferae is a family of plants that includes cabbage, broccoli, and cauliflower.</definition> "They will produce food soon." <definition>Produce means to make or grow something.</definition>

Lily was excited. She watered the cruciferae every day. She pulled out any weeds. Soon, little green heads started to grow.

"Broccoli!" Mom said. "The cruciferae produce broccoli!"

Lily helped Mom pick the broccoli. They washed it and cooked it. Lily ate a big plate of broccoli. It was the best broccoli ever. She loved helping the garden produce food.

Today, Bobo learned about the word "unit." <definition>A unit is a single thing that is part of a group.</definition> His teacher said a banana was one unit. A bunch of bananas was a group. ===== DOC START split=test idx=597 id=story_qa_029833 =====
Timmy the turtle loved to read. He read books about bugs. He read books about boats. He even read books about outer space! Timmy was very smart. He was so smart, he was advanced. <definition>Advanced means being at a higher level than others.</definition>

One day, Timmy had a race. It was a race to the big oak tree. All the fast animals were there. There was a rabbit. There was a fox. And there was Timmy!

The race started. The rabbit zoomed ahead. <definition>Ahead means to be in front.</definition> He was very fast. The fox ran fast too. Timmy walked slowly. He read a book as he walked.

The rabbit stopped to eat carrots. The fox stopped to chase butterflies. Timmy kept walking and reading. He walked past the rabbit. He walked past the fox. Timmy got to the oak tree first! He was the winner because he never stopped!

<question>What did Timmy the turtle love to do?</question>
<answer>Timmy loved to read.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy read books about?</question>
<answer>He read books about bugs, boats, and outer space.</answer>

<question>What does advanced mean?</question>
<answer>Advanced means being at a higher level than others.</answer>

<question>Where was the race to?</question>
<answer>The race was to the big oak tree.</answer>

<question>Who was in the race?</question>
<answer>The rabbit, the fox, and Timmy were in the race.</answer>

<question>Who zoomed ahead?</question>
<answer>The rabbit zoomed ahead.</answer>

<question>What does ahead mean?</question>
<answer>Ahead means to be in front.</answer>

<question>What did the rabbit stop to do?</question>
<answer>The rabbit stopped to eat carrots.</answer>

<question>What did the fox stop to do?</question>
<answer>The fox stopped to chase butterflies.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy do while he walked?</question>
<answer>Timmy read a book.</answer>

<question>Who got to the oak tree first?</question>
<answer>Timmy got to the oak tree first.</answer>

<question>Why did Timmy win?</question>
<answer>He won because he never stopped.</answer>
===== DOC END =====

Suddenly, a big fish was appearing. <definition>Appearing means showing up.</definition> It jumped out of the water! Lily and Tom gasped. The fish was gone fast! The duck family kept swimming. Lily and Tom laughed. It was a fun day!

"Chirp, chirp," he cried. A wise owl heard him. She hooted, "What is wrong, little bird?"

"I can't find my nest!" he said. "I am a native bird here." <definition>Native means you were born in a place.</definition>

The owl said, "Think! Where do you like to fly?"

The little bird thought hard. "I like the big tree!" he said. "Especially the one by the river!" <definition>Especially means more than others.</definition>

The owl smiled. "Go look there!"

The little bird flew to the big tree. There it was! His nest! He was so happy. He chirped a happy song.

<answer>A wise owl heard him.</answer>

<answer>Native means you were born in a place.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=639 id=story_qa_032487 =====
Timmy did not want any germs. He washed his hands all day. He did not want to get sick. He learned about germs in school. Some germs are called staphylococcal. <definition>Staphylococcal is a type of germ that can make you sick.</definition> They can make your skin itchy. They can make your tummy hurt. Timmy used soap to wash the germs away. He washed before lunch. He washed after playing. He washed after petting his cat. Now Timmy felt very clean! He could play all day.

<question>Why did Timmy wash his hands?</question>
<answer>Timmy washed his hands because he did not want any germs.</answer>

<question>Did Timmy want to get sick?</question>
<answer>No, Timmy did not want to get sick.</answer>

<question>Where did Timmy learn about germs?</question>
<answer>Timmy learned about germs in school.</answer>

<question>What is staphylococcal?</question>
<answer>Staphylococcal is a type of germ that can make you sick.</answer>

<question>What can staphylococcal do to your body?</question>
<answer>They can make your skin itchy and your tummy hurt.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy use to wash the germs away?</question>
<answer>Timmy used soap to wash the germs away.</answer>

<question>When did Timmy wash his hands?</question>
<answer>He washed before lunch, after playing, and after petting his cat.</answer>

<question>How did Timmy feel after washing his hands?</question>
<answer>Timmy felt very clean.</answer>

<question>What could Timmy do after he felt clean?</question>
<answer>Timmy could play all day.</answer>

The other toys felt sad. They needed the train. They had a meeting. It was a toy union. <definition>A union is a group of people who work together.</definition> They wanted to help the train.

The teddy bear said, "We need oil!" The rag doll said, "Yes! Oil will help!" They looked for oil. They found some! They put oil on the train's wheels. The train started to move! He was happy. The toys were happy, especially the train. The union had saved the day!

A little girl came out. She had a red bow in her hair. <definition>In means to be inside of something.</definition> "Can you help me?" asked the car. "I am lost." The little girl smiled. "I can help! I know the way back to the road."

The little girl got in the car. She told the car where to go. Soon, the car was back on the road. "Thank you!" said the little blue car. The little girl waved goodbye. The little blue car was happy again.

===== DOC START split=test idx=658 id=story_qa_033010 =====
Pip was a pig. Pip loved to play in the mud. One day, Pip saw a sign. The sign was in the public park. <definition>Public means a place where everyone can go.</definition> The sign had rules. One rule was no pigs. Pip felt sad.

His friend, Dot the dog, saw Pip. "Why are you sad?" asked Dot. Pip showed her the sign. Dot looked closely. "That is a false sign!" she barked. <definition>False means not true.</definition> "It is marked wrong!"

Dot pointed to a small sticker on the sign. The sticker said, "Pigs Welcome!" Pip jumped for joy. He ran into the park. He rolled on the grass. He splashed in a puddle. Pip was happy to play in the park. He was not breaking any rules.

<question>What kind of animal was Pip?</question>
<answer>Pip was a pig.</answer>

<question>What did Pip love to do?</question>
<answer>Pip loved to play in the mud.</answer>

<question>What did Pip see one day?</question>
<answer>Pip saw a sign.</answer>

<question>Where was the sign?</question>
<answer>The sign was in the public park.</answer>

<question>What does public mean?</question>
<answer>Public means a place where everyone can go.</answer>

<question>What was one rule on the sign?</question>
<answer>One rule was no pigs.</answer>

<question>How did Pip feel when he saw the sign?</question>
<answer>Pip felt sad.</answer>

<question>Who saw Pip?</question>
<answer>Dot the dog saw Pip.</answer>

<question>What did Dot ask Pip?</question>
<answer>Dot asked why he was sad.</answer>

<question>What did Pip show Dot?</question>
<answer>Pip showed her the sign.</answer>

<answer>Dot said it was a false sign.</answer>

<question>What does false mean?</question>
<answer>False means not true.</answer>

<question>What did Dot point to on the sign?</question>
<answer>Dot pointed to a small sticker.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=659 id=story_qa_033043 =====
Patty the Pig was a detective. She looked for clues. Clues are evidence. <definition>Evidence are things that help show if something is true.</definition> She lived in the southwestern part of town. One day, Mrs. Goat came to see her.

"My pie is gone!" cried Mrs. Goat. "Someone took it!"

Patty put on her thinking cap. "I will find the thief," she said. She looked all around. She found a note by the pie dish.

"Hmm," Patty said. "Let's look at this handwriting." <definition>Handwriting is how someone writes with their hand.</definition>

The note said, "The pie was yummy!" Patty knew this was important evidence.

Patty looked closely at the handwriting. It looked as if a chicken wrote it! <definition>As if means like something is true, but it might not be.</definition> Patty knew just the chicken. It was Charles! She went to Charles' house and found crumbs on his face. Patty smiled. "Case closed!"

<question>
What kind of animal was Patty?
</question>
<answer>
Patty was a pig.
</answer>

<question>
What was Patty's job?
</question>
<answer>
Patty was a detective.
</answer>

<question>
What did Patty look for?
</question>
<answer>
Patty looked for clues.
</answer>

What are clues?
</question>
<answer>
Clues are evidence.
</answer>

</question>
<answer>
Evidence are things that help show if something is true.
</answer>

Who came to see Patty?
</question>

Mrs. Goat came to see Patty.
</answer>

Tim bought the rock candy. He laughed. It looked like a rock, but it tasted like sweet berries. He liked the surprise. He told all his friends about the rock candy. They all went to the store to buy some. The confectioner was happy. Everyone loved his tricky, tasty treat.

<definition>An attendant is a person who helps people.</definition> A nice attendant worked at the zoo. The attendant wore a blue hat. He smiled at everyone. He helped Leo find the monkeys.

"Thank you!" said Leo. He gave the attendant a high five.

Frederick wanted to see the penguins eat. The attendant knew when they ate. He told Frederick to go to the penguin pool at three o'clock.

Leo and Frederick went to the penguin pool. They watched the penguins swim. Then, a zookeeper came with a bucket of fish. He threw the fish to the penguins. The penguins gobbled them up!

"Wow!" said Frederick. "Penguins are my favorite!"

Leo smiled. He liked the penguins too. The zoo was a great place to visit. He was glad the attendant helped them.

Lily saw a big, black rock. It was shiny. She picked it up. "Wow!" she said. It felt smooth.

Lily giggled. "Hello, Mr. Turtle!" she called. The turtle kept walking into the tall grass. He wanted to go back to nature. Lily smiled. She was happy she saw the turtle. She went back to playing near the trees. She looked for more animals. Maybe she would see a bird. Or maybe she would see a frog. She loved exploring.

===== DOC START split=test idx=673 id=story_qa_033890 =====
The cat was stuck in a tree. Oh no! The firemen came to help. They looked up at the cat. The cat looked down at them. The wait felt very prolonged. <definition>Prolonged means lasting for a long time.</definition> It felt like a long, long time.

"We need a plan," said the first fireman. "Yes, a good plan," said the second fireman. They had to confer. <definition>Confer means to talk to each other to make a decision.</definition> They needed to talk.

The firemen started to talk. They talked about ladders. They talked about nets. They talked about treats for the cat.

"I have an idea!" said a little girl. "My kitty likes toys!"

The firemen looked at each other. They liked that idea! They used a toy mouse. The cat saw the toy. It climbed down to get it. Hooray! The cat was safe. The firemen were happy. The little girl was happy too.

<answer>The cat was stuck in a tree.</answer>

<answer>The firemen came to help.</answer>

<answer>Prolonged means lasting for a long time.</answer>

<answer>They said they needed a plan.</answer>

<answer>Confer means to talk to each other.</answer>

<answer>They talked about ladders and nets.</answer>

<answer>A little girl had an idea.</answer>

<answer>She said her kitty liked toys.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=688 id=story_qa_034715 =====
The little blue car sat by the road. It was old and a little damaged. <definition>Damaged means something is broken or not working right.</definition> A girl named Lily saw the car. She wanted to fix it.

Lily had a box of tools in her garage. She took the tools to the car. She looked at the flat tire. She looked at the broken light. "I can fix this!" she said.

First, Lily found a new tire. She put it on the car. Then, she found a new light. She put it in the car. The car looked much better!

An old man walked by. He saw Lily working. "That was my car," he said. "It got damaged a long time ago."

"I fixed it!" said Lily. "I want to give it back to you!" <definition>Give means to hand something to someone.</definition>

The old man smiled. "Thank you," he said. "You are very kind." He got in the car and drove away. Lily was happy she could help.

===== DOC START split=test idx=693 id=story_qa_035241 =====
The ants lived in a big hill. Ants are commonly black or red. <definition>Commonly means that you see it a lot.</definition> One day, a big bird wanted to attack the ant hill. <definition>Attack means to try and hurt something.</definition> The bird was being mean. <definition>Being means how someone is acting.</definition> The bird pecked at the hill. The ants ran out.

"We must stop him!" said a tiny ant. He knew a lot about geology. <definition>Geology is the study of rocks and the earth.</definition> "I have an idea!" he said. The ant ran to a big rock. He pushed the rock. The rock rolled down the hill. It rolled right at the bird!

The bird saw the rock. He flew away fast. The ants were safe. They cheered for the tiny ant. He saved the day with geology! Now the ants could go back to being happy in their hill.

It was a very tall castle! They worked hard on it all day. Finally, they finished the sandcastle. <definition>Finished means done.</definition> It looked so cool! Everyone cheered. They were proud of what they made together.

===== DOC START split=test idx=708 id=story_qa_036139 =====

Today, Timmy was playing with his boat in the tub. He filled the tub with water. He put the boat in. “Ahoy, matey!” he shouted.

Suddenly, a part fell off the boat! It was a small, round part. Timmy looked at it. What was it? He asked his dad. His dad laughed. "That's the boat's naval," he said. <definition>A naval is where boats are built and fixed.</definition>

Timmy put the naval back on the boat. Now the boat was ready for more fun. He sailed it around the tub. He made boat noises. It was the best part of his day. He loved his little blue boat. He would take good care of it.

We are having so much fun. We dig in the sand. We find little crabs. The crabs are small. They are hiding.

One day, he hopped to a new area. This area was near the woods. It was a distinct place. <definition>Distinct means different from everything else.</definition> He had never been there before. He saw other frogs. They were a different color.

"Hello!" he said to the other frogs.

"Hello!" they said back.

They showed him their part of the pond. It was fun to see a new place. He liked making new friends in the water. Now he had two favorite places to swim! He could visit his old friends and his new friends. The pond was a big and happy place.

The knot was very tight. It was like a binding. <definition>Binding means something that holds things together tightly.</definition> The kitten tried to pull it apart. She could not!

“I can make an approximate guess,” said the cat. <definition>Approximate means close to the real number, but not exact.</definition> “This knot has one hundred strings!” The kitten did not care. She batted the yarn. It was fun! She made it her toy.

===== DOC START split=test idx=720 id=story_qa_036998 =====

"It's not broken," the doctor said. "But it is sore." He prescribed some medicine. <definition>Prescribed means the doctor told you to take a certain medicine.</definition> Lily had to rest. She could not go climbing for a week.

Lily was sad. She wanted to play. Her dad brought her a book about bugs. The book talked about the genus of beetles. <definition>Genus is a group of similar living things, like animals or plants.</definition> Lily learned so much! She learned about ladybugs and beetles. She even learned about ants! Lily decided she liked learning about bugs. She could not wait to tell her friends. When her clavicle felt better, she could tell them all about the bugs!

===== DOC START split=test idx=723 id=story_qa_037172 =====
The little duck had webbed feet. <definition>Webbed means having skin between the toes.</definition> She lived by a big lake. She liked to swim all day. The duck swam as fast as she could. She liked to race the other ducks. One day, a big frog hopped by. The frog wanted to race too. The duck laughed. "Okay!" she said. "Let's see who is faster." They raced to the other side of the lake. The duck won! She was very happy. The frog was happy too. They were good friends.

<question>What kind of feet did the duck have?</question>
<answer>The duck had webbed feet.</answer>

<question>What does webbed mean?</question>
<answer>Webbed means having skin between the toes.</answer>

<question>Where did the duck live?</question>
<answer>The duck lived by a big lake.</answer>

<question>What did the duck like to do all day?</question>
<answer>She liked to swim all day.</answer>

<question>Who did the duck like to race?</question>
<answer>She liked to race the other ducks.</answer>

<question>Who hopped by one day?</question>
<answer>A big frog hopped by.</answer>

<question>What did the frog want to do?</question>
<answer>The frog wanted to race too.</answer>

<question>Did the duck race the frog?</question>
<answer>Yes, the duck raced the frog.</answer>

<question>Who won the race?</question>
<answer>The duck won the race.</answer>

<question>How did the duck feel?</question>
<answer>She was very happy.</answer>

<question>How did the frog feel?</question>
<answer>The frog was happy too.</answer>

<question>What were the duck and the frog?</question>
<answer>They were good friends.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=725 id=story_qa_037249 =====
The sun came up in the eastern sky. It was a bright, happy sun. Lily went outside. She wanted to plant some flowers. <definition>Plant means to put something in the ground so it can grow.</definition>

She had some seeds. She put them in the dirt. She gave them water. "Grow, little flowers, grow!" she said.

Her friend, Ben, came over. "What are you doing?" he asked.

"I am talking to my flowers," Lily said. "Flowers like to be talked to."

"Do flowers understand our language?" Ben asked. <definition>Language means the words that people use to talk to each other.</definition>

"Maybe," said Lily. "Some people think they do!" She smiled. "Maybe flowers speak a different language!"

<question>Where did the sun come up?</question>
<answer>The sun came up in the eastern sky.</answer>

<question>What kind of sun was it?</question>
<answer>It was a bright, happy sun.</answer>

<question>What did Lily want to do?</question>
<answer>She wanted to plant some flowers.</answer>

<question>What does plant mean?</question>
<answer>Plant means to put something in the ground so it can grow.</answer>

<question>What did Lily put in the dirt?</question>
<answer>She put seeds in the dirt.</answer>

<question>What did Lily give the seeds?</question>
<answer>She gave them water.</answer>

<question>What did Lily say to the flowers?</question>
<answer>She said grow little flowers grow.</answer>

<question>Who came over to Lily's house?</question>
<answer>Her friend Ben came over.</answer>

<question>What did Ben ask Lily?</question>
<answer>He asked what are you doing.</answer>

<question>Who was Lily talking to?</question>
<answer>Lily was talking to her flowers.</answer>

<question>What does language mean?</question>
<answer>Language means the words that people use to talk to each other.</answer>

<question>Did Lily think flowers understood her?</question>
<answer>Maybe flowers understand her.</answer>

<question>What did Lily say about flowers speaking?</question>
<answer>She said maybe flowers speak a different language.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=726 id=story_qa_037164 =====

One day, Lily made a plan. She would put the plant by the window. The sun would help it grow. She gave it water every day. She talked to the plant. "Grow big!" she said.

Soon, the plant grew taller. It had more leaves. Lily was happy. She knew her plan worked. The plant was made with love. She took good care of it. The plant grew bigger and bigger because it was made by her. It was her special plant. She loved her green plant.

===== DOC START split=test idx=732 id=story_qa_037339 =====

Pip lived by a big park. He loved to play in the park. He would chase the squirrels. He would bark at the birds. Sometimes he would just roll in the grass.

One day, Pip saw a little girl. She was sitting on a bench. She looked very sad. Pip walked over to her. He wagged his tail. The girl smiled. She patted Pip on the head. Pip licked her hand. The girl felt much better. Pip's wagging tail made her happy.

This rock was special, especially to Lily. She put the rock in her pocket. Now she and Patches could play. Patches was happy again. They ran home as fast as they could.

"Percy is a good pig," he said. "He likes to play in the sun." The little girl gave Percy an apple. Percy ate it in one bite! The little girl laughed. Percy was happy. It was a very good day.

The cat was sad. He wanted another friend. He walked down the street. He saw a big building. It was a hotel. <definition>A hotel is a place where people can stay when they are traveling.</definition>

A little dog walked out of the hotel. The dog was small and fluffy. The cat walked up to the dog. "Want to play?" asked the cat. The dog wagged its tail. The cat and the dog played all day. They were good friends. The cat was not sad anymore. He had a new friend.

===== DOC START split=test idx=737 id=story_qa_037395 =====
The little bluebird wanted to build a nest. She needed a good place. She looked by the big oak tree. "This is perfect!" she sang. But, uh oh! The branch was too weak. It would not support her nest. <definition>Support means to hold something up.</definition>

She needed a stronger branch. She flew to the maple tree. This branch was strong! She gathered twigs and leaves. She worked very hard. She wove them together. Soon, she had a cozy nest. It was held up by the strong branch.

"Chirp, chirp!" she sang. "My nest is safe and sound!" She was happy to have a good place to lay her eggs. The nest would support her babies. Now she could start her family. She loved her new home. It was the best nest ever!

<question>What did the bluebird want to build?</question>
<answer>The bluebird wanted to build a nest.</answer>

<question>Where did she first look for a place to build?</question>
<answer>She looked by the big oak tree.</answer>

<question>Why couldn't she build there?</question>
<answer>The branch was too weak.</answer>

<question>What does support mean?</question>
<answer>Support means to hold something up.</answer>

<question>Where did she go next?</question>
<answer>She flew to the maple tree.</answer>

<question>Was the branch strong?</question>
<answer>Yes, the branch was strong.</answer>

<question>What did she gather for her nest?</question>
<answer>She gathered twigs and leaves.</answer>

<question>What kind of nest did she make?</question>
<answer>She made a cozy nest.</answer>

<question>What did she sing?</question>
<answer>She sang "Chirp, chirp!"</answer>

<question>Why was she happy?</question>
<answer>She was happy to have a good place to lay her eggs.</answer>

<question>What will the nest support?</question>
<answer>The nest will support her babies.</answer>

<question>What did she think of her new home?</question>
<answer>She loved her new home.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=739 id=story_qa_037599 =====
The big red bus went by. A nice person waved from the window. <definition>A person is a boy, a girl, or a grown-up.</definition> The bus was going to the park. Lily wanted to go to the park too. She asked her mom, "Can we go to the park?"

Mom said, "Yes! But first, clean your room." Lily put all her toys in the toy box. She put her books on the shelf. Cleaning was hard work. She only had one more thing to do. She had to pick up the part of her doll that fell off. <definition>Part means a piece of something.</definition>

Lily found the doll's arm. She put it back on. Now her room was clean. "All done!" said Lily. Mom smiled. "Let's go to the park!" Lily and Mom went to the park and had lots of fun.

<question>What color was the bus?</question>
<answer>The bus was red.</answer>

<question>Where was the bus going?</question>
<answer>The bus was going to the park.</answer>

<question>Who wanted to go to the park?</question>
<answer>Lily wanted to go to the park.</answer>

<question>What did Lily ask her mom?</question>
<answer>Lily asked if they could go to the park.</answer>

<question>What did Mom say Lily had to do first?</question>
<answer>Mom said to clean her room.</answer>

<question>Where did Lily put her toys?</question>

One day, Lily went to school. Her class was learning about culture. <definition>Culture is the way of life of a group of people, including their food, clothes, and art.</definition> The teacher showed them pictures. They saw different types of houses. One house was made of mud. Another was made of ice.

Lily learned that people live in many different ways. It was fun to see. Lily thought about her small car. She wondered what kind of cars people had in other cultures. Maybe they had wooden cars. Or cars made of straw. That would be neat! Lily smiled. She loved learning new things. She wanted to learn about everything in the world.

===== DOC START split=test idx=746 id=story_qa_038002 =====
The sun was up. It was high above. The birds flew below. They flew by a big tree. <definition>Below means lower than something else.</definition>

"Chirp, chirp!" said one bird. "Chirp!" said another.

"What is your name?" asked the first bird.

"My name is Blue," said the other bird. "What is your name?"

"My name is Red," said the first bird. "I have two seeds. <definition>Two means one plus one.</definition> Do you want one?"

"Yes, please!" said Blue.

Red gave Blue a seed. They ate them together. They were very happy.

A cat watched them. The cat's name was Patches. Patches wanted to play.

"Meow!" said Patches.

Red and Blue flew away. They flew into the big tree. Patches could not climb the tree. He was sad. Maybe tomorrow they would play.

<question>Was the sun up or down?</question>
<answer>The sun was up.</answer>

<question>Where did the birds fly?</question>
<answer>They flew by a big tree.</answer>

<answer>Below means lower than something else.</answer>

<answer>One bird said chirp chirp.</answer>

<answer>The other bird's name was Blue.</answer>

<answer>The first bird's name was Red.</answer>

<answer>Two means one plus one.</answer>

<answer>Red gave Blue a seed.</answer>

The vacuum was still loud. Lily did not like the noise. She wished the vacuum would go away. Finally, the noise stopped. Lily slowly came out of the closet. She was happy the vacuum was gone. She went back to the fireplace. It was warm and cozy.

===== DOC START split=test idx=763 id=story_qa_038900 =====
The class made paper airplanes. Timmy said his plane was faultless. <definition>Faultless means perfect and without any mistakes.</definition> "It will fly the best!" he said. He had a theory. <definition>A theory is an idea that explains something.</definition> His theory was that pointy planes fly far.

Lily said her plane would fly better. Her plane was flat. Timmy laughed at her plane. "That will not work!" he said.

They went outside to fly the planes. Timmy threw his plane. It went up, then down. It crashed!

Lily threw her plane. It went up, up, up! It flew very far. Timmy was surprised. Maybe his theory was wrong. Lily smiled. "All planes can fly!" she said. Now they had a new theory. All planes are fun!

<question>What did the class make?</question>
<answer>The class made paper airplanes.</answer>

<answer>Faultless means perfect and without any mistakes.</answer>

<answer>His theory was that pointy planes fly far.</answer>

<answer>A theory is an idea that explains something.</answer>

<answer>She made a flat plane.</answer>

<answer>No, Timmy laughed at her plane.</answer>

One day, Mom said, "Timmy, clean your room!"

Timmy looked at his room. Toys were everywhere! In general, it was a mess. <definition>In general means usually or mostly.</definition> He started with the toy box. He put all the cars in one pile. He put all the trucks in another pile.

"This is too hard!" he said.

Dad came in. "Let's make it a game," he said. "We can put all the toys back in the box. Then, we can play!"

Timmy smiled. He liked games. He and Dad cleaned up fast. Soon, all the toys were back in the box. Timmy and Dad played with the toys. It was fun!

<answer>Timmy had a toy box.</answer>

<answer>Timmy's room was a mess.</answer>

Timmy smiled. "I can help! We can look for it together." They looked on the ground. They looked by the pond. They even looked up in the tree!

"Maybe your bearing is inside," said Timmy. Bear went inside his house. He looked and looked. "Aha!" said Bear. He found his bearing on the table! It was a small compass. Now Bear knew which way to go. He could be in the play after all.

One day, Pat's friend, Tim, came over. Tim only spoke english. Pat did not speak english. But they were still related. <definition>Related means that you are in the same family.</definition> Pat and Tim were best friends!

Tim said, "Want to play, Pat?" Pat did not understand the words. But he saw Tim point at the ball. Pat knew what that meant! He started to get down low. He wiggled his tail. Tim laughed. "Okay, Pat! Let's play fetch!" Pat barked happily. He loved to play ball with Tim. It was his favorite game.

<answer>The dog's name is Pat.</answer>

<answer>He would wiggle.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=775 id=story_qa_039442 =====
The little bear wanted a snack. He saw a big berry on a bush. "Yummy!" said the bear. He tried to reach it. But he was too short.

Someone walked by. It was a tall giraffe. "Can you help me?" asked the bear. The giraffe smiled. She used her long neck to get the berry.

The bear ate the berry fast. It went down his throat. <definition>Esophageal means having to do with the esophagus, which is the tube that connects your mouth to your stomach.</definition> The berry went down his esophageal area. "Thank you!" he said to the giraffe. "That was good!"

The giraffe was happy to help. She ate some leaves from the tall tree. The bear found another berry. He ate it slowly this time. He did not want to eat too fast! He wanted to taste it. It was sweet and juicy.

<question>What did the bear want?</question>
<answer>The bear wanted a snack.</answer>

<question>What did the bear see?</question>
<answer>He saw a big berry.</answer>

<question>Could the bear reach the berry?</question>
<answer>No, he was too short.</answer>

<question>Who walked by?</question>
<answer>A tall giraffe walked by.</answer>

<question>Did the giraffe help the bear?</question>
<answer>Yes, she helped the bear.</answer>

<question>How did the giraffe get the berry?</question>
<answer>She used her long neck.</answer>

<question>What did the bear say after eating the berry?</question>
<answer>He said thank you.</answer>

<question>What does esophageal mean?</question>
<answer>Esophageal means having to do with the esophagus.</answer>

<question>What did the giraffe eat?</question>
<answer>She ate some leaves.</answer>

<question>Did the bear find another berry?</question>
<answer>Yes, he found another berry.</answer>

<question>Did the bear eat the second berry fast or slow?</question>
<answer>He ate it slowly.</answer>

<question>Why did the bear eat it slowly?</question>
<answer>He wanted to taste it.</answer>

<question>How did the second berry taste?</question>
<answer>It was sweet and juicy.</answer>

The little pig, Pip, loved mud. He loved to roll in it. One day, Pip found a big mud puddle. He jumped in! But something went wrong. The mud felt hot! It started to burn his skin. Pip squealed. He needed help urgently! <definition>Urgently means you need something right away.</definition> He ran to his mom. Mom knew what to do. She started the washing process. <definition>Process means a set of actions that lead to a result.</definition> She washed Pip with cool water. The hot mud was the cause of his sore skin. <definition>Cause means what makes something happen.</definition> Pip felt much better after his bath. He learned to check the mud before he jumps in!

===== DOC START split=test idx=783 id=story_qa_039496 =====
The little bear loved honey. He loved being outside, too. One day, he found a big beehive. "Wow!" said the bear. He reached up to grab some honey.

Suddenly, bees flew out! They were not happy. The bees stung the bear's nose. "Ouch!" he cried. His nose got red and big. He ran away fast.

His friend, the fox, saw him. "What happened?" asked the fox. The bear told him about the bees. He was very affected by the bee stings. <definition>Affected means that something happened that made you feel sad or hurt.</definition>

The fox felt bad for the bear. "Being stung is no fun," said the fox. <definition>Being means what you are, or how you are.</definition> "Let's get you some ice!" They went to the river and found a big, cold rock. The bear put it on his nose. It felt better soon.

<question>What did the little bear love?</question>
<answer>The little bear loved honey.</answer>

<answer>The bear loved being outside.</answer>

<answer>He found a big beehive.</answer>

<answer>Bees flew out.</answer>

<answer>His nose got red and big.</answer>

One day, Timmy saw a big boat. It was very nautical. <definition>Nautical means having to do with boats and the sea.</definition> Timmy wanted to be on the boat. He walked sideways to the dock. He climbed aboard. "Hello!" he said. A sailor smiled. "Hello, little crab!" The sailor gave Timmy a tiny sailor hat. Timmy was so happy. He had a new, nautical, habitual place to be!

===== DOC START split=test idx=794 id=story_qa_040137 =====
Pip was a little bird. He wanted to fly high. But a branch was restricting his way. <definition>Restricting means stopping something from moving or growing freely.</definition> The branch was too low. Pip felt sad.

A big owl saw Pip. The owl felt sympathy for Pip. <definition>Sympathy is when you feel sorry for someone.</definition> "I can help!" hooted the owl.

The owl specified a plan. <definition>Specified means to say exactly what you want.</definition> "I will hold the branch," the owl said. "You fly under it!"

Pip chirped with joy. The owl held the branch high. Pip flew under it. He flew very high! Pip thanked the owl. Now Pip could fly anywhere!

<question>What kind of animal was Pip?</question>
<answer>Pip was a little bird.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=801 id=story_qa_041042 =====
A tiny snail lived in a big garden. He liked to slide on the leaves. He was a slow snail. He wanted to know more about snails.

His mom said, "Snails belong to a group called a genus." <definition>Genus is a name for a group of similar animals or plants.</definition>

The snail asked, "What is a genus?"

Mom said, "It is like a family for snails!"

The snail smiled. He had many cousins in his genus. He liked being a snail. He slid into a flower. The flower was big and red. He ate a little bit of the flower. Then he slid out. He went back to his mom.

"I love being a snail in my genus!" he said.

Mom smiled. "I love you too!"

The snail and his mom snuggled together. It was nice to be a snail.

<question>Where did the snail live?</question>
<answer>The snail lived in a big garden.</answer>

<answer>He liked to slide on the leaves.</answer>

<answer>He was a slow snail.</answer>

<answer>He wanted to know more about snails.</answer>

<answer>Genus is a name for a group of similar animals or plants.</answer>

<answer>She said it is like a family for snails.</answer>

<answer>He slid into a flower.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=810 id=story_qa_041480 =====
The sun was hot. Lily and Tom went to the park. They wanted to play by the big tree. It was a very leafy tree. It gave them lots of shade.

"Let's swing!" said Lily. The swings were near the tree. Tom ran to the swings. He liked to swing high.

Lily looked at the tree's roots. They were big and bumpy. Some roots were arcuate. <definition>Arcuate means shaped like a bow.</definition> Lily thought the arcuate roots looked like bridges for ants.

"I see ants!" she said. She watched the ants march up and down the roots. Tom jumped off the swing. He watched the ants too.

"They are busy," said Tom.

Lily and Tom played by the leafy tree all day. They liked the arcuate roots and the busy ants. It was a good day.

<answer>He liked to swing high.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=812 id=story_qa_041616 =====
The bug had a lot of wit. <definition>Wit means being funny and smart.</definition> He liked to tell jokes. One day, he saw a cloud. The cloud had a funny form. <definition>Form means the shape of something.</definition> It looked like a giant ice cream cone!

"Hey cloud," said the bug. "Are you going to melt?"

The cloud did not answer. It just floated away. The bug laughed. He told his friend, the frog, about the cloud.

"It was so funny!" said the bug. "It looked like ice cream! But clouds don't form from ice cream."

The frog smiled. "You are very funny, bug," he said. "You have a lot of wit."

<question>
What did the bug have a lot of?
</question>
<answer>
The bug had a lot of wit.
</answer>

<question>
What does wit mean?
</question>
<answer>
Wit means being funny and smart.
</answer>

<question>
What did the bug like to do?
</question>
<answer>
He liked to tell jokes.
</answer>

<question>
What did the bug see one day?
</question>
<answer>
He saw a cloud.
</answer>

<question>
What did the cloud look like?
</question>
<answer>
It looked like a giant ice cream cone.
</answer>

<question>
What does form mean?
</question>
<answer>
Form means the shape of something.
</answer>

<question>
What did the bug ask the cloud?
</question>
<answer>
He asked if it was going to melt.

"I will fix it!" Timmy said. But he could not. Lily was sad. Timmy was still sad. He wished he could go to Scotland. <definition>Scotland is a country far away.</definition> Maybe in Scotland, he could find a new toy. A toy just like Lily's. Then she would not be sad anymore. Tim jumped up and down. "You are fixed!" he told the truck. The little blue truck smiled. Tim and the truck went for a drive. They were happy to be together again. They drove all around the block. It was a fun day!

It looked like a shark! <definition>A shark is a type of fish that lives in the ocean and has sharp teeth.</definition> This shark was a lamnidae. <definition>Lamnidae is a family of sharks that are fast swimmers.</definition> The little fish swam faster. He went under the rock. The lamnidae swam away. The little fish was safe by the rock. He was happy to be small. Small fish can hide! He stayed under the rock all day.

<question>Where did the little fish swim?</question>
<answer>He swam by a big rock.</answer>

<answer>It looked like a shark.</answer>

<question>What is a shark?</question>
<answer>A shark is a type of fish that lives in the ocean and has sharp teeth.</answer>

<question>What kind of shark was it?</question>
<answer>It was a lamnidae.</answer>

<answer>Lamnidae is a family of sharks that are fast swimmers.</answer>

<answer>He swam faster.</answer>

<question>Where did the little fish go?</question>
<answer>He went under the rock.</answer>

<answer>The shark swam away.</answer>

<answer>He was happy to be small.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=846 id=story_qa_043490 =====
The little bear loved to play in the forest. He liked all the games. He liked hide and seek. He liked tag. But he loved making mud pies the best. <definition>Making means to create something.</definition>

One day, he was making a big mud pie. He put lots of berries on top. He put in some shiny rocks too. It was a very special mud pie. He liked making mud pies, especially when he could share them. <definition>Especially means more than others.</definition>

His friend, the fox, came by. "Want some mud pie?" asked the bear. The fox smiled. "Yes, please!" The bear and the fox ate the mud pie. It was the best mud pie ever.

<question>Where did the little bear love to play?</question>
<answer>The little bear loved to play in the forest.</answer>

Grandma wrote about her cat. The cat was named Patches. Patches liked to chase yarn. Lily giggled. She loved cats too.

Lily wanted to write back. She got a crayon and paper. She thought for one minute. <definition>A minute is a short amount of time, like sixty seconds.</definition> What should she say?

She wrote, "Dear Grandma, I love Patches! I wish I could see her. Love, Lily."

Lily put the letter in an envelope. She asked her mom to mail it. She knew Grandma would be happy. It made Lily happy too. Sending letters is fun!
