One day, the cat jumped on the table. He ate too much tuna. He ate excessively. <definition>Excessively means having too much of something.</definition> His tummy hurt.

He made a loud noise. It was very offensive. <definition>Offensive means something that is rude and makes people upset.</definition> The dog did not like the noise. He ran away.

The cat felt bad. He did not want to be offensive. He jumped down. He went to sleep by the warm fire. He would not eat too much tuna again. He learned a good lesson about being polite. He wished he had not jumped so high. He was happy to be in his warm house.

Once, there was a little bunny. He lived near a big field. He usually hopped in the field. <definition>Usually means most of the time.</definition> He liked to eat clover. One day, he saw two butterflies. They were flying between the flowers. <definition>Between means in the space that separates two things.</definition> The bunny wanted to fly too! He jumped up. He flapped his ears. He did not fly. He giggled. Hopping was more fun. He hopped all the way home. He ate one carrot. It was a good day.

<question>Who lived near a big field?</question>

===== DOC START split=test idx=864 id=story_qa_044098 =====
Timmy the toad was feeling ill. <definition>Ill means not feeling well or being sick.</definition> He did not want to hop. He did not want to eat flies. Timmy looked at his fleshy toes. <definition>Fleshy means having lots of soft flesh.</definition> "I feel bad," he said. He said it openly. <definition>Openly means saying what you think or feel in a direct and honest way.</definition>

His mom said, "It is okay to feel bad." She had a helpful trait. <definition>A trait is something that makes someone special or different.</definition> She always knew what to do! She told Timmy, "Rest and you will feel better."

Timmy did rest. Soon, he felt better. He could utter a happy sound. <definition>Utter means to say something.</definition> "I feel great!" he croaked.

<question>How was Timmy the toad feeling?</question>
<answer>Timmy was feeling ill.</answer>

<answer>Ill means not feeling well or being sick.</answer>

The little bear wanted a rock. He wanted a shiny rock. He heard rocks could be mined. <definition>Mined means dug out of the ground.</definition> So he took his shovel. He went to the hill.

He started to dig. He dug and dug. It was hard work. He found a worm. The worm wiggled in the dirt. The bear giggled. He put the worm back.

He dug some more. He found a root. He pulled the root out. He kept digging in the dirt. Finally, he saw something shiny. It was a rock! It was small and round. It sparkled in the sun. It was just what he wanted. The bear smiled. "My very own mined rock!" he said. He put the rock in his pocket. He went home to show his mom.

One day, a big storm came. The wind blew hard. Rain fell down, down, down. The little mouse ran inside. He felt safe in his little blue house.

After the storm, the sun came out. The field was very wet. But the flowers looked so pretty! The mouse liked the red flowers especially. <definition>Especially means more than others.</definition> He picked one and put it in his house. Now his house was even better. He was happy to be the inhabitant of the little blue house in the flower field. He smiled.

The little bird lived by a big tree. It was a very old tree. It was an ancient tree. <definition>Ancient means something very, very old.</definition> The bird was an avian creature. <definition>Avian means relating to birds.</definition> It had wings and feathers. The avian bird liked to sing.

One day, a cat came by. The cat wanted to catch the bird. The bird saw the cat. It flew up high in the ancient tree. The cat jumped. It could not reach the bird.

The bird sang a song. It sang a song about the cat. The cat did not like the song. The cat went away. The bird was happy. It was safe by the ancient tree.

===== DOC START split=test idx=890 id=story_qa_045204 =====
The crayons lived in a box. They were all different colors. One day, the red crayon was sad. He felt he was not used enough. The blue crayon saw him. "What is wrong?" asked Blue.

"I want to achieve something big!" said Red. <definition>Achieve means to do something you worked hard for.</definition> "But I only color small things."

Blue smiled. "We can all achieve something big if we work together!"

Blue called all the crayons. "Let's make one big picture!" he said. The crayons all agreed. They worked hard. Red colored a big, round sun. Yellow colored happy stars. Green colored tall trees.

When they were done, the crayons were united. <definition>United means to be joined together.</definition> They had made a beautiful picture. They all felt proud. They had all used their colors to make something special. The red crayon was happy now. He helped make the sun shine bright!

<question>Where did the crayons live?</question>
<answer>The crayons lived in a box.</answer>

<answer>The sad crayon was red.</answer>

<answer>Blue asked Red what was wrong.</answer>

<answer>Achieve means to do something you worked hard for.</answer>

<answer>Blue wanted them to make one big picture.</answer>

<answer>Red colored a big, round sun.</answer>

<answer>Yellow colored happy stars.</answer>

The bear was soft. The bear was fluffy. It was a good quality bear. The pig was very happy. He took the bear home. He played with it all day long. He loved his large, new bear. He gave the bear a hug. The pig was not sad anymore.

===== DOC START split=test idx=902 id=story_qa_045874 =====
Lily saw an angel. The angel was made of clouds. It was sitting on a star. The star was very large. The angel smiled at Lily. "Hello!" said the angel. "I am here to help you."

"How can you help?" asked Lily.

"I have a special function," said the angel. <definition>Function means what something is used for or what it does.</definition> "I can make wishes come true."

Lily thought hard. "Can I use your star as a slide?" she asked.

The angel laughed. "Yes, you can use it! That is a fun wish."

Lily climbed on the large star. Whee! She slid down. It was the best slide ever. The angel clapped. "I like to use my function for fun things!"

<question>Who did Lily see?</question>
<answer>Lily saw an angel.</answer>

<question>What was the angel made of?</question>
<answer>The angel was made of clouds.</answer>

<answer>The angel was sitting on a star.</answer>

<answer>The angel said hello.</answer>

<answer>The angel said it could make wishes come true.</answer>

<answer>Function means what something is used for.</answer>

<answer>Lily wanted to use the star as a slide.</answer>

<question>Did the angel let Lily use the star?</question>
<answer>Yes, the angel let Lily use the star.</answer>

<question>Did Lily like the slide?</question>
<answer>Yes, Lily liked the slide.</answer>

The little bear cub was happy. He loved being outside. He found a big puddle. He dipped his paw in the water. It was cold!

A wise abbess lived nearby. <definition>An abbess is a woman who is the head of a group of nuns.</definition> She saw the bear. She typically wore a long brown dress. <definition>Typically means usually.</definition>

She walked over to the bear. She smiled. "Hello, little one," she said. "What are you doing?"

The bear looked up. He put his wet paw on her dress. "Oops!" he said.

The abbess laughed. "That is okay," she said. "The puddle is fun, right?"

The bear nodded. He dipped his paw in again. Splash! He was having so much fun being a bear. The abbess smiled. She liked watching him play on that sunny day.

===== DOC START split=test idx=913 id=story_qa_046359 =====
Lily loved to paint. She used all the colors. One day, she painted a monster. The monster had big teeth. It had one eye. It looked very unfriendly. <definition>Unfriendly means not nice or kind.</definition>

Lily showed her friend, Tom, the painting. Tom pointed his finger at the monster. <definition>Your finger is one of the five parts at the end of your hand.</definition> "That monster looks mad," Tom said.

Lily giggled. "He is not real," she said. "I made him up."

Just then, the wind blew. The painting flew away! It landed in Mrs. Davis's garden. Mrs. Davis did not like monsters.

Lily and Tom ran to get the painting. They saw Mrs. Davis looking at it. She smiled. "He is funny!" she said. "I like him!"

Lily and Tom were happy. Even the unfriendly monster made someone smile. Lily took the painting home. She hung it on her wall.

<question>What did Lily love to do?</question>
<answer>Lily loved to paint.</answer>

<question>What did Lily paint one day?</question>
<answer>She painted a monster.</answer>

<question>How many eyes did the monster have?</question>
<answer>It had one eye.</answer>

<question>What does unfriendly mean?</question>
<answer>Unfriendly means not nice or kind.</answer>

<question>Who was Lily's friend?</question>
<answer>Lily's friend was Tom.</answer>

<question>What did Tom say about the monster?</question>
<answer>Tom said the monster looked mad.</answer>

<question>What does finger mean?</question>
<answer>Your finger is one of the five parts at the end of your hand.</answer>

<question>What happened to the painting?</question>
<answer>The wind blew the painting away.</answer>

<question>Where did the painting land?</question>
<answer>It landed in Mrs. Davis's garden.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=919 id=story_qa_046506 =====
The sun was up. A little bird woke up too. She wanted to play! She looked around. What could she do?

The bird saw some clouds. The clouds were shaped like funny things. One cloud was shaped like a dog. Another was shaped like a fish. The bird decided to play a game.

She would fly actively around the clouds. <definition>Actively means doing something with lots of energy.</definition> She would try to find new shapes. She flew up to the dog cloud. She looked closely. Now it looked like a cat!

She flew to the fish cloud. She looked at it from the side. Now it looked like a hat! The bird was happy. It was fun to find new shapes in the clouds. She flew higher and higher, looking for more clouds. Playing made her very happy.

<question>What was up in the sky?</question>
<answer>The sun was up.</answer>

<question>Who woke up?</question>
<answer>A little bird woke up.</answer>

<answer>The bird wanted to play.</answer>

<answer>The bird saw some clouds.</answer>

<answer>One cloud was shaped like a dog.</answer>

<answer>Another cloud was shaped like a fish.</answer>

<question>What game did the bird play?</question>
<answer>She tried to find new shapes in the clouds.</answer>

<question>What does actively mean?</question>
<answer>Actively means doing something with lots of energy.</answer>

<question>What did the dog cloud look like after the bird flew around it?</question>
<answer>It looked like a cat.</answer>

<question>What did the fish cloud look like from the side?</question>
<answer>It looked like a hat.</answer>

<question>How did the bird feel?</question>
<answer>The bird was happy.</answer>

<question>What did the bird do at the end of the story?</question>
<answer>She flew higher looking for more clouds.</answer>

Tim watched them dance. He tried to dance as they did. It was hard! He tripped on his feet. The pig laughed.

"It's okay," she said. "Keep trying!"

Tim tried again and again. Soon, Tim could do some of the steps. He felt happy. He made new friends and learned a new dance!

The chick liked to play in the turned dirt. He found yummy bugs there. The tractor helped the farmer. The chick liked the tractor too! It made finding bugs easy. He was a happy, yellow chick.

A duck saw the pig. She wanted to play too. She jumped in the puddle by the pig. The puddle was now very muddy. The pig and the duck laughed. They had so much fun! They made the large puddle even muddier. The farmer saw them. He laughed too. "Silly pig and duck!" he said. "Time for a bath!"

<question>Where did the pig live?</question>
<answer>The pig lived by a farm.</answer>

<question>What did the pig like to do?</question>
<answer>He liked to play in the mud.</answer>

<question>What did the pig find one day?</question>
<answer>He found a large puddle.</answer>

<answer>Large means something is very big.</answer>

<question>What did the pig do when he found the puddle?</question>
<answer>He jumped in with a splash.</answer>

<question>Who saw the pig in the puddle?</question>
<answer>A duck saw the pig.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=931 id=story_qa_046776 =====
The little bear lived far to the north. <definition>North is the direction towards the top of a map.</definition> It was very cold there. He wore a hat on his head. He wore mittens on his paws. He still felt cold! One day, he saw a bird. The bird was small and yellow. "Hello!" said the bear. The bird chirped a song.

The bear liked the song very much. He asked, "Where are you going?" The bird said, "I am flying south. It is warmer there!" The bear looked at his mittens. He looked at his hat on his head. He said, "I want to go south too!" The bird smiled. "Then come on!"

The bear and the bird walked south. It got warmer and warmer. Soon, the bear took off his hat. Then he took off his mittens. He was very happy to be warm.

<question>Where did the little bear live?</question>
<answer>The little bear lived far to the north.</answer>

One day, the bear wanted a snack. He looked in his house. But there was no food. “Oh no!” said the bear. “I need food!”

The bear's dad works at the bakery. <definition>Works means has a job.</definition> He makes yummy bread. The bear went to see his dad.

"Dad, I am hungry!" said the bear.

Dad smiled. He gave the bear a small loaf of bread. It was warm and smelled good.

"Thank you, Dad!" said the bear. He ran back to his tree. He ate the bread with his friends. It was the best bread ever! The bear was happy to be living in his tree.

===== DOC START split=test idx=939 id=story_qa_047328 =====

A little blue bird watched from a tree. He chirped, "Go straight! Go fast!"

But the road would not let him. It was a silly road.

The car drove past a big green field. This was Farmer Joe's property. <definition>Property is something that someone owns, like land or a house.</definition> Farmer Joe waved. The car beeped hello.

The road went straight for a little bit. The car went zoom! Then the road turned again. The bird laughed. "Silly car! Silly road!"

The car did not mind. It liked going slow. It liked seeing the bird. It liked seeing Farmer Joe's property. It was a happy little car.

<answer>The car was red.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=940 id=story_qa_047156 =====
The sun was hot. Lily and Tom wanted to play outside. Mom said, "Wear sunscreen!"

Lily did not want to. She said, "I will not burn!" She said it falsely. <definition>Falsely means not telling the truth.</definition> Lily thought she was strong.

Tom put on sunscreen. He knew the sun was strong. He did not want a sunburn.

Lily and Tom played all day. They played tag. They built a fort. They had lots of sun exposure. <definition>Exposure means being out in the sun or weather.</definition>

That night, Lily felt hot. Her skin was red. "Ouch!" she cried. She had a sunburn.

Tom felt fine. His skin was soft. "Sunscreen works!" he said.

Lily learned a lesson. She should listen to her mom. And she should wear sunscreen! Now she knows the sun can hurt you.

===== DOC START split=test idx=946 id=story_qa_047609 =====
Lily had a small plant. It was in a blue pot. She loved her plant. She gave it water. She gave it sun.

Lily's plant was a sunflower. Usually, sunflowers grow very tall. <definition>Usually means most of the time or normally.</definition> But Lily's sunflower was small. It only had one leaf.

"Why aren't you growing?" Lily asked. She looked closely at the dirt. Maybe it needed more water. She gave it a little bit more.

One day, Lily saw something. It was a tiny, green sprout. It was next to her small sunflower. Another plant was growing!

Lily smiled. "Maybe my sunflower is helping the new plant grow," she said. She knew that plants help each other. She was happy her little sunflower had a friend in the pot. She would take care of both plants.

<answer>Lily had a small plant.</answer>

<answer>It was a sunflower.</answer>

<answer>It only had one leaf.</answer>

<answer>Usually means most of the time or normally.</answer>

<answer>She asked why it wasn't growing.</answer>

<answer>She saw a tiny, green sprout.</answer>

<question>Where was the sprout?</question>
<answer>It was next to her small sunflower.</answer>

<answer>She thought it was helping the new plant grow.</answer>

<question>How did Lily feel about the new plant?</question>

===== DOC START split=test idx=947 id=story_qa_047742 =====
The sun was up. Lily the ladybug woke up. She loved to do her morning exercises. She called them her calisthenic moves. <definition>Calisthenic moves are exercises you do to get strong.</definition> First, she stretched her legs. Then, she wiggled her antennae. Next, she flew around her favorite flower.

The flower was a pretty red color. It grew back every year. It was a perennial flower. <definition>Perennial means it lives for many years.</definition> Lily loved the perennial flower. It was her home. She did a calisthenic jump onto a petal.

“Good morning, flower!” she said. The flower did not talk back. But Lily knew it was happy to see her. She did more calisthenic moves on the flower. She felt strong and happy. It was going to be a good day.

<question>What time of day was it?</question>
<answer>It was morning.</answer>

<question>Who woke up?</question>
<answer>Lily the ladybug woke up.</answer>

<question>What did Lily love to do?</question>
<answer>Lily loved to do her morning exercises.</answer>

<question>What did she call her exercises?</question>
<answer>She called them her calisthenic moves.</answer>

<question>What are calisthenic moves?</question>
<answer>Calisthenic moves are exercises you do to get strong.</answer>

<question>What did Lily do first?</question>
<answer>First, she stretched her legs.</answer>

<question>What did she do after stretching?</question>
<answer>She wiggled her antennae.</answer>

<question>What did Lily do next?</question>
<answer>She flew around her favorite flower.</answer>

<question>What color was the flower?</question>
<answer>The flower was a pretty red color.</answer>

<question>Did the flower grow back every year?</question>
<answer>Yes, the flower grew back every year.</answer>

<question>What kind of flower was it?</question>
<answer>It was a perennial flower.</answer>

<question>What does perennial mean?</question>
<answer>Perennial means it lives for many years.</answer>

<question>What did Lily do on the petal?</question>
<answer>She did a calisthenic jump onto a petal.</answer>

<question>Did the flower talk back to Lily?</question>
<answer>No, the flower did not talk back.</answer>

<question>How did Lily feel?</question>
<answer>She felt strong and happy.</answer>

Someone was coming! It was a dragon. The dragon was big and green. Timmy was not scared. He ran to the kitchen. He found a metal lid. He used it as his shield.

The dragon roared. Timmy held up his shield. It worked! The fire bounced off. The dragon ran away. Timmy was so happy! He saved the day. He was as brave as could be.

===== DOC START split=test idx=950 id=story_qa_047854 =====
The little bird wanted to talk. <definition>Talk means to say words.</definition> He wanted to talk to his mom. She was building a nest by the big tree.

“Chirp, chirp!” he said. Momma bird did not talk back. She was busy. She put a twig in the nest.

The little bird flew away. He flew to the southeastern part of the yard. <definition>Southeastern means the part that is to the south and the east.</definition> He saw a big bug. The bug was eating a leaf.

The bird noted the bug. <definition>Noted means saw or noticed.</definition> He wanted to eat the bug! He hopped closer. The bug did not see him. The bird ate the bug. It tasted yummy!

He flew back to the nest by the tree. Momma bird smiled. “Chirp!” she said. The little bird chirped back. He was happy to talk to his mom.

<question>What did the little bird want to do?</question>
<answer>He wanted to talk.</answer>

<answer>Talk means to say words.</answer>

<answer>He wanted to talk to his mom.</answer>

<answer>No, she did not talk back.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=962 id=story_qa_048569 =====
The cat was named Pat. Pat liked to play. Some days, Pat would chase bugs. He chased them all day long. Pat was fast as a race car.

Pat knew a little latin. <definition>Latin is a very old language that some people used to speak.</definition> He learned it from his owner. His owner read latin books. Pat liked to listen. He only knew a small part of the words.

One day, Pat saw a big bug. It was bigger than all the other bugs. He chased it to a fence. He jumped over the fence. The bug flew away. Pat looked around. He did not know where he was.

To some extent, Pat was lost. He meowed. He meowed again. He meowed very loud. His owner heard him. She came to get him. Pat was happy to be home.

<answer>The cat's name was Pat.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=963 id=story_qa_048695 =====
The little blue car usually went to the park. It used to drive the same way every day. But today, the car wanted to change. <definition>Change means to become different.</definition> It turned left instead of right.

"Wow!" said the car. It saw a big red barn. Cows were eating green grass. The car had never seen cows before. Usually, it only saw trees.

Then, it saw a fluffy white sheep. The sheep used its teeth to munch the grass. The car liked this new way. It would change its drive again tomorrow!

<question>Where did the little blue car usually go?</question>
<answer>The little blue car usually went to the park.</answer>

<question>What did the car want to do today?</question>
<answer>The car wanted to change.</answer>

<question>What does change mean?</question>
<answer>Change means to become different.</answer>

<question>Which way did the car turn today?</question>
<answer>The car turned left.</answer>

<question>What did the car see first?</question>
<answer>The car saw a big red barn.</answer>

<question>What were the cows doing?</question>
<answer>The cows were eating green grass.</answer>

<question>Had the car seen cows before?</question>
<answer>No, the car had never seen cows before.</answer>

<question>What did the car usually see?</question>
<answer>The car usually saw trees.</answer>

<question>What other animal did the car see?</question>
<answer>The car saw a fluffy white sheep.</answer>

<question>What was the sheep doing?</question>
<answer>The sheep was munching grass.</answer>

<question>Did the car like the new way?</question>
<answer>Yes, the car liked the new way.</answer>

The big pig said, "No more mud!"

Pip was sad. He liked mud. But the big pig was in charge. Being clean was the big pig's main characteristic. <definition>Characteristic means what someone or something is like.</definition> He liked everything clean.

Pip missed the mud. He saw other pigs were sad too. So Pip talked to the big pig. He said, "Mud is fun! Can we have a little mud?"

The big pig thought. He said, "Okay, but just a little!"

Pip and the other pigs cheered. They played in a little mud. The big pig smiled. Maybe mud was a little fun!

Lily pretended to be in danger. "Help, Super Cat!" she shouted. Patches looked at her. Then, to Lily's surprise, Patches jumped into action. He chased a fly buzzing by! Lily laughed. Being a superhero was fun. Being a super cat was even better. It was fun to watch Patches as he played.

"Wow!" said the bear. He wanted to make something. He found some more material. This piece was red. He found yellow and green pieces too.

He tried to make them equal. <definition>Equal means the same.</definition> He wanted all the pieces to be the same size. It was hard! He asked his mom for help.

Mom smiled. She cut the material into squares. Now they were all equal. The bear used the squares to make a blanket. It was a very colorful blanket! He used it every night by the lake. He was so happy.

"Oh no!" said the bear. "What will I do?"

He looked in the house. He saw a big red boot. "Perfect!" he said. He put dirt in the boot. Then, he put the seed in the dirt. He gave it some water. He waited.

After a few days, something popped up. It was a little green plant! The bear was so happy.

He took very good care of the plant. He watered it in a gentle manner. <definition>Manner means the way that you do something.</definition> He sang to it every day. The plant grew big and strong. The bear loved his plant very much.

They flew a long distance. <definition>Distance is how far away something is.</definition> They flew far away from the grasshopper. They found a new flower. It was red and smelled sweet. The little bird was happy again. He sat by the red flower with his mom. He forgot all about the large, green grasshopper.

===== DOC START split=test idx=999 id=story_qa_050031 =====

The bug did not say hello back. That was okay. Bugs do not talk. Timmy asked, "Are you being good?" <definition>Being means how someone is acting.</definition> The bug just sat still.

Timmy touched the leaf. The bug jumped! It flew away. That was its response. <definition>Response means what someone does after something happens.</definition> Timmy laughed. The bug's response was funny. He liked watching the bug in the light. The park was fun.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1001 id=story_qa_050088 =====
The cat sat on a mat. It was a red mat. The cat's name was Pat. Pat liked to nap on the mat.

One day, Pat saw someone new. It was a little bird. The bird was brown and small. It was a native bird. <definition>Native means that the bird was born in that place.</definition> It hopped close to Pat.

Pat looked at the bird. The bird looked at Pat. Pat was big, as cats usually are. The bird was little. "Chirp, chirp," said the bird. Pat blinked. The bird hopped on Pat's head!

Pat did not like that. Pat jumped up. The bird flew away. Pat went back to his mat. He went back to napping on his red mat. He hoped the bird would not come back. Napping was better than birds!

<question>Where did the cat sit?</question>
<answer>The cat sat on a mat.</answer>

<question>What color was the mat?</question>
<answer>The mat was red.</answer>

<question>What was the cat's name?</question>
<answer>The cat's name was Pat.</answer>

<question>What did Pat like to do on the mat?</question>
<answer>Pat liked to nap on the mat.</answer>

<question>Who did Pat see one day?</question>
<answer>Pat saw a little bird.</answer>

<question>What color was the bird?</question>
<answer>The bird was brown.</answer>

<question>What does native mean?</question>
<answer>Native means that the bird was born in that place.</answer>

<question>What did the bird say?</question>
<answer>The bird said chirp chirp.</answer>

<question>Where did the bird hop?</question>
<answer>The bird hopped on Pat's head.</answer>

<question>What did Pat do when the bird hopped on his head?</question>
<answer>Pat jumped up.</answer>

<question>What did the bird do after Pat jumped?</question>
<answer>The bird flew away.</answer>

<question>What did Pat do after the bird flew away?</question>
<answer>Pat went back to his mat.</answer>

<question>What did Pat go back to doing?</question>
<answer>Pat went back to napping.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1004 id=story_qa_050206 =====

But then the boat hit a rock. It stopped moving. Tim started dragging a stick in the water. He wanted to push the boat. But the stick was too short.

The boat was stuck. It slowly sunk. <definition>Sunk means to go under the water.</definition> Only a small part of the boat was showing. Tim felt sad.

He tried to move the rock. He pulled and pulled. He could not move it. Tim changed his position. <definition>Position means the way something is sitting or standing.</definition> He stood on his tiptoes. He reached as far as he could. He still could not reach the boat.

Tim’s mom came over. “What’s wrong?” she asked. Tim told her about his boat. His mom smiled. “We can get it later,” she said. "Let's get ice cream!" Tim smiled too.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1017 id=story_qa_050646 =====
The cat, Mittens, thought she was so great. She was contemptuous of the little dog. <definition>Contemptuous means thinking you are better than everyone else.</definition> She thought dogs were silly. Mittens saw her chance. She saw an advantage. <definition>Advantage means a good thing that helps you.</definition> The dog's toy was close to her.

Mittens wanted the toy. She thought, "I am the only one who should have toys!" She crept closer. But the dog saw her! The dog wagged his tail. He wanted to play. Mittens learned something. It is a virtue to share. <definition>Virtue means being good and kind.</definition> She learned that being nice is better than being mean. Mittens was happy about her new friend.

<question>What was the cat's name?</question>
<answer>The cat's name was Mittens.</answer>

<question>What did Mittens think of herself?</question>
<answer>Mittens thought she was great.</answer>

<question>What does contemptuous mean?</question>
<answer>Contemptuous means thinking you are better than everyone else.</answer>

<question>What did Mittens think of dogs?</question>
<answer>She thought dogs were silly.</answer>

<question>What did Mittens see?</question>
<answer>Mittens saw the dog's toy.</answer>

<question>What does advantage mean?</question>
<answer>Advantage means a good thing that helps you.</answer>

<question>What did Mittens want?</question>
<answer>Mittens wanted the toy.</answer>

<question>What did the dog do when he saw Mittens?</question>
<answer>The dog wagged his tail.</answer>

<question>What did the dog want to do?</question>
<answer>He wanted to play.</answer>

<question>What is a virtue?</question>
<answer>Virtue means being good and kind.</answer>

<question>What did Mittens learn?</question>
<answer>She learned it is a virtue to share.</answer>

The soup was in a bowl. The cat looked at it. He did not understand. It was not fish. Why would he eat soup? His friend, the dog, loved soup. The dog said it was yummy.

The cat used his logic. <definition>Logic means thinking about something in a way that makes sense.</definition> If the dog liked it, maybe he would too. He took a lick. The soup was warm. It tasted good! The cat ate all the soup. Now he liked soup and fish. He was a happy black cat.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1035 id=story_qa_051633 =====
The firefly was a female. <definition>Female means a girl or a woman.</definition> She loved to fly at night. The moon gave off a soft light. She blinked her own light, too. It was a tiny spark.

One night, the firefly felt lonely. She wanted friends. She flew around the yard. She saw other fireflies blinking. She blinked back at them.

More and more fireflies came. They all blinked together. Their lights made the yard glow. It was a beautiful sight.

The fireflies felt united. <definition>United means to be joined together as one.</definition> They flew together in a big group. They made shapes in the air. It looked like a dancing light show. The female firefly was happy. She had found her friends. Now, every night, they all light up the sky.

<question>Was the firefly a boy or a girl?</question>
<answer>The firefly was a girl.</answer>

<question>What does female mean?</question>
<answer>Female means a girl or a woman.</answer>

<question>When did the firefly like to fly?</question>
<answer>The firefly liked to fly at night.</answer>

<question>What kind of light did the moon make?</question>
<answer>The moon made a soft light.</answer>

<question>What did the firefly do with her light?</question>
<answer>She blinked her light.</answer>

<question>How did the firefly feel one night?</question>
<answer>The firefly felt lonely.</answer>

<question>What did the firefly want?</question>
<answer>She wanted friends.</answer>

<question>Where did she fly to find friends?</question>
<answer>She flew around the yard.</answer>

<question>What were the other fireflies doing?</question>
<answer>They were blinking.</answer>

<question>What happened when the fireflies blinked together?</question>
<answer>Their lights made the yard glow.</answer>

<question>What does united mean?</question>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1036 id=story_qa_051660 =====
The little bear was sad. He was lacking honey. <definition>Lacking means missing or not having something.</definition> He looked in his honey pot. It was empty! This made him sad. He sat down on a rock. He had a feeling that today was not a good day. <definition>Feeling means an emotion, like happy or sad.</definition>

A bee flew by. "Why are you sad?" she asked.

"I have no honey," said the bear. "This will cause me to be hungry." <definition>Cause means to make something happen.</definition>

The bee buzzed. "I can help! Follow me!"

The bee flew to a field of flowers. "See all these flowers? They have lots of nectar. Nectar makes honey, as you know!"

The bear smiled. He wasn't sad anymore. He was happy. He would get honey soon! He thanked the bee and started to gather flowers. He had a happy feeling now.

<question>Why was the little bear sad?</question>
<answer>He was lacking honey.</answer>

<answer>Lacking means missing or not having something.</answer>

The little pig lived by a farm. He liked to play in the mud. One day, a businessman came to the farm. <definition>A businessman is a man who works to make money.</definition> He wore a very nice suit. The pig saw a weird thing on the businessman's desk. It was paper! The paper was perforated. <definition>Perforated means it has tiny holes in it, so it can be torn easily.</definition>

"What is that?" asked the pig.

"It's paper!" said the businessman. "I use it to write."

The pig looked at his skin. He thought about his sweat gland. <definition>A gland is a part of your body that makes liquids.</definition> "I don't need paper," he said. "I can write with mud!"

The businessman laughed. He liked the pig.

The toys were sad. They sat in a box. They had been there for a long time. "I miss playing!" said a teddy bear. He sighed plaintively. <definition>Plaintively means sounding sad.</definition> A toy car said, "Me too!"

"We need a plan," said a toy soldier. "Before we get stuck here forever." He looked around the box.

The toys all talked at once. It was loud! Then, the toy soldier shouted, "Be quiet! We need to be united!" <definition>United means to come together.</definition>

The toys listened. The soldier said, "We will climb out, one by one." A small train went first. He chugged to the top. Then he helped the others.

It was hard work. But finally, all the toys were out! They were free!

"It was worth it!" said the teddy bear. <definition>Worth means how important or useful something is.</definition>

They all smiled. They were happy to be out of the box. Now they could play again. They would never go back in the box. They were united as friends.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1040 id=story_qa_051855 =====
The little pig, Pip, loved mud. He loved to roll in it. He loved to splash in it. He loved mud, especially after a warm day. <definition>Especially means more than usual.</definition>

One day, Pip's mom said, "Pip, please be clean!" <definition>Be means to exist or to happen.</definition> Pip did not want to be clean. He wanted to play. He ran outside to the mud.

His mom sighed. She knew Pip loved mud more than anything. She decided to make a game.

"Pip," she called. "If you can find the shiniest mud, you can play in it all day!"

Pip's eyes opened wide. Shiny mud? He had to find it! He looked and looked. He found mud with sticks. He found mud with leaves. Then, he found it. The shiniest mud was near the big oak tree! It sparkled in the sun.

Pip jumped in. He was so happy to be playing in the mud!

Timmy the turtle loved to garden. He grew big red tomatoes. But something was stopping his plants from growing. It was an inhibitor! <definition>An inhibitor is something that slows down or stops something else.</definition> The inhibitor was a big rock.

One day, a big storm came. The sky was dark and threatening. <definition>Threatening means something that looks like it will cause harm.</definition> Timmy ran inside his shell. He was scared! The wind blew hard.

After the storm, Timmy went outside. He saw something amazing! The big rock, the inhibitor, was gone. It had rolled away! Timmy's tomato plants were now standing upright. <definition>Upright means standing straight up.</definition>

Timmy was so happy. He started singing. He was showing all his friends his garden. He gave them all big, red tomatoes. They were the best tomatoes ever!

===== DOC START split=test idx=1049 id=story_qa_052195 =====
The frog lived by a pond. He loved the water. He swam all day. He liked to splash. He liked to float. He liked to sing.

The frog sang a song. His song was very rhythmic. <definition>Rhythmic means having a steady beat, like music.</definition> He sang, "Plip, plop, plip, plop." The water made sounds too. It went, "Swish, swoosh, swish, swoosh."

A little turtle heard the song. He came to the water. He saw the frog. The frog smiled. "Want to sing with me?" asked the frog. The turtle said, "Yes!"

The frog and turtle sang together. Their songs made everyone happy. Even the fish danced in the water! It was a fun day at the pond.

<question>Where did the frog live?</question>
<answer>The frog lived by a pond.</answer>

<question>What did the frog love?</question>
<answer>The frog loved the water.</answer>

<question>What did the frog do all day?</question>
<answer>He swam all day.</answer>

<question>What did the frog like to do?</question>
<answer>He liked to splash, float, and sing.</answer>

<question>What was the frog's song like?</question>
<answer>His song was very rhythmic.</answer>

<question>What does rhythmic mean?</question>
<answer>Rhythmic means having a steady beat, like music.</answer>

<question>What did the frog sing?</question>
<answer>He sang, "Plip, plop, plip, plop."</answer>

<question>What sound did the water make?</question>
<answer>It went, "Swish, swoosh, swish, swoosh."</answer>

<question>Who heard the frog's song?</question>
<answer>A little turtle heard the song.</answer>

<question>Did the turtle want to sing with the frog?</question>
<answer>Yes, the turtle wanted to sing with the frog.</answer>

<question>Who sang together?</question>
<answer>The frog and turtle sang together.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1066 id=story_qa_053235 =====

"I want to drive up the hill," said the person. But the car was old. It went slowly. Puff, puff, puff. The car could not get up the hill.

The person was sad. He got out to look at the car. He looked at every part of the car. <definition>Part means one piece of something.</definition> He saw a small rock stuck in one tire. He took the rock out.

"Let's try again!" said the person.

This time, the car went up the hill! Vroom, vroom! It was easy. The person was happy. The car was happy too. They went for a long drive in the sun.

He used all his force. <definition>Force is strength or power.</definition> He pushed and pushed. The bottle still would not pour. Timmy asked his mom for help. Mom used her force. She opened the bottle. Timmy smiled. He got more juice! He said, "Thank you, Mom!"

<question>What color was the juice?</question>
<answer>The juice was red.</answer>

<answer>A liquid is something that flows.</answer>

<answer>Timmy wanted more juice.</answer>

<answer>The bottle was stuck.</answer>

<answer>Force is strength or power.</answer>

<answer>Timmy's mom helped him.</answer>

<question>Did Timmy get more juice?</question>
<answer>Yes, Timmy got more juice.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1072 id=story_qa_053413 =====
Lily loved to play outside. One day, she saw something new in the woods. It was a little white mushroom. It was growing on a log. Lily wondered what it was.

Her mom told her it was fungi. <definition>Fungi are living things that are not plants or animals, like mushrooms.</definition> Fungi grow in lots of places. Some fungi are big. Some are small.

Lily learned about fungi at school too. She learned that some fungi are good to eat. But some fungi can make you sick. You have to be careful!

Lily liked to read books about nature. She looked for fungi in the forest. She liked to draw pictures of them in her notebook. She drew the white mushroom she saw. She drew other colorful fungi too. She learned so much about the woods. It was fun to learn!

<question>Who liked to play outside?</question>
<answer>Lily liked to play outside.</answer>

<answer>She saw a little white mushroom.</answer>

<answer>It was growing on a log.</answer>

<answer>She wondered what it was.</answer>

<answer>Her mom said it was fungi.</answer>

<answer>Fungi are living things that are not plants or animals, like mushrooms.</answer>

<answer>Fungi grow in lots of places.</answer>

His mom said, "Pip, you must eat nicely!" Pip tried. It was hard. He loved apples, especially. He liked to eat them fast. <definition>Especially means more than other things.</definition>

One day, Pip's mom made pie. It was apple pie. Pip was so excited. He ate the pie in a faithless way. <definition>Faithless means you did not do what you promised.</definition> He promised to eat nicely, but he forgot. He made a mess. His mom sighed. "Pip," she said. "Try again tomorrow." Pip smiled. Tomorrow was another day for pie!

Today, the club needed help. "We need nuts!" said the leader. "Winter is coming!"

Tim was as fast as a rocket. He ran to the big oak tree. He saw lots of nuts. He picked them up, one by one. He put them in his bag.

Other squirrels helped too. Soon, all the nuts were in a pile. It was as big as Tim!

"Great job!" said the leader. "You are a great member, Tim!" Tim smiled. He liked being a Squirrel Club member. He liked helping his friends. He liked nuts too!

===== DOC START split=test idx=1085 id=story_qa_053900 =====
Timmy liked to play outside. He wanted to be strong. He knew he needed to exercise. <definition>Exercise means to move your body to make it stronger.</definition> He did jumping jacks. He ran around the yard. He lifted small rocks.

Today, Timmy learned about the romans. <definition>The romans were people who lived a long, long time ago.</definition> They were very strong. Timmy wanted to be like them.

Beforehand, Timmy planned his day. <definition>Beforehand means to plan before you do something.</definition> First, he would exercise. Then, he would read about romans. He ate a banana for energy. He put on his shoes. He ran outside. He jumped in the air. He felt strong and ready to learn! He knew learning about the romans would be fun. He was happy.

<question>What did Timmy like to do?</question>
<answer>Timmy liked to play outside.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy want to be?</question>
<answer>He wanted to be strong.</answer>

<question>What does exercise mean?</question>
<answer>Exercise means to move your body to make it stronger.</answer>

<question>What exercises did Timmy do?</question>
<answer>He did jumping jacks, ran, and lifted rocks.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy learn about today?</question>
<answer>Timmy learned about the romans.</answer>

<question>Who were the romans?</question>
<answer>The romans were people who lived a long, long time ago.</answer>

<question>What does beforehand mean?</question>
<answer>Beforehand means to plan before you do something.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy plan to do first?</question>
<answer>He planned to exercise first.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy eat for energy?</question>
<answer>He ate a banana.</answer>

<question>How did Timmy feel at the end?</question>
<answer>He felt strong and happy.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1086 id=story_qa_053937 =====
The little blue bird was sad. Usually, she loved to sing. But not today. She sat in her nest. The nest was in a tall tree. She looked down at the ground.

"Why are you sad?" asked a squirrel.

"I can't sing," said the bird. "My voice is gone!"

The squirrel thought hard. "Maybe you need juice!" he said. He ran to find a berry. He brought it back to the bird. She ate the berry.

"Try to sing now," said the squirrel.

The bird took a deep breath. She opened her beak. A little sound came out. Then a bigger sound! Soon, she was singing a happy song. The squirrel danced. The bird was happy again. She loved to sing in the tree.

<question>What color was the bird?</question>
<answer>The bird was blue.</answer>

<question>How did the bird feel?</question>
<answer>The bird felt sad.</answer>

<question>Where was the bird's nest?</question>
<answer>The nest was in a tall tree.</answer>

<question>Who asked the bird why she was sad?</question>
<answer>A squirrel asked the bird.</answer>

<question>What was wrong with the bird?</question>
<answer>The bird could not sing.</answer>

<question>What did the squirrel think the bird needed?</question>
<answer>The squirrel thought the bird needed juice.</answer>

<question>What did the squirrel bring to the bird?</question>
<answer>The squirrel brought a berry.</answer>

<question>What happened after the bird ate the berry?</question>
<answer>The bird could sing again.</answer>

<question>What did the squirrel do when the bird sang?</question>
<answer>The squirrel danced.</answer>

<question>How did the bird feel at the end?</question>
<answer>The bird was happy again.</answer>
===== DOC END =====

===== DOC START split=test idx=1087 id=story_qa_053998 =====
The cat was in a tree. He did not want to come down. "I will act like I am stuck!" he thought. He made a sad face. "Meow! Help!"

A little girl saw the cat. She felt bad. She wanted to help the cat. She knew cats climb trees. She knew the cat was not stuck. It was wrong to trick people. It was an immoral thing to do. <definition>Immoral means something that is wrong or not fair.</definition>

She said, "Come down, kitty! I have a treat!" The cat jumped down. He ate the treat. The girl said, "It is not nice to trick people."

The cat felt bad. He did not want to act that way again. He went inside the house. He took a nap in his bed.

<question>Where was the cat?</question>
<answer>The cat was in a tree.</answer>

<question>Did the cat want to come down?</question>
<answer>No, the cat did not want to come down.</answer>

<answer>He said, "Meow! Help!"</answer>

<answer>A little girl saw the cat.</answer>

<answer>She felt bad.</answer>

<answer>No, she did not think the cat was stuck.</answer>

<answer>Immoral means something that is wrong or not fair.</answer>

<answer>Yes, the cat came down.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1096 id=story_qa_054583 =====
Timmy had a farm. He was always tending to his chickens. <definition>Tending means taking care of something.</definition> He gave them food. He gave them water. He collected their eggs. Every day was the same. It was very monotonous. <definition>Monotonous means boring because it is always the same.</definition>

Timmy liked things neat. His egg cartons were always organized. <definition>Organized means everything is in its place.</definition> He put the big eggs in one row. He put the small eggs in another row.

Timmy sold his eggs at the market. Eggs were a good commodity. <definition>A commodity is something that can be bought or sold.</definition> People needed eggs to make cakes. People needed eggs to make breakfast. Timmy liked knowing his eggs helped people. Even if his days were monotonous, he was happy.

On the way, they saw a cat. The cat was black and white. That was one characteristic of the cat. <definition>Characteristic means a special part of someone or something.</definition> Tim used the slide. He used the swings. Pat watched him.

Suddenly, a big dog came near Tim. Pat was surprised! Pat barked and stood in front of Tim. He wanted to keep Tim safe. The big dog went away. Tim hugged Pat. "Good dog!" said Tim. "You helped me get my ball." Pat wagged his tail. He was a good, protective dog.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1103 id=story_qa_055076 =====

It sounded as sweet as candy. <definition>As means like or similar to.</definition> The pig smiled. He liked the bird. He liked the song. He wished he could sing as well as the bird. But pigs cannot sing. They oink! The pig oinked at the bird. The bird flew away. The pig was sad.

<answer>The pig lived in a pen.</answer>

<answer>He liked to play and eat.</answer>

<answer>He liked to roll in the mud.</answer>

<answer>A bird flew in.</answer>

<answer>The bird was blue.</answer>

<answer>The bird sang a song.</answer>

<answer>It sounded like candy.</answer>

<question>What does as mean?</question>
<answer>As means like or similar to.</answer>

<question>Did the pig like the bird?</question>
<answer>Yes, the pig liked the bird.</answer>

<question>What did the pig wish he could do?</question>
<answer>He wished he could sing.</answer>

<question>What can pigs do?</question>
<answer>Pigs can oink.</answer>

<question>What did the pig do to the bird?</question>
<answer>The pig oinked at the bird.</answer>

<question>What happened after the pig oinked?</question>
<answer>The bird flew away.</answer>

<question>How did the pig feel when the bird flew away?</question>
<answer>The pig was sad.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1122 id=story_qa_055844 =====
Timmy had a small red car. He loved his car as much as candy! Timmy liked to drive it all over the house. He drove it on the rug. He drove it on the table.

One day, Timmy wondered to what extent he could drive the car. <definition>Extent means how far you can go or how much you can do.</definition> Could he drive it outside?

He took the car to the yard. He drove it on the grass. He drove it on the rocks. But then, the car got stuck!

Timmy tried to pull it out. He pulled and pulled. It would not move! He needed other toys to help. He got his truck and a rope. He tied the rope to the car and the truck. He pulled with the truck. Pop! The car was free. Timmy was so happy.

<question>What color was Timmy's car?</question>
<answer>Timmy's car was red.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy love as much as his car?</question>
<answer>Timmy loved candy as much as his car.</answer>

<question>Where did Timmy drive his car in the house?</question>
<answer>He drove it on the rug and the table.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy wonder about?</question>
<answer>Timmy wondered if he could drive the car outside.</answer>

<question>Where did Timmy drive the car in the yard?</question>
<answer>He drove it on the grass and the rocks.</answer>

<question>What happened to the car in the yard?</question>
<answer>The car got stuck.</answer>

<question>What does extent mean?</question>
<answer>Extent means how far you can go or how much you can do.</answer>

<question>Could Timmy pull the car out by himself?</question>
<answer>No, Timmy could not pull the car out by himself.</answer>

<question>What toys did Timmy use to help?</question>
<answer>He used his truck and a rope.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy tie the rope to?</question>
<answer>He tied the rope to the car and the truck.</answer>

<question>What sound did Timmy hear when the car came out?</question>
<answer>He heard pop.</answer>

<question>How did Timmy feel when the car was free?</question>
<answer>Timmy was happy.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1129 id=story_qa_056237 =====
The sun was hot. It was hot in the desert. <definition>A desert is a very dry place with lots of sand.</definition> Leo the lizard lived there. He was thirsty. He found a strange plant. It had long, fibrous leaves. <definition>Fibrous means something that is made of fibers, like string.</definition> Leo tried to drink from the leaves. But no water came out.

Suddenly, Leo saw something. It was a letter! It was stuck to the plant. Leo opened the letter. It said, "Dig here!" Leo started to dig in the sand. He dug and dug. He found a bottle. The bottle was full of water! Leo drank it all up. "Thank you, letter!" said Leo. Now he was not thirsty anymore. He went back to his home in the hot desert. He was happy.

<question>Where was the sun hot?</question>
<answer>The sun was hot in the desert.</answer>

<answer>Leo the lizard lived there.</answer>

Lily learned it in school. Her teacher spoke about plants in science class. Science was her favorite subject.

Lily wanted to learn another language. <definition>A language is how people talk to each other.</definition> Maybe she could learn plant language! She thought about all the amazing things she could learn. She would study hard. Plants were the best!

He hoped it would not rain. He did not like rain. He walked and walked. He saw a funny shape in the grass. It was a big ball! The ball was a strange form. <definition>Form means the shape of something.</definition> It was not round like a normal ball. It was lumpy. "What is this?" asked the bear. He kicked it. The ball bounced! It was made of natural rubber. <definition>Natural means something that comes from the earth and is not made by people.</definition> The bear played with the ball. He forgot about the weather. He forgot about his friend. The ball was so fun!

The little bear went for a walk. He walked down a path. He saw a big tree. He looked under the tree. He saw a tiny seed.

“Hello, seed!” said the bear. The seed did not say hello back. It was just a seed. But the bear felt its presence. <definition>Presence means being there.</definition> He knew the seed was alive.

The bear watched the seed every day. He watched as the seed started to grow. It grew a little root. The root went down into the dirt. Then a tiny sprout popped up.

"Wow!" said the bear. He was happy to see the plant's development. <definition>Development means growing and changing.</definition> The plant grew bigger and bigger. Soon, it was a small tree, just like its parent. The bear was so proud. He had watched it grow from a tiny seed.

<answer>Presence means being there.</answer>

The ants lived in a big society. <definition>A society is a group of people or animals living together in an organized way.</definition> They worked hard. One day, they learned about a giant cookie crumb! The crumb was of great magnitude. <definition>Magnitude means how big or important something is.</definition>

The Queen Ant marked a path to it. She used tiny flags. The ants followed the path. They used a small scale to weigh the crumb. It was the biggest crumb ever! The ants cheered. They carried the crumb home. They had a feast. It was a great day for the ant society.

<question>Where did the ants live?</question>
<answer>The ants lived in a big society.</answer>

<question>What is a society?</question>
<answer>A society is a group of people or animals living together in an organized way.</answer>

<question>What did the ants learn about?</question>
<answer>They learned about a giant cookie crumb.</answer>

<question>What was the crumb's magnitude?</question>
<answer>The crumb was of great magnitude.</answer>

<question>What does magnitude mean?</question>
<answer>Magnitude means how big or important something is.</answer>

<question>Who marked a path to the crumb?</question>
<answer>The Queen Ant marked the path.</answer>

<question>What did she use to mark the path?</question>
<answer>She used tiny flags.</answer>

<question>What did the ants use to weigh the crumb?</question>
<answer>They used a small scale.</answer>

<question>How big was the crumb?</question>
<answer>It was the biggest crumb ever.</answer>

<question>What did the ants do when they found the crumb?</question>
<answer>The ants cheered.</answer>

<question>Where did the ants carry the crumb?</question>
<answer>They carried the crumb home.</answer>

<question>What did the ants have?</question>
<answer>They had a feast.</answer>

Uh oh! A big block was in the way. Timmy did not want the car to crash. He wanted to prevent an accident. <definition>Prevent means to stop something from happening.</definition> He picked up the block. Now the small car could keep going. Timmy used his hands to make car noises. "Vroom, vroom!" he said. He was having so much fun. The little blue car was the best toy.

The fish looked all around. He saw bubbles. He saw seaweed. He did not see his red ball. He swam faster. He bumped into someone! It was a big, friendly whale.

"Are you okay?" asked the whale.

The fish started to cry. "I lost my ball!" he said. "I am very sad."

The whale smiled. "I will help you find it," she said. The whale looked under a rock. She looked by a plant. Soon, they saw something red. It was the ball! The fish was happy. He thanked the whale. He played with his ball in the water.

"Are you okay?" asked Lily. Tim nodded. He got up slowly. He rubbed his chest. "I think so," he said. "Let's play a different game." They decided to draw pictures with chalk. That was safer than running!

He pulled the wagon home. "Look at my rocks!" he said to his mom. She smiled. "You found many rocks!" she said. Timmy was happy. It was a good day for rock collecting.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1210 id=story_qa_060632 =====
The little bunny lived by a big tree. He had soft, white fur. One day, the bunny found a strange thing. It was pink and fleshy. <definition>Fleshy means something that looks like it is made of soft, thick tissue, like skin or a fruit.</definition> What could it be?

A wise owl hooted from the tree. "That is a mushroom!" she said. "But be careful! Some mushrooms are not good to eat."

The bunny was sad. He wanted to eat it. Just then, a truck drove by. It was the mushroom mechanics. <definition>Mechanics are people who fix machines.</definition> But these mechanics fixed mushrooms!

They looked at the mushroom. "This one is safe!" they said. "We use special mechanics to check." The bunny hopped with joy. He ate the fleshy mushroom. It was yummy! He thanked the mushroom mechanics. It was a good day by the big tree.

<question>Where did the bunny live?</question>
<answer>The bunny lived by a big tree.</answer>

<answer>The bunny's fur was white.</answer>

<answer>The bunny found a strange thing.</answer>

<answer>It was pink.</answer>

<answer>Fleshy means something that looks like soft, thick tissue.</answer>

<answer>A wise owl told the bunny.</answer>

<question>What did the owl say it was?</question>
<answer>The owl said it was a mushroom.</answer>

<question>Did the owl say it was safe to eat?</question>
<answer>No, the owl said some mushrooms are not good to eat.</answer>

<question>Who drove by in a truck?</question>
<answer>Mushroom mechanics drove by.</answer>

Fred was a funny frog. He liked to eat bugs. But he was very specific. <definition>Specific means being exact about what you want.</definition> He only liked green bugs. Not red bugs. Not black bugs. Only green bugs!

One day, Mia could not find any green bugs. She looked and looked. Fred was sad. Mia said, "I will find you green bugs, Fred!" She went to the park. She looked under every leaf. Finally, she found ten green bugs! Fred was so happy. He ate them all up. "Ribbit!" he said. "Thank you, Mia!"

The cat sat at the table. He wanted some milk. The milk was in a glass. The glass was big. The cat was small. He tried to reach the milk. He jumped up and down. He could not reach it.

A dog walked in. The dog was big. He saw the cat. He saw the milk. The dog wagged his tail. He wanted the milk too! He helped the cat. The dog pushed the glass closer to the edge. The cat lapped at the milk. The dog lapped at the milk too. They drank it all up. Now they are friends. They sat at the table, happy.

<question>Where did the cat sit?</question>
<answer>The cat sat at the table.</answer>

Rain fell on the seed. Soon, tiny roots started to grow. <definition>Roots are the parts of a plant that grow underground.</definition> The roots helped the plant drink water. They also kept the plant safe in the ground.

A little sprout pushed up. It grew bigger and bigger. It grew into a large tree. <definition>Large means something is very big.</definition> Birds made nests in its branches. Squirrels ran up and down its trunk.

The tree was happy. It gave shade in the summer. It was home to many animals. The tree liked living in the forest. It was a good tree.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1221 id=story_qa_060864 =====
Frederick was a little frog. He lived near a big pond. He loved to help his friends. One day, Frederick saw a problem. A big storm came. It made a mess! There was trash everywhere. This trash could cause problems for the animals. <definition>Cause means to make something happen.</definition>

Frederick wanted to help. He started dealing with the trash. <definition>Dealing means taking care of something.</definition> He picked up paper. He picked up cans. He asked his friends to help too.

A bird asked, "What are you doing, Frederick?"
Frederick said, "I am cleaning up the pond! I care about the animals." <definition>About means to care for.</definition>

All the animals helped Frederick. They cleaned up all the trash. The pond was clean again. The animals were happy. Frederick did a meritorious thing. <definition>Meritorious means deserving praise; doing something good.</definition> Everyone said, "Thank you, Frederick!" He made the pond a better place.

</question>
<answer>
Frederick was a frog.
</answer>

The box was big. It contained many toys. <definition>Contained means held inside.</definition> Zip closed the box. He put tape on it. He rolled the box away.

A little girl stood by the conveyor belt. She smiled at Zip. <definition>By means close to.</definition> "Thank you!" she said. Zip beeped happily. He liked making children happy. That made him feel good too! He packed another toy car. It was a red one!

Her grandma told her that the garden was a sacred place. <definition>Sacred means something very special that should be respected.</definition> She said the plants felt happy when Lily sang to them.

Tom wanted to know if he could sing too. "No," said Lily, "you have to be quiet and listen." Tom sat quietly. He listened to the birds and the wind. He thought the garden felt sacred too.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1233 id=story_qa_061255 =====
The train went through a tunnel. It was very dark inside! The ride felt very short. Soon, the train was passing a big farm. Cows and pigs were eating grass. The farmer needed a measurement. <definition>Measurement tells you how big something is.</definition> He wanted to know how tall the pig was. He used a special stick. The pig was two feet tall! That is a big pig! The train kept going. It was a fun trip!

<question>Where did the train go?</question>
<answer>The train went through a tunnel.</answer>

<question>What was it like in the tunnel?</question>
<answer>It was very dark.</answer>

<question>What did the train pass after the tunnel?</question>
<answer>The train passed a big farm.</answer>

<question>What animals were on the farm?</question>
<answer>Cows and pigs were on the farm.</answer>

<question>What were the animals doing?</question>
<answer>They were eating grass.</answer>

<question>What did the farmer need?</question>
<answer>The farmer needed a measurement.</answer>

<question>What does measurement mean?</question>
<answer>Measurement tells you how big something is.</answer>

<question>What animal did the farmer measure?</question>
<answer>The farmer measured the pig.</answer>

<question>How tall was the pig?</question>
<answer>The pig was two feet tall.</answer>

<question>Was the pig big?</question>
<answer>Yes, the pig was big.</answer>

<question>Was the trip fun?</question>
<answer>Yes, the trip was fun.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1235 id=story_qa_061436 =====
The little bird wanted to fly. But he was inexperienced. <definition>Inexperienced means you have not done something very much.</definition> He had never flown before. All the other birds could fly. They flew in the sky. They flew in the trees. "I want to fly!" said the little bird.

He jumped up and down. He flapped his wings. He flapped them fast. He flapped them slow. He tried and tried. But he could not fly.

Then, Mama Bird came. "I will help you," she said. She showed him how. She pushed him a little. He flapped his wings. He started to fly! He flew in the air. It was so fun! Now he could fly like all the other birds.

<question>What did the little bird want to do?</question>
<answer>The little bird wanted to fly.</answer>

<question>What does inexperienced mean?</question>
<answer>Inexperienced means you have not done something very much.</answer>

<question>Could the little bird fly at the beginning?</question>
<answer>No, he could not fly.</answer>

<question>What could the other birds do?</question>
<answer>The other birds could fly.</answer>

<question>Where did the other birds fly?</question>
<answer>They flew in the sky and in the trees.</answer>

<question>What did the little bird do to try to fly?</question>
<answer>He jumped and flapped his wings.</answer>

<question>Who helped the little bird?</question>
<answer>Mama Bird helped the little bird.</answer>

<question>What did Mama Bird do?</question>
<answer>She showed him how and pushed him a little.</answer>

<question>Did the little bird fly after Mama Bird helped?</question>
<answer>Yes, he started to fly.</answer>

<question>Did the little bird have fun flying?</question>
<answer>Yes, it was so fun.</answer>

<question>Could the little bird fly like the other birds at the end?</question>
<answer>Yes, he could fly like all the other birds.</answer>

He dreamed of going to Tasmania. <definition>Tasmania is an island far away.</definition> He heard it was full of fun animals. His sister looked at the rope. She gave him a funny look. Her look was suggestive. <definition>Suggestive means it makes you think of something.</definition> She thought he would fail. But Timmy was strong! He would not fail!

===== DOC START split=test idx=1238 id=story_qa_061466 =====
Timmy had a toy car. He liked to drive it around. One day, a new person came to the park. The person sat on a bench. Timmy drove his car near the person.

Suddenly, the car stopped! "It's broken!" Timmy said. He started carping. <definition>Carping means complaining about little things.</definition> He complained and complained.

The person on the bench smiled. "Let's look at it," she said. She looked at the car. "I see the problem!" she said. She fixed it as easy as pie.

Timmy smiled. His car worked! He and the person played together. They were now united by the toy car. <definition>United means joined together.</definition> They drove the car around and around. It was a fun day!

<question>What toy did Timmy have?</question>
<answer>Timmy had a toy car.</answer>

<question>Where did the person sit?</question>
<answer>The person sat on a bench.</answer>

<question>What happened to Timmy's car?</question>
<answer>The car stopped.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy say when the car broke?</question>
<answer>Timmy said it was broken.</answer>

<question>What does carping mean?</question>
<answer>Carping means complaining about little things.</answer>

<question>What did the person do when Timmy's car stopped?</question>
<answer>She looked at the car.</answer>

<question>Did the person fix the car?</question>
<answer>Yes, she fixed the car.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy do after the car was fixed?</question>
<answer>Timmy smiled.</answer>

<question>What does united mean?</question>
<answer>United means joined together.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy and the person do together?</question>
<answer>They played together.</answer>

<question>Did Timmy have a fun day?</question>
<answer>Yes, he had a fun day.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1239 id=story_qa_061543 =====
The little blue car was lost. It was not on the road. It was in the woods! Oh no! The car was very sad. It wanted to go home.

A big brown bear saw the car. The bear was not mean. He was nice. "Are you lost?" asked the bear.

The car beeped, "Yes! I am lost in the woods."

The bear said, "Do not worry. I can help." <definition>Worry means to feel nervous or scared.</definition>

The bear pushed the car. He pushed it out of the woods. Now the car was on the road. The car was so happy!

"Thank you!" said the car. It drove away. It was not lost anymore. The car went home to its garage. It was happy to be in its own garage. The end.

<question>Who was lost?</question>
<answer>The little blue car was lost.</answer>

<question>Where was the car?</question>
<answer>The car was in the woods.</answer>

<question>How did the car feel?</question>
<answer>The car was sad.</answer>

<answer>A big brown bear saw the car.</answer>

<answer>No, the bear was not mean.</answer>

<answer>The bear asked, "Are you lost?"</answer>

<answer>The car said, "Yes! I am lost in the woods."</answer>

<question>What did the bear say he could do?</question>
<answer>The bear said he could help.</answer>

<question>What does worry mean?</question>
<answer>Worry means to feel nervous or scared.</answer>

<question>What did the bear do for the car?</question>
<answer>The bear pushed the car out of the woods.</answer>

<answer>The car was on the road.</answer>

<answer>The car was so happy.</answer>

<answer>The car said, "Thank you!"</answer>

<answer>The car went home to its garage.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1240 id=story_qa_061554 =====
Ben and Jen were best friends. They liked to play games. Usually, they played tag. But today, they had a big job. Their mom asked them to clean their toy room. It was a big business. <definition>Business means a job or something you need to do.</definition>

The toys were everywhere! Ben had a toy with a funny eye. It looked a little crossed. That is called strabismus. <definition>Strabismus means when your eyes point in different directions.</definition> Jen giggled at the toy.

Cleaning was hard work. "Let's use cooperation!" said Jen. <definition>Cooperation means working together.</definition> Ben would pick up the blocks. Jen would pick up the cars. Soon, the room was clean.

They sat on the soft rug. "We did it!" said Ben. Their mom was happy. She gave them cookies. Cleaning together was fun. They liked using cooperation.

<question>Who are Ben and Jen?</question>
<answer>Ben and Jen are best friends.</answer>

<question>What did Ben and Jen like to play?</question>
<answer>They liked to play games.</answer>

<question>What did their mom ask them to do?</question>
<answer>Their mom asked them to clean their toy room.</answer>

<question>What does business mean?</question>
<answer>Business means a job or something you need to do.</answer>

<question>Where were the toys?</question>
<answer>The toys were everywhere.</answer>

<question>What was funny about Ben's toy?</question>
<answer>It had a funny eye.</answer>

<question>What is it called when your eyes point in different directions?</question>
<answer>It is called strabismus.</answer>

<question>What did Jen say they should use?</question>
<answer>Jen said they should use cooperation.</answer>

<question>What does cooperation mean?</question>
<answer>Cooperation means working together.</answer>

<question>What did Ben pick up?</question>
<answer>Ben picked up the blocks.</answer>

<question>What did Jen pick up?</question>
<answer>Jen picked up the cars.</answer>

<answer>Their mom gave them cookies.</answer>

<answer>Yes, cleaning together was fun.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1241 id=story_qa_061469 =====
Timmy liked to play with his toys. He had a red car and a blue truck. He also had a big box of blocks. Timmy liked to make towers. Sometimes the towers would fall.

One day, Timmy wanted to know what color block was used the most. <definition>Data is information, like numbers or colors.</definition> He looked at all his blocks. He started to count. He wrote down how many red blocks he had. Then he counted the blue blocks. He did this for all the colors. This was his data.

Timmy saw that he had more yellow blocks. He liked to use the yellow blocks in his towers. He wanted to be a good builder. He knew he could use this data to build even better towers. His towers would be strong! Timmy smiled. He was happy to learn about data. It helped him in his building!

<question>What did Timmy like to play with?</question>
<answer>Timmy liked to play with his toys.</answer>

<question>What color was Timmy's car?</question>
<answer>Timmy's car was red.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy like to make?</question>
<answer>Timmy liked to make towers.</answer>

<question>What would sometimes happen to the towers?</question>
<answer>Sometimes the towers would fall.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy want to know one day?</question>
<answer>Timmy wanted to know what color block was used the most.</answer>

<question>What does data mean?</question>
<answer>Data is information, like numbers or colors.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy count?</question>
<answer>Timmy counted his blocks.</answer>

<question>What color blocks did Timmy have the most of?</question>
<answer>Timmy had the most yellow blocks.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy want to be?</question>
<answer>Timmy wanted to be a good builder.</answer>

<question>What made Timmy smile?</question>
<answer>Learning about data made Timmy smile.</answer>

The dog, Pat, loved his bone. It was his favorite toy. Playing is essential for dogs. <definition>Essential means something you really, really need.</definition> He liked to chew on it all day long.

One day, Pat lost his bone. He looked under the couch. He looked behind the chair. The bone was not there. Pat felt sad. He needed his bone. It was essential to his happiness.

Then, Pat looked in his bed. He saw something! It was his bone! He jumped in his bed and wagged his tail. He was so happy to have his bone back. Now he could chew it all day long. He loved being in his bed with his bone.

Lily wanted to make something special. She got colorful paper. She got glitter and glue. She was going to make cards. <definition>Cards are like small notes you can give to someone.</definition> She made a card for her teacher, Mrs. Davis.

The card had a big sun on it. It said, "Thank you, Mrs. Davis!" Then she made cards for her friends. She drew funny pictures on them. She used lots of glitter.

On the day of the event, Lily gave out her cards. Everyone smiled. Mrs. Davis gave Lily a hug. "These are the best cards ever!" she said. Lily felt happy. June was the best month, and the event was super fun. She couldn't wait for next June!

===== DOC START split=test idx=1249 id=story_qa_061992 =====
Pip was a little cloud. He lived in the sky. He was a happy cloud. One day, Pip had a thought. What makes me a cloud? <definition>Thought means something you think in your head.</definition>

He knew water was one element of a cloud. <definition>Element means one of the things that something is made of.</definition> But what else? He looked down. He saw a tiny being on the ground. <definition>Being means a person or animal.</definition> The being was a girl.

Pip felt a little tension. <definition>Tension means feeling nervous or worried.</definition> He wanted to know! He floated down. "Hello!" said Pip. The girl looked up. "You are fluffy!" she said. "That is your best characteristic!" <definition>Characteristic means something that makes you, you!</definition> Pip smiled. Fluffy! That was it! He floated back up, happy to be a fluffy cloud.

<question>What was Pip?</question>
<answer>Pip was a little cloud.</answer>

<question>Where did Pip live?</question>
<answer>Pip lived in the sky.</answer>

<question>How did Pip feel?</question>
<answer>Pip felt happy.</answer>

<question>What did Pip wonder?</question>
<answer>Pip wondered what made him a cloud.</answer>

<question>What does thought mean?</question>
<answer>Thought means something you think in your head.</answer>

<question>What was one element of a cloud?</question>

His best characteristic was his color. <definition>A characteristic is something that makes you special.</definition> He was a bright, sparkly blue. But he still did not want to shine.

One night, a girl looked up at the sky. She needed a little light. The star saw her. He thought, "Maybe shining is not so bad."

He took a deep breath. Then, he shined his light for the girl. She smiled. The star felt happy! His attitude changed. Shining was fun after all! Now, he shines bright every night.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1258 id=story_qa_062322 =====
Once, there was a puppy named Gus. Gus loved to play! He lived with a girl named Lily. Lily loved Gus very much.

One day, Lily told Gus, "Stay here, Gus! Do not dig in the garden." But Gus was a puppy. He forgot what Lily said. That is called disobedience. <definition>Disobedience means not doing what you are told to do.</definition>

Gus saw a butterfly in the garden. He started to dig! He dug and dug. He found a shiny rock.

"Wow!" said Gus. "This rock looks like something from mythology!" <definition>Mythology is a collection of old stories, like stories about Greek gods and heroes.</definition> The rock had pictures of Zeus. Zeus was the king of the gods.

Lily came outside. "Gus! What did I say?" Gus showed her the rock. Lily smiled. "It's okay, Gus. The rock is cool, but no more digging!" Gus wagged his tail. He learned a good lesson about disobedience.

<question>What was the puppy's name?</question>
<answer>The puppy's name was Gus.</answer>

<question>Who did Gus live with?</question>
<answer>Gus lived with a girl named Lily.</answer>

<answer>Lily told Gus to stay and not dig in the garden.</answer>

The bird and the worm worked in the sun. They worked in the rain. At last, the nest was done. It was the best nest any bird had ever seen! The bird was happy. He thanked the worm. Now he had a home.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1270 id=story_qa_063108 =====
The little fish swam in the sea. He liked to swim against the current. <definition>Current is when water moves in one direction.</definition> It made him strong! One day, he saw something new. It was a sea star! Sea stars were common. <definition>Common means you see them a lot.</definition>

But this sea star was blue! Most sea stars were red or orange. This was a big change. <definition>Change means something is different.</definition> The little fish swam closer.

Surprisingly, the sea star smiled. <definition>Surprisingly means it is unexpected.</definition> "Hello!" said the sea star. "I am new here." The little fish was happy to have a new friend. They swam together all day. He learned the blue sea star's name was Bluey. It was fun to swim with someone different. The little fish was happy about the change.

He called for help. Another ant came. Then another. Soon, many ants were there. They all pushed together. The crumb moved! They pushed it all the way to the hill.

"We did it!" shouted one ant. All the ants cheered. They were happy to work together. They took the crumb inside. It was a good day to be a worker ant. They all used their body to help. They were one big, strong team!
