===== DOC START split=test idx=1285 id=story_qa_063755 =====
The kids wanted to play space explorers. They needed to equip their spaceship. <definition>Equip means to get ready with the things that are needed.</definition> Jan got a box. Ben got a black marker. They drew buttons all over the box.

"This button makes us fly!" said Jan.

"No! That button is for snacks," said Ben.

Their mom watched them. She smiled at their interpretation of space. <definition>Interpretation means what something means to you.</definition> Mom thought they were very creative.

The opposite of up is down. <definition>Opposite means the other side or something completely different.</definition> The opposite of black is white!

The spaceship was parked by a big tree. "We are ready for space!" they shouted. They put on their helmets. Blast off!

<question>What did the kids want to play?</question>
<answer>They wanted to play space explorers.</answer>

<answer>They needed to equip their spaceship.</answer>

<question>What did Jan get?</question>
<answer>Jan got a box.</answer>

<question>What did Ben get?</question>
<answer>Ben got a black marker.</answer>

<question>What did they draw on the box?</question>
<answer>They drew buttons.</answer>

<answer>She said it made them fly.</answer>

<question>What did Ben say another button did?</question>
<answer>He said it was for snacks.</answer>

The blue bird felt a little better. He looked around. He saw a pretty red bird. She was eating berries by the bush. He flew closer. He was certain he wanted to meet her. Maybe she would be his friend. He chirped a happy song. She chirped back!

The cat meowed. "I am Mittens!"

Lucky told Mittens he came from far away. "I came from japan!" he said. <definition>Japan is a country far away across the ocean.</definition>

Mittens had never been to japan. She wanted to go!

"Maybe someday we can go to japan by boat!" said Lucky. Mittens purred. She liked her new friend. They played in the park until it was dark.

It was hard work to go up. They got to the top. "Wow!" said a duck. The view was very nice. They could see the whole lake. They could see all their friends. Now the ducks were happy they went up the hill. They all slid down. It was very fun.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1304 id=story_qa_064820 =====
Tim was a brave dog. One day, Tim heard some news. The news said a cat was the town's antagonist. <definition>An antagonist is a bad guy in a story.</definition> The cat liked to steal toys. Tim decided to stop him.

Tim followed the cat. The cat walked backward. He did not see Tim! The cat tripped. He dropped a strange substance. <definition>A substance is a type of material.</definition> It looked like jelly.

Tim picked up the jelly. He gave it to the police. The police knew what to do. The cat was caught! Tim was a hero. He got one big bone as a reward.

<question>Who was Tim?</question>
<answer>Tim was a brave dog.</answer>

<question>What did Tim hear in the news?</question>
<answer>A cat was the town's antagonist.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1313 id=story_qa_065026 =====
The little bluebird was sad. She did not know her purpose. <definition>Purpose means the reason why you do something.</definition> All the other birds knew what to do. They built nests. They sang songs. She wanted to have one, too.

"What is my purpose?" she asked a big owl. The owl blinked. "Your purpose is just being you!" he hooted. <definition>Being means what you are, like a bird or a cat.</definition>

The bluebird thought about this. She flew into the sky. She saw a little girl crying. The girl had lost her balloon. The bluebird sang a sweet song. The girl smiled. The bluebird felt happy. Maybe her purpose was to make others happy. She liked being a bird who could sing.

<answer>She did not know her purpose.</answer>

<answer>Purpose means the reason why you do something.</answer>

<answer>They built nests and sang songs.</answer>

<answer>She asked a big owl.</answer>

<answer>She saw a little girl crying.</answer>

<answer>She had lost her balloon.</answer>

<answer>The bluebird felt happy.</answer>

The dog licked the ice cream. It was so good! He was a happy dog. The truck had an american flag. <definition>American means something from the United States of America.</definition> The dog barked happily at the flag. He loved ice cream and flags!

===== DOC START split=test idx=1323 id=story_qa_065922 =====
The little blue bird wanted to fly far. But his wings were not strong. He needed to strengthen them. <definition>Strengthen means to make something stronger.</definition> He flapped his wings every day. He flapped them up and down. He flapped them fast and slow.

Soon, his wings developed muscles. <definition>Developed means that something grew or got better.</definition> They were big and strong. He felt ready for anything. He asked, "Is there any wind today?" <definition>Any means even one.</definition>

The wind said, "Yes! I am here!" The bird smiled. He jumped up and flew. He flew higher than the trees. He flew far, far away. He was so happy.

<question>What color was the bird?</question>
<answer>The bird was blue.</answer>

<question>What did the bird want to do?</question>
<answer>The bird wanted to fly far.</answer>

<question>Why couldn't the bird fly far at first?</question>
<answer>His wings were not strong.</answer>

<question>What does strengthen mean?</question>
<answer>Strengthen means to make something stronger.</answer>

<question>What did the bird do every day?</question>
<answer>He flapped his wings.</answer>

<question>How did the bird flap his wings?</question>
<answer>He flapped them up and down, fast and slow.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1324 id=story_qa_066094 =====
The sun came up. It was daylight. <definition>Daylight is the light from the sun.</definition> Lily woke up. She wanted to play. "Can I play outside, Mama?" she asked. Mama said, "Yes, you can. But be careful."

Lily ran outside. She saw her friend, Tom. Tom was playing with his dog. The dog was small and brown. "Can I play too?" asked Lily. Tom said yes.

They played tag. They ran as fast as they could. The dog ran with them. He liked to run, especially in the grass. <definition>Especially means more than other things.</definition>

Tom said, "Let's build a fort!" Lily liked that idea. They made an agreement. <definition>An agreement is when people decide on something together.</definition> "We will build the best fort ever!" said Lily. They used sticks and leaves. It was a fun day.

<answer>The sun came up.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1329 id=story_qa_066228 =====

"Hello!" said Timmy Ant. The bug did not say hello back. It was a parasitic bug. <definition>Parasitic means it takes food from others.</definition> It only wanted to eat Timmy Ant's food! Timmy Ant ran back to his friends. "That bug is no good," he said. The ants worked all day long. It was continuous work. <definition>Continuous means it never stops.</definition> They carried all their food away from the bug. The bug went away.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1342 id=story_qa_066490 =====
The pig had a fan. He liked to blow air on his face. It felt cool! One day, Pig wanted to write a story. He sat at his desk. He wanted to type. <definition>Type means to write using a keyboard.</definition>

But the fan was on his desk! It kept blowing his papers away. "Oh no!" said Pig. He moved the fan to the floor. Now he could type.

Pig typed a story about a cat. The cat liked to play with yarn. She would bat it all around the room. Then, the wind would blow the yarn outside. The cat would chase it. Pig liked his story. He typed, "The End!" He was happy. He gave the fan a little wave.

<question>What did the pig have?</question>
<answer>The pig had a fan.</answer>

<question>What did the pig like to do with the fan?</question>
<answer>He liked to blow air on his face.</answer>

<question>What did Pig want to write?</question>
<answer>Pig wanted to write a story.</answer>

<question>Where was the fan?</question>
<answer>The fan was on his desk.</answer>

<question>What did the fan do to his papers?</question>
<answer>It blew his papers away.</answer>

<question>What did Pig say when the papers blew away?</question>
<answer>He said oh no.</answer>

<question>Where did Pig move the fan?</question>
<answer>He moved the fan to the floor.</answer>

<question>What does type mean?</question>
<answer>Type means to write using a keyboard.</answer>

<question>What was Pig's story about?</question>
<answer>His story was about a cat.</answer>

<question>What did the cat like to play with?</question>
<answer>The cat liked to play with yarn.</answer>

<question>What would the cat do with the yarn?</question>
<answer>She would bat it all around the room.</answer>

<question>What happened to the yarn outside?</question>
<answer>The wind would blow the yarn outside.</answer>

<question>What did the cat do when the yarn blew outside?</question>
<answer>The cat would chase it.</answer>

Running fast was her best skill. <definition>Skill means something you are good at doing.</definition> Her mom said running had a very important function. <definition>Function means what something is used for.</definition> It helped her escape from danger!

One day, Zoe was playing near the watering hole. She saw a lion! The lion was big and scary. Zoe knew just what to do. She used her running skill.

She ran very fast. Her stripes helped too. <definition>Stripes are lines of color.</definition> The stripes made it hard for the lion to see her clearly when she ran. They made her look blurry.

Zoe ran all the way back to her mom. Her mom was happy to see her safe. "See," Mom said, "running is a very important function!" Zoe was glad she had such a good skill. She was also glad for her stripes. They helped keep her safe!

===== DOC START split=test idx=1358 id=story_qa_067097 =====
The little dog, Patches, was very fast. He liked to run in the park. One day, he saw a car. It was going fast! Patches ran right in front of it. Oh no!

A girl saw Patches. She had a bad feeling. <definition>Feeling means how you sense or experience something.</definition> She knew the car could hurt Patches. She had to prevent it. <definition>Prevent means to stop something from happening.</definition> She ran very fast.

She grabbed Patches just in time. Patches was safe! He was not hurt. He did not face death. <definition>Death means when someone stops living.</definition> The girl hugged Patches.

"Good dog," she said. "Be careful!"

Patches wagged his tail. He liked the girl. He learned a good lesson. He would not run in front of cars again. He would be safe. He was a good type of dog. <definition>Type means a kind of something.</definition> He was a smart dog. He was happy to be alive.

<question>What was the little dog's name?</question>
<answer>The little dog's name was Patches.</answer>

<question>Where did Patches like to run?</question>
<answer>Patches liked to run in the park.</answer>

<question>What did Patches see one day?</question>
<answer>Patches saw a car.</answer>

<question>Was the car going fast or slow?</question>
<answer>The car was going fast.</answer>

<question>What did Patches do in front of the car?</question>
<answer>Patches ran right in front of it.</answer>

<question>Who saw Patches run in front of the car?</question>
<answer>A girl saw Patches.</answer>

<question>What kind of feeling did the girl have?</question>
<answer>She had a bad feeling.</answer>

<question>What does feeling mean?</question>
<answer>Feeling means how you sense or experience something.</answer>

<question>What did the girl know the car could do?</question>
<answer>She knew the car could hurt Patches.</answer>

<question>What does prevent mean?</question>
<answer>Prevent means to stop something from happening.</answer>

<question>What did the girl do to help Patches?</question>
<answer>She ran very fast and grabbed Patches.</answer>

<question>Was Patches hurt by the car?</question>
<answer>No, Patches was not hurt.</answer>

"I need to go home," Timmy said.

The teacher called Timmy's mom. Soon, Timmy was in the car. He held his tummy all the way home.

Mom gave Timmy some ginger ale. He drank it slowly. He lay down in his bed. His dog, Buster, jumped up on the bed too. Buster licked Timmy’s face.

“Thanks, Buster,” Timmy said.

Timmy closed his eyes. He felt a little better with Buster near him. He knew his mom was there to help him. He was safe in his house. He hoped his gastrointestinal problem would go away soon. He wanted to play outside. He wanted to run and jump. But for now, he would rest.

<answer>The moth held on tight.</answer>

Today they would learn about the letter A. A is for apple. A is for ant. A is for... astronaut! Lily wanted to be an astronaut as she grew up. <definition>As means while or during the time that.</definition> She wanted to fly to the moon!

"Goodbye, Patches!" Lily said. Patches barked. He wanted to go to school, too! But dogs can't go to school.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1381 id=story_qa_068049 =====
The little pig wanted to be the governor. <definition>A governor is the person in charge of a state.</definition> He wore a tiny suit. He waved to all his friends. He even made a speech!

"I will give everyone cookies!" he said. The other animals cheered. Being governor seemed fun.

But being governor also meant doing work. The pig had to learn about administrative things. <definition>Administrative means related to managing a company or organization.</definition> That sounded boring!

His friend, the cow, helped him. She showed him papers. She showed him charts. The pig yawned. It was hard to understand.

"Maybe," said the pig, "being governor is too much work."

The cow smiled. "Maybe you can just bake cookies?"

The pig grinned. He liked that idea better. He baked cookies for everyone. They were yummy! Being a baker was more fun than the administrative work of a governor.

The pig smiled. He rolled in the mud. Then he went to the contest. He was the muddiest pig by far. He won! He got a big, blue ribbon. He was happy to be himself. He didn't need any hats.

The ants worked hard. They pushed and pulled. The grass was interwoven. <definition>Interwoven means things that are connected or laced together.</definition> It made it hard to walk.

"We need help!" said a small ant. So they called their friends. More ants came to help. They all pulled together.

Suddenly, the cadaver moved! It wasn't dead! It was just sleeping. The bug woke up. It stood up and did a funny act. <definition>Act means to do something, or to pretend to be someone else.</definition> The ants laughed. The bug bowed. Then it flew away. The ants waved goodbye. What a fun day!

A bird flew by. "Which direction should I go?" asked the car. <definition>Direction means which way you are going.</definition>

The bird said, "Go straight ahead!"

The car zoomed in that direction. The road was still hard, but the car didn't give up. He went faster and faster. Soon, he was at the finish line! He won the race!

The little red hen lived on a farm. She liked to work in the garden. One day, she found a seed. "Who will help me plant this?" she asked. Someone said, "Not I!" The hen was sad. <definition>Not means no.</definition>

So, she planted it all by herself. The seed grew into a long stalk. <definition>Long means it takes a while to go from one end to the other.</definition> It grew and grew. Soon, there was corn on the stalk.

"Who will help me cut this corn?" she asked. Again, someone said, "Not I!" The hen sighed. She cut the corn all by herself. She put it in her little red house. It was not easy work. She was happy it was done.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1410 id=story_qa_069698 =====
The little blue car was new. It was designed to be fast. But the other cars were judging it. <definition>Judging means to form an opinion about something.</definition> "You are too small," they said. "You are too blue!"

The little blue car felt sad. It wanted to race. It wanted to be liked. It went to its garage. A wise old truck was there. "Why are you sad?" asked the truck.

"The other cars are mean," said the little blue car. "They are judging me."

The wise old truck smiled. "How you are perceived is up to you." <definition>Perceived means how you are seen or understood.</definition> "Show them how fast you are! That is what you were designed to do!"

The little blue car smiled. It went to the race track. It raced very fast. The other cars were surprised. The little blue car won! Now, no one was judging it. They all wanted to be its friend.

<question>What color was the car?</question>
<answer>The car was blue.</answer>

<answer>The car was new.</answer>

<answer>Judging means to form an opinion about something.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1413 id=story_qa_069735 =====
Timmy had a toy box by his bed. One day, toy soldiers were strongly marching. <definition>Strongly means with power and force.</definition> They were invading the blocks! <definition>Invading means entering a place with force.</definition>

"Stop!" yelled Timmy. The toy soldiers did not listen. Timmy wiggled his big toe. He wiggled his other toe. He stood up and toed the toy box. <definition>Toed means to touch or kick something with your toes.</definition> All the toys fell back inside.

Timmy smiled. He was the boss of the toys! He put the lid on the toy box. "No more invading," he said. Now it was time for a nap. He was a very good boss.

<question>Where was Timmy's toy box?</question>
<answer>Timmy's toy box was by his bed.</answer>

<answer>The toy soldiers were marching.</answer>

<answer>Strongly means with power and force.</answer>

<answer>Invading means entering a place with force.</answer>

<answer>Timmy yelled stop.</answer>

<question>Did the toy soldiers listen to Timmy?</question>
<answer>No, they did not listen.</answer>

<answer>Timmy wiggled his toes.</answer>

<answer>He toed the toy box.</answer>

<answer>Timmy was the boss of the toys.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1414 id=story_qa_069717 =====
The ants needed help. They had a big financial problem. <definition>Financial means about money.</definition> They needed food. Timmy Ant found a netlike web. <definition>Netlike means it looks like a net.</definition> A juicy bug was stuck! "I can't move it alone," Timmy said. He called his friends. They pulled and pulled. The bug came free. The ants cheered. They had solved their problem! Now they could eat.

<question>Who needed help?</question>
<answer>The ants needed help.</answer>

<question>What kind of problem did they have?</question>
<answer>They had a big financial problem.</answer>

<question>What does financial mean?</question>
<answer>Financial means about money.</answer>

<question>What did the ants need?</question>
<answer>They needed food.</answer>

<question>Who found something good?</question>
<answer>Timmy Ant found something good.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy Ant find?</question>
<answer>Timmy Ant found a netlike web.</answer>

<question>What does netlike mean?</question>
<answer>Netlike means it looks like a net.</answer>

<question>What was in the web?</question>
<answer>A juicy bug was in the web.</answer>

<question>Could Timmy move it alone?</question>
<answer>No, Timmy could not move it alone.</answer>

<question>Who did Timmy call?</question>
<answer>He called his friends.</answer>

<question>What did they do to the bug?</question>
<answer>They pulled and pulled.</answer>

<question>What happened when they pulled?</question>
<answer>The bug came free.</answer>

<question>What did the ants do when the bug was free?</question>
<answer>The ants cheered.</answer>

<question>Did they solve their problem?</question>
<answer>Yes, they solved their problem.</answer>

<question>What could they do now?</question>
<answer>Now they could eat.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1420 id=story_qa_070000 =====
The little duck was yellow. She lived near a big lake. She liked to swim all day. She would flap her wings. She would dive down low.

One day, the duck got hurt. A bee stung her foot. Her foot got red and sore. It even had pus. <definition>Pus is a thick yellowish liquid that can form when you have an infection.</definition>

"Oh no!" said the duck. She felt very sad.

A wise owl saw the duck. The owl knew a lot about animals. He said, "Do not worry. You are a duck of the Anas genus. <definition>Genus is a group that animals are sorted into.</definition> Ducks in your genus are very strong. You will be okay!"

The duck felt better. She cleaned her foot. Soon, she was swimming again.

<question>What color was the little duck?</question>
<answer>The little duck was yellow.</answer>

<question>Where did the duck live?</question>
<answer>She lived near a big lake.</answer>

<question>What did the duck like to do all day?</question>
<answer>She liked to swim all day.</answer>

<question>What happened to the duck one day?</question>
<answer>A bee stung her foot.</answer>

<question>What happened to her foot?</question>
<answer>Her foot got red and sore.</answer>

<question>What is pus?</question>
<answer>Pus is a thick yellowish liquid that can form when you have an infection.</answer>

<question>Who saw the duck?</question>
<answer>A wise owl saw the duck.</answer>

<question>What did the owl say to the duck?</question>
<answer>He said she would be okay.</answer>

<question>What is a genus?</question>
<answer>Genus is a group that animals are sorted into.</answer>

<question>What did the duck do to her foot?</question>
<answer>She cleaned her foot.</answer>

<question>What did the duck do after cleaning her foot?</question>
<answer>She was swimming again.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1433 id=story_qa_070772 =====
Timmy liked to play in the woods. One day, he discovered a big rock. <definition>Discovered means to find something new or something you didn't know about.</definition> It was covered in moss. He climbed on top of the rock.

"I am the king!" he said. He looked all around. He saw a tiny hole in the rock. He peeked inside. What was in there?

Timmy reached in the hole. He pulled out a shiny, red button. "Wow!" he said. "A magic button!"

He pushed the button. Nothing happened. Timmy giggled. Maybe the magic was hiding. He put the button in his pocket. He would try it again later.

<question>Where did Timmy like to play?</question>
<answer>Timmy liked to play in the woods.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy discover one day?</question>
<answer>He discovered a big rock.</answer>

<question>What does discovered mean?</question>
<answer>Discovered means to find something new.</answer>

<question>What was the rock covered in?</question>
<answer>It was covered in moss.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy say when he climbed on the rock?</question>
<answer>He said I am the king.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy see in the rock?</question>
<answer>He saw a tiny hole.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy find in the hole?</question>
<answer>He found a shiny, red button.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy call the button?</question>
<answer>He called it a magic button.</answer>

<question>What happened when Timmy pushed the button?</question>
<answer>Nothing happened.</answer>

<question>Where did Timmy put the button?</question>
<answer>He put the button in his pocket.</answer>
===== DOC END =====

One day, Timmy had an idea. He wanted to give rides. He put a sign on the car. It said, "Rides for 1 Penny!" Soon, many people came. <definition>People are more than one person.</definition> They wanted to ride in the little red car.

Timmy drove them all around the yard. Everyone had so much fun. Timmy felt happy. The little red car was used, but it made many people smile. He loved his job. The people loved the ride. It was a good day.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1436 id=story_qa_070612 =====
The little bear liked honey. He loved the sweet flavor. <definition>Flavor is how something tastes.</definition> One day, he found an empty honey pot. He was in a bad condition. <definition>Condition is how something or someone feels or looks.</definition> He wanted more honey!

He saw a bee. "Where is the honey?" he asked. It was a rhetorical question. <definition>Rhetorical means a question you don't need an answer to.</definition> He didn't really want the bee to answer. He just wanted honey!

The bee buzzed. It flew to a big tree. The bear followed. He saw a whole hive of honey! He was happy to be a bear who loved honey. He ate and ate! He was the happiest being in the forest.

<question>What did the little bear like?</question>
<answer>The little bear liked honey.</answer>

One Sunday, Lily brought her teddy bear. She wanted Teddy to hear the songs. She sat Teddy next to her. The church was full! Every seat was taken. Even Teddy's seat!

After church, Lily's mom made cookies. Lily ate three cookies. Teddy ate zero cookies. Teddy did not have the capacity to eat cookies. He was a bear! Lily giggled. "Maybe next Sunday, Teddy!" she said.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1449 id=story_qa_071714 =====
The ants had a big hill. They worked hard. One day, Timmy came to play. He was not careful. He stepped on the ant hill. He wanted to destroy it. <definition>Destroy means to break something so it can't be used again.</definition>

"Stop!" yelled a tiny voice. Timmy looked down. He saw an ant. The ant was special. He was naturalized. <definition>Naturalized means that someone who wasn't born here now belongs here.</definition> He now lived in the ant hill.

"Please do not destroy our home!" said the ant. "Someone worked very hard."

Timmy felt bad. He did not want to hurt the ants. "I am sorry," said Timmy. "I will not step on your hill again." The ants cheered. Timmy went to play somewhere else. He was careful this time.

<question>What did the ants have?</question>
<answer>The ants had a big hill.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy do to the ant hill?</question>
<answer>He stepped on the ant hill.</answer>

<question>What does destroy mean?</question>
<answer>Destroy means to break something so it can't be used again.</answer>

<question>What did the ant say to Timmy?</question>
<answer>Please do not destroy our home.</answer>

<question>How did Timmy feel?</question>
<answer>Timmy felt bad.</answer>

Kim and Ben looked sad. Then Kim smiled. "We can build together!" she said. Ben smiled too. They both used the blocks. They built a tall tower. It was the best tower ever. It was good to be friends. It was good to share.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1467 id=story_qa_072567 =====
Lola was a tiny mouse. But she was very capable. <definition>Capable means you are able to do something.</definition> She could climb anything! One day, the circus came to town. Lola saw a poster. It showed a brave bullfighter! <definition>A bullfighter is someone who fights bulls in a ring.</definition>

"Wow!" said Lola. "I want to be a bullfighter!"

The other mice laughed. "You are too small!" they said.

But Lola did not listen. She found a red scarf. She stood in front of a big cat. The cat yawned. Lola waved the scarf. The cat just blinked.

Lola kept waving. She was a very capable mouse. Finally, the cat chased the scarf! Lola laughed. She was a bullfighter after all!

<answer>Lola was a tiny mouse.</answer>

<answer>The circus came to town.</answer>

<answer>A bullfighter is someone who fights bulls in a ring.</answer>

<answer>Lola wanted to be a bullfighter.</answer>

<answer>They said she was too small.</answer>

<answer>The scarf was red.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1468 id=story_qa_072635 =====
The little car was red. It was a very shiny red. Timmy loved his red car. He drove it all around the house. He made car noises. "Vroom, vroom!" he said. He drove it to the kitchen. He drove it to his room.

Timmy wanted a friend to play with. He saw his teddy bear. The teddy bear was brown. Timmy had an idea. He took some blocks. He made a road for his car. <definition>Made means to create or build something.</definition>

The road was long and bumpy. The red car drove on the road. Vroom! Vroom! Teddy watched the car go by. Timmy and Teddy played all day. They had so much fun with the red car. Now Timmy was happy.

<question>What color was the little car?</question>
<answer>The car was red.</answer>

<question>Who loved the red car?</question>
<answer>Timmy loved the red car.</answer>

<question>What noises did Timmy make?</question>
<answer>Timmy said vroom vroom.</answer>

<question>Who did Timmy want to play with?</question>
<answer>Timmy wanted a friend.</answer>

<question>What color was the teddy bear?</question>
<answer>The teddy bear was brown.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy make for his car?</question>
<answer>Timmy made a road.</answer>

<question>What does made mean?</question>
<answer>Made means to create or build something.</answer>

Suddenly, the mayor ran up to her. "No hats today!" he said. Lily smiled. She pulled a tiny bird out of her hat. "It's just my pet!" she said. The mayor laughed. The law was silly, after all. Lily went to school. She had fun in class.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1488 id=story_qa_073315 =====
Timmy had a red bike. It was his best toy. One day, Timmy left his bike in the rain. The next day, he saw brown spots. They looked like rust. Rust is an oxide. <definition>Oxide is a compound that contains oxygen and another element.</definition> Timmy was sad. His bike was getting rusty.

Dad said, "Don't worry, Timmy!" They went to the store. They got some cleaner. They rubbed the brown spots. The rust went away! Timmy was happy again.

They went to the park. Timmy saw another boy. The boy had a blue bike. It was such a cool bike! <definition>Such means of a kind that is about to be specified.</definition> Timmy and the boy rode their bikes together. They had so much fun. Timmy loved his red bike and his new friend.

<question>What color was Timmy's bike?</question>
<answer>Timmy's bike was red.</answer>

<question>What was Timmy's best toy?</question>
<answer>His bike was his best toy.</answer>

<question>Where did Timmy leave his bike?</question>
<answer>Timmy left his bike in the rain.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy see on his bike the next day?</question>
<answer>He saw brown spots.</answer>

<question>What did the brown spots look like?</question>
<answer>They looked like rust.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1489 id=story_qa_073407 =====

The pig ran outside. He looked at the mud. Some of the mud was dry. He needed more water. He got his little red bucket. He filled it with water. He poured the water on the dry mud. Now the conditions were right! The mud was wet and sloppy. The little pig jumped in. He was so happy! He played in the mud all day long.

Pete followed the smell. He looked up at the house. He saw smoke coming from the chimney. He sniffed the air again. The smell was stronger near the eaves. <definition>Eaves are the part of the roof that hangs over the side of the house.</definition>

Pete stood on his tippy toes. He peeked under the eaves. He saw a little girl. She was eating a sandwich. It was a pork sandwich! Pete wanted a bite. He oinked very loudly. The girl looked down. She smiled at Pete. She gave him a small piece of her pork sandwich. Pete was very happy.

Tim saw a bug. It was almost under a rock! He reached for it. The bug ran away. "Oh no!" said Tim.

Ben saw a bug, too. It was red with black spots. "I see one!" he said. He tried to catch it. The bug flew up high.

"What is your bug's name?" asked Tim. Ben looked sad. "I don't know," he said.

They kept looking. They saw a big, green bug. It was in a jar. Another team had found it. Tim and Ben smiled. Finding bugs was fun! They would find one soon.

One day, a goat came to the farm. Goats are hooved animals. <definition>Hooved means having hooves, which are hard coverings on the feet of animals like goats and horses.</definition> The goat said, "I will be your bell!" He stood on a hill. "Baaaa!" he yelled. Now the community knew when it was time to eat! The farm was not lacking anymore.

"Let's share it," said Lily.

"Okay!" said Tom.

They put the atom between two rocks. They wanted to keep it safe. But then, a big dog came! The dog ran near the rocks. He accidentally kicked the atom. It flew to the left!

Lily and Tom were sad. They looked everywhere. They looked for a long time. "Let's work together," said Lily. "If we are united, we can find it!" <definition>United means working together as a team.</definition>

So, they worked together. They looked and looked. At last, they found the atom! It was behind a tree. They were so happy to have their toy back.

The little pig, Pip, had a toy car. He liked to put it on the table. Then he would push it. It would zoom to the edge. Falling off was inevitable. <definition>Inevitable means something that is sure to happen.</definition> Pip knew it would happen. But he did it again and again.

One day, his friend May came over. "Can I play?" she asked.

Pip said, "Yes! Do you want to push my car?"

May looked at the car. "Will it fall?"

Pip said, "Yes, that is what always happens!"

May shook her head. "I don't want to put it on the table. Can we play with any of your other toys?"

Pip smiled. "Yes! Let's play with blocks!"

===== DOC START split=test idx=1520 id=story_qa_074867 =====

His teacher taught them to respect their tools. <definition>Respect means to treat something nicely.</definition> They put them away when they were done. Ben was careful with his clay. He did not want to break it.

He had to consider what to make next. <definition>Consider means to think about something carefully.</definition> Should he make a bird? Or a car? He smiled. He would make a funny monster! It would have big teeth and small feet. It would be the best monster ever.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1522 id=story_qa_075111 =====
The little pig loved mud. He loved to roll in it. One day, he found a new mud puddle. It was big and brown. He ran to it. But something was different. There was a funny cloth in the mud. <definition>Twilled means the cloth has a pattern of diagonal lines.</definition> It was a twilled cloth. It felt bumpy.

The pig poked it with his nose. He sniffed it. He still wanted to roll in the mud. He tried to move the cloth. It was stuck. He pulled and pulled. Finally, the cloth came loose. The pig fell back!

Under the cloth was a big, juicy worm! The pig forgot all about the mud. He gobbled up the worm. It was the best day ever! He looked at the cloth. He decided it was good for finding worms. He put the twilled cloth back in the mud. Maybe there were more worms under it!

<answer>The little pig loved mud.</answer>

<answer>It felt bumpy.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1527 id=story_qa_075386 =====
Pip was a small pig. He had one big problem. Pip did not like mud! All the other pigs loved mud. They rolled in it all day. Pip thought it was yucky. This was abnormal. <definition>Abnormal means not normal, or not like everyone else.</definition>

One day, Pip found a soft cloth. It was red and blue. He used the cloth to wipe his feet. Now they were clean! The other pigs looked at him. They were covered in mud. Pip was not.

“Why aren’t you in the mud?” asked a pig.
“I don’t like it,” said Pip. He showed them his cloth. “I like being clean!”

The other pigs shrugged. They went back to their mud. Pip smiled. He liked being the only one with a clean cloth. He was happy being different.

<question>What kind of animal was Pip?</question>
<answer>Pip was a pig.</answer>

<question>What did Pip not like?</question>
<answer>Pip did not like mud.</answer>

<answer>The other pigs loved mud.</answer>

<answer>Abnormal means not normal.</answer>

<answer>Pip found a soft cloth.</answer>

<answer>The cloth was red and blue.</answer>

<answer>He used it to wipe his feet.</answer>

<question>What did the other pigs look like?</question>
<answer>They were covered in mud.</answer>

<question>What did Pip say he liked?</question>
<answer>Pip said he liked being clean.</answer>

Pip's mom made him apple juice. It was a new product. <definition>Product means something that is made.</definition> Pip tried the juice. "Yummy!" he said. His tummy felt better.

Pip's mom said, "All done with juice. No more apples today!" <definition>Done means finished.</definition> Pip was sad, but he knew his mom was right. Too many apples are not good. He would eat carrots instead.

"Ha!" said the cloud. "I will use deception!" <definition>Deception means to trick someone.</definition> He floated closer and closer. He thought he was going to win.

The cloud giggled. It was fun to try. The sun and the cloud were friends. They liked to play games all day. The cloud went back to its normal form. They both smiled.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1537 id=story_qa_075785 =====
The cat sat on a mat. The mat was at the door. The cat was big and fluffy. It had a long tail. The cat liked to sleep. Sleeping was its favorite thing. One day, a bird landed near the cat. The bird was small and brown. The cat opened one eye. It wiggled its tail. The bird hopped closer. The cat got ready to pounce! It wiggled its whole body. <definition>Your body is all of you, from your head to your toes.</definition> Then, the cat fell back asleep. The bird flew away. The cat did not see it go. The cat went back to dreaming about fish. Fish are yummy to eat.

<question>Where did the cat sit?</question>

<answer>Timmy saw an ascomycetous fungus.</answer>

<question>What is an ascomycetous fungus?</question>
<answer>Ascomycetous is a kind of fungus with spores in a sac.</answer>

<question>Did Timmy pick the fungus?</question>
<answer>Timmy did not pick it.</answer>

<question>What did Timmy use to catch a fish?</question>
<answer>Timmy used his hooks to catch a fish.</answer>

<question>Was it a fun day?</question>
<answer>Yes, it was a fun day.</answer>

He drove past a farm. He drove past a school. Soon, he could smell the salt. He could hear the waves. He was almost to the coast! He parked his car and smiled. The beach was so much fun!

===== DOC START split=test idx=1571 id=story_qa_077563 =====
The little bear lived up north. One day, he wanted to go south. <definition>South is the direction that is down on a map.</definition> He packed a bag. He put in berries and a map.

He walked for a long time. He saw a bird with brownish feathers. The bird sang a song. It was a happy song!

The bear kept walking. He saw a fox. The fox wanted to act in a play. <definition>Act means to pretend to be someone else in a story.</definition> "Will you act with me?" asked the fox.

The bear said, "Yes!"

They found a stage. The fox was a king. The bear was a silly clown. They made everyone laugh.

After the play, the bear kept going south. He was happy he helped the fox. He had a good day. He ate his berries. He looked at his map. He found a cozy cave to sleep in for the night. He dreamed of the play.

<question>Where did the little bear live?</question>
<answer>The little bear lived up north.</answer>

<answer>The bear wanted to go south.</answer>

<answer>South is the direction that is down on a map.</answer>

"Kindness!" said the little car. The truck smiled, and the little blue car drove home.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1606 id=story_qa_079142 =====
The little red car was stuck in the mud. Oh no! Timmy tried to make it go. But the wheels just spun. He needed to remove the mud. <definition>Remove means to take something away.</definition>

"I can help!" said his friend, Lily. She had a big, blue shovel. She started to dig. She dug around the wheels. She helped to remove the yucky mud.

Timmy put the car in gear. Vroom! The car moved a little. Lily dug some more. More mud fell away. Vroom, vroom! The car moved again.

Finally, the car was free! It was no longer stuck in the mud. Timmy and Lily cheered. "Thank you, Lily!" said Timmy. "You are a good friend." Lily smiled.

<question>Where was the little red car stuck?</question>
<answer>The car was stuck in the mud.</answer>

<question>Who tried to make the car go?</question>
<answer>Timmy tried to make the car go.</answer>

<question>What did the wheels do?</question>
<answer>The wheels spun.</answer>

<question>What does remove mean?</question>
<answer>Remove means to take something away.</answer>

<question>Who said they could help?</question>
<answer>Lily said she could help.</answer>

<question>What did Lily have?</question>
<answer>Lily had a big, blue shovel.</answer>

<answer>Lily dug around the wheels.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1610 id=story_qa_079328 =====
The little bird loved his tree. It was big and green. He liked to sit on the branches. One day, a tiny sprout grew on the tree. It looked like a bump.

"What is that?" asked the bird.

"That is a blastemal," said the wise old owl. <definition>Blastemal means a group of cells that can grow into something new.</definition> "It might become a new branch!"

The bird was excited. He watched the blastemal every day. It grew bigger. Soon, it was a small branch. The branch was coextensive with the tree. <definition>Coextensive means the same size and length.</definition> It was part of the tree.

The bird smiled. Now he had a new place to sit. He sang a happy song in his tree. He loved his tree and its new branch.

<question>What did the little bird love?</question>
<answer>The little bird loved his tree.</answer>

<question>What color was the tree?</question>
<answer>The tree was green.</answer>

<question>Where did the bird like to sit?</question>
<answer>He liked to sit on the branches.</answer>

<answer>A tiny sprout grew on the tree.</answer>

<question>What did the sprout look like?</question>
<answer>It looked like a bump.</answer>

<answer>The wise old owl told him.</answer>

<question>What is blastemal?</question>
<answer>Blastemal means a group of cells that can grow into something new.</answer>

<question>What might the blastemal become?</question>
<answer>It might become a new branch.</answer>

<question>Did the bird watch the blastemal?</question>
<answer>Yes, the bird watched the blastemal.</answer>

<question>What did the blastemal become?</question>
<answer>It became a small branch.</answer>

<question>What does coextensive mean?</question>
<answer>Coextensive means the same size and length.</answer>

<question>Was the branch part of the tree?</question>
<answer>Yes, it was part of the tree.</answer>

Pat jumped out of the box. He ran with his mouse to his bed. He curled up for a nap. He was happy as could be with his special toy. He purred and purred. Naps are the best, especially after playing.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1627 id=story_qa_080187 =====
The school clock looked funny. The numbers were all mixed up! It was a nonstandard clock. <definition>Nonstandard means not normal or not like the others.</definition>

"That clock is silly!" said Lily.

"It does not tell the right time," said Tom.

Lily and Tom were best friends. They liked to play games. They knew how to tell time on a normal clock.

Their teacher, Mrs. Green, smiled. "Let's make our own clocks!" she said.

Mrs. Green gave everyone paper plates. She gave them markers too. The children wrote numbers on their clocks. They wrote their names on the back.

Lily made a clock with flowers. Tom made a clock with race cars. Some children made clocks with animals. These were standard clocks.

"Now we all know how to tell time!" said Mrs. Green. "Even if some clocks are nonstandard!"

===== DOC START split=test idx=1629 id=story_qa_080259 =====
A little frog was born by a big, green lily pad. He was a happy frog, but he was not very good at hopping. All the other frogs could hop very high. He wanted to hop high too!

One day, a wise old turtle saw him trying. "Hopping is a process," she said. <definition>Process means a series of steps that help you do something.</definition> "You must practice!"

The turtle showed him how to swing his legs. She showed him how to push off the ground. The little frog tried and tried. He started to hop rhythmically. <definition>Rhythmically means moving with a steady beat.</definition> Hop, hop, hop!

He was not hopping very high yet, but he was getting better. He knew that if he kept practicing, he would hop as high as the other frogs. He was happy he was learning!

<question>Where was the little frog born?</question>
<answer>He was born by a big, green lily pad.</answer>

<answer>The frog was happy.</answer>

<answer>He was not good at hopping.</answer>

<answer>A wise old turtle helped him.</answer>

<answer>Process means a series of steps that help you do something.</answer>

<answer>She told him to practice.</answer>

<question>What did the turtle show the frog?</question>
<answer>She showed him how to swing his legs.</answer>

<question>What does rhythmically mean?</question>
<answer>Rhythmically means moving with a steady beat.</answer>

<answer>No, he was not hopping very high yet.</answer>

The buzzing sound was affecting Patches. <definition>Affecting means having an influence on something.</definition> It made him want to bark. "Woof! Woof!" he barked. The bee flew away.

Patches found a ball in the grass. Playing ball was a big intensifier of fun! <definition>Intensifier means something that makes something else stronger or more.</definition> He grabbed the ball and ran inside. He wanted to play ball with his best friend. They played all day.

The farmer made the pig a pie. <definition>Made means that you created something.</definition> It was a mud pie. The pig loved mud. He dove right in. He ate the whole pie. It was the best day ever. He was very, very full! He smiled a big pig smile.

<answer>Yes, he was still reliable.</answer>

The play was located in the gym. <definition>Located means where something can be found.</definition> Many people came to watch. Leo waited backstage. He felt nervous.

The teacher used a special sword for the play. <definition>Used means something was not new, someone had it before.</definition> It was shiny and gold. Leo held the sword. He took a deep breath.

It was his turn! Leo ran on stage. He fought the dragon. He saved the princess. Everyone clapped. Leo was the best knight ever! He smiled. He liked to act.

Suddenly, he saw something sparkle in the water. It was a shiny rock! He picked it up. Maybe Mama Bear would like a rock better than a fish. He ran home to give her his gift. Mama Bear smiled. "Thank you!" she said. She loved the shiny rock.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1698 id=story_qa_084042 =====
The little bear loved being outside. He liked to play by the big tree. It was his favorite spot. He would look for bugs. Most bugs were small and brown. But one day, he saw a bug that was not brown. It was green and shiny!

"Wow!" said the little bear. He wanted to show his mom. She was in the house. She was baking a pie. He ran inside. "Mom! Mom! Look!"

His mom smiled. "That is a pretty bug," she said. "The other bears will want to see it too."

The little bear ran back outside. But the bug was gone! He looked under the leaves. He looked by the rocks. He could not find it. He was sad.

His mom came outside. "Don't worry," she said. "There are many other bugs to find. Being sad won't help." <definition>Being is another word for feeling.</definition>

The little bear smiled. He knew his mom was right. He would find another bug soon.

The little pig lived in a small house. He liked to play outside. The sun was shining. It was a mild day. <definition>Mild means not too hot and not too cold.</definition> Not too hot, not too cold, just right!

The pig had a red ball. He bounced the ball. He bounced it high. He bounced it low. He bounced it all around.

Suddenly, the ball bounced away. It bounced into the woods. The pig ran after it. He wanted his ball back.

The woods seemed big. The pig had never been this far. He thought, "This is a big world!" <definition>The world is the planet that we live on.</definition> He looked left. He looked right. Where did his ball go?

Then he saw it. The ball was by a tree. A little bunny was pushing it. The pig smiled. He asked, "Can I have my ball back?" The bunny gave him the ball. The pig and the bunny played together all day long.

<answer>The little pig lived in a small house.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1720 id=story_qa_085108 =====
Lily loved to act. She liked to dress up. She would act as a queen. She would act as a cat. One day, Lily wanted to act as a witch.

She put on a black hat. She got a broom. "I will do witchcraft!" she said. <definition>Witchcraft is using magic.</definition> She waved her broom. "Make cookies appear!" she said. No cookies came.

Lily tried again. She waved her broom faster. "Make my toys clean up!" she said. The toys did not move. Lily giggled. "Witchcraft is silly!" she said. It was still fun to act like a witch! She made believe, and that was the best act of all.

<question>What did Lily love to do?</question>
<answer>Lily loved to act.</answer>

<question>What did Lily like to do when she acted?</question>
<answer>She liked to dress up.</answer>

His mom smiled. "That's a great game, Timmy," she said. "Maybe one day, you can play it for real!" Timmy grinned. He knew it would be the best sport ever. He ran to the yard to practice. He was fast! He would be an interstellar tag champion.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1731 id=story_qa_085795 =====
Billy the frog lived in a marsh. <definition>A marsh is a wet and muddy area of land.</definition> He was a happy frog, as happy as could be. One day, Billy saw a bird. The bird was singing a song. Billy wanted to sing too.

Billy tried to sing. But he just croaked. "Croak, croak," he said. That is characteristically what frogs do. <definition>Characteristically means something that is typical of a certain person or thing.</definition> The bird laughed. "Frogs don't sing!" she said.

Billy felt sad. He wanted to sing like one of the birds. He hopped away. He would find a way to sing! He knew it. He just needed to practice as much as he could. Maybe he could sing after all.

Timmy was Patches' owner. <definition>An owner is someone who has something.</definition> He took good care of Patches. One day, Timmy looked at Patches' posterior. <definition>Posterior means the back part of something.</definition> "Your posterior is dirty!" Timmy said. He decided to give Patches a bath.

Timmy filled a small dish with water. He put Patches in the dish. Patches did not move. Timmy thought, "Maybe Patches has schizophrenia!" <definition>Schizophrenia is when someone's brain makes it hard to know what is real and what is not, but snails cannot get schizophrenia.</definition> That would be silly. Snails cannot have that. Timmy gently cleaned Patches with a soft brush. Now Patches was clean. Patches peeked out of his shell. He smiled. Timmy smiled too. Patches was happy to be clean. He started to slide around in the water.

"Wow!" Timmy said.

"Let's have a race!" said Timmy.

"Okay!" said Lily.

They lined up their cars. "Ready, set, GO!"

Timmy's car zoomed ahead. Lily's car was right behind. They raced around the bed. Timmy's car won!

"I won!" said Timmy. "My car is the best!"

"Let's race again," said Lily.

They raced again and again. Lily won some races. Timmy won some races.

"Let's rank our cars," said Lily. <definition>Rank means to put things in order from best to worst.</definition>

Timmy and Lily decided their cars were both in the number one rank. They were both the best! They played together all day.

Pip looked at the cookie jar. It was up high. He got a chair. He climbed up, up, up! He almost fell. He reached for the jar. He got it! The jar was heavy. He took one cookie. Then he took two. He ate them fast. Mom came in. "Pip! What are you doing at the cookie jar?" Pip smiled. "Eating cookies!"

The animals in the garden had a meeting. They called it the animal parliament. <definition>Parliament is like a group of leaders who make rules.</definition> The wise old owl was in charge. The owl said, "We must help Lily with her plant!"

The ants carried tiny bits of food to the plant. The bees brought sweet honey. Soon, the plant grew big and strong. Lily was very happy. She thanked the animal parliament for their help.

But the little train was not very fast. <definition>Not means the opposite of is.</definition> The other trains laughed. "You will not win!" they said. The little train felt sad.

The day of the race came. The fast trains zoomed ahead. The little train went slow. But the little train did not give up. Choo! Choo! It kept going.

Suddenly, the fast trains crashed! The little train went around them. It crossed the finish line! The little train won the thousandth race. Everyone cheered.

Then she used the blue pencil. She liked the colors. The end of her composition was good. She drew a picture of the dog. The dog was in his little house.

But one of the new cells felt bad. He felt a lot of hatred. <definition>Hatred is a very strong feeling of not liking someone or something.</definition> He did not like the other cell. "You are too round!" he said. The other cell felt sad. Then, the cell with hatred decided to be nice. "Let's play!" he said. The two cells were friends.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1781 id=story_qa_088344 =====
The dog was sad. He did not have a toy. He looked all around the house. He looked under the bed. He looked behind the couch. Still, he did not see a toy.

"I am as bored as can be," he thought.

The cat walked by. She had a toy mouse. The dog asked, "Can I play?"

The cat said, "No. This is my toy."

The dog was sad. He went outside. He saw a bird. The bird was teaching its baby how to fly. The bird told the baby bird, “Flapping is very important. That is our doctrine.” <definition>Doctrine means a set of important rules or beliefs.</definition>

The dog did not know about doctrine. He just wanted to play. He was not happy. He needed a toy. Maybe he could find a stick. Sticks are good toys, too. He wagged his tail as he looked for a stick.

<answer>He did not have a toy.</answer>

<answer>No, the cat did not let him play.</answer>

<answer>The bird said flapping was very important.</answer>

<question>What does doctrine mean?</question>
<answer>Doctrine means a set of important rules or beliefs.</answer>

<question>What did the dog want to find outside?</question>
<answer>The dog wanted to find a stick.</answer>

<question>What did the dog do when he looked for a stick?</question>

One day, Pat had an idea. He would write his own letters! He found some paper. He found a crayon. He wrote lots of letters. They were not real words. They were just lines.

Pat put the letters in a pile. He gave them to his mom. “Can you mail these?” he asked.

Mom smiled. “Of course!” she said. She took the letters to the post office.

Pat also learned about organs. <definition>Organs are parts inside your body, like your heart.</definition> His mom told him his heart was important. It helped him play fetch! Pat decided to write a thank you letter to his heart. He was very grateful for all his organs.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1787 id=story_qa_088578 =====
The little blue car liked to go fast. He lived in a town by a big hill. He wanted to win the race! The race was in a different region. <definition>A region is a large area of land.</definition> It was far away. He had to drive on the big road.

"Beep beep!" he said to his friend, the red truck. "I am going to win!"

The red truck smiled. "Good luck!"

The little blue car drove and drove. He saw cows and sheep. He saw green trees. At last, he got to the region where the race was. Many cars were there. They were big and shiny. The little blue car felt small.

The race began! The cars zoomed by. The little blue car went fast. He went on the dirt track. He tried his best. He did not win, but he had fun! He drove back home by the big hill. He was happy.

<question>What color was the car?</question>
<answer>The car was blue.</answer>

<question>What did the little blue car like to do?</question>
<answer>He liked to go fast.</answer>

<question>Where did he live?</question>
<answer>He lived in a town by a big hill.</answer>

<question>What did he want to win?</question>
<answer>He wanted to win the race.</answer>

But Timmy did not have a scooter. He looked sad as he walked home. "I need someone to help me," he said. <definition>Someone means a person.</definition>

He saw his grandma. "Grandma, I want to race!" he said. "But I don't have a scooter."

Lily waited and waited. She wanted her clock back. She missed the tick-tock. She missed her father. She wanted him to come back.

After a long time, her father came back! He gave Lily the clock. "I fixed it!" he said. The clock did not tick-tock. It played music! Lily was so happy. "It's magic!" she said. The clock had become a music box. <definition>Become means to start to be something else.</definition> Lily hugged her father.

After they found the tree, they built a cozy new nest. It was high up. The bird was happy. The cat could not get him now! He gave the squirrel a big hug as compensation.

Benny felt better soon. He learned not to eat too many carrots.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1802 id=story_qa_089335 =====
The little cloud was round. It was a funny shape. One day, the cloud floated far away. It floated to the southeastern part of the sky. <definition>Southeastern is the direction that is both south and east.</definition>

“Hello?” someone called. It was a little bird. The bird had not spoken to a cloud before. <definition>Spoken means to have talked.</definition>

"Hello," said the cloud.

“What shape are you?” asked the bird.

"I am round," said the cloud. "But I can be any shape I want!"

The cloud changed its shape. It was not round anymore. Now it looked like a heart. The bird smiled. "That is a nice shape," the bird said. The cloud was happy.

<question>What shape was the little cloud at first?</question>
<answer>The little cloud was round.</answer>

<question>Where did the cloud float?</question>
<answer>It floated far away.</answer>

<question>What part of the sky did it float to?</question>
<answer>It floated to the southeastern part of the sky.</answer>

<question>Who called to the cloud?</question>
<answer>A little bird called to the cloud.</answer>

<question>Had the bird spoken to a cloud before?</question>
<answer>No, the bird had not spoken to a cloud before.</answer>

<question>What does spoken mean?</question>
<answer>Spoken means to have talked.</answer>

<question>What did the bird ask the cloud?</question>
<answer>The bird asked what shape the cloud was.</answer>

<question>What did the cloud say it could be?</question>
<answer>The cloud said it could be any shape it wanted.</answer>

<question>What shape did the cloud change to?</question>
<answer>The cloud changed to a heart.</answer>

<question>Did the bird like the new shape?</question>
<answer>Yes, the bird liked the new shape.</answer>

<question>How did the cloud feel at the end?</question>
<answer>The cloud was happy.</answer>

<question>What does southeastern mean?</question>
<answer>Southeastern is the direction that is both south and east.</answer>

"Those were my original blocks!" <definition>Original means the first one.</definition> The yellow robot felt bad. He helped put the blocks back. He put them back by color too. Now they both had a color system.

The cat was in a state of inactivity. <definition>Inactivity means not moving or doing anything.</definition> He was just sleeping! He slept by the warm fire. He did not chase mice. He did not play with toys. He just slept.

A little mouse ran by. The cat did not wake up. The mouse wiggled his nose. He ran closer to the cat. Still, the cat did not wake up.

The mouse jumped on the cat. The cat opened one eye. He looked at the mouse. The cat yawned. Then, he closed his eye again. More inactivity!

The mouse giggled. He jumped off the cat. He ran away to find some cheese. The cat started to purr. It was a good nap.

<question>What was the cat doing?</question>
<answer>The cat was sleeping.</answer>

<question>What does inactivity mean?</question>
<answer>Inactivity means not moving or doing anything.</answer>

<answer>No, the cat did not chase mice.</answer>

The pig lived on a farm. The farm had lots of livestock. <definition>Livestock are animals like cows, pigs, and chickens.</definition> The pig loved his life! He felt a lot of happiness. <definition>Happiness is a feeling of being happy and joyful.</definition>

One day, the farmer put the pig in a small cell. <definition>A cell is a tiny room.</definition> The pig was sad. He wanted to be with the other animals. He wanted to play.

The pig thought about the sun. The sun was constant. <definition>Constant means that it always happens.</definition> Every day the sun came up. This made him a little happy. He knew he would see his friends again. He just had to wait. His happiness would return soon. He just had to be patient.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1825 id=story_qa_090452 =====
The little fish, Finny, loved to swim. He swam in the clear blue water. He liked to play with his friends. One day, Finny felt very tired. He had a bad case of debilitation. <definition>Debilitation means being very weak and not having any energy.</definition> He could not swim fast. He could not play tag.

An old turtle saw Finny. "What is wrong?" she asked. Finny said, "I feel weak". The turtle smiled. "I have a good memory," she said. <definition>Memory is when you can remember something from the past.</definition> "I remember a special plant. It grows near the waterfall. It will make you strong again."

Finny swam to the waterfall. He found the plant. He ate it all up. Soon, Finny felt much better. He was not tired anymore. He swam fast and played with his friends.

<answer>Finny liked to swim.</answer>

<answer>The water was clear blue.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1838 id=story_qa_091038 =====
Timmy had a pet frog. His frog lived in a small tank. One day, Timmy gave his frog too many bugs. The frog ate them all up. Uh oh!

Soon, the frog started feeling sick. He felt very, very bad. Then, the frog started vomiting. <definition>Vomiting means throwing up food you ate.</definition> Oh no!

Timmy felt bad for his frog. He cleaned the tank. He gave the frog fresh water. The frog sat very still. Timmy petted the frog.

"I am sorry," Timmy said. "I gave you too much food."

The next day, the frog felt better. He hopped around in his tank. Timmy smiled. He gave the frog just a few bugs. The frog ate them slowly. This time, he did not get sick. Timmy and the frog were happy again.

<answer>Timmy had a pet frog.</answer>

<answer>Timmy gave the frog too many bugs.</answer>

<answer>The frog felt sick.</answer>

<answer>Vomiting means throwing up food you ate.</answer>

<answer>Timmy cleaned the tank and gave the frog fresh water.</answer>

<answer>Timmy said he was sorry.</answer>

<answer>The frog felt better.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1842 id=story_qa_091295 =====
The ant was small. He lived in a big hill. Many ants lived there too. Ants commonly carry food. <definition>Commonly means that it happens often.</definition> This ant found a crumb. It was bigger than his head!

He tried to pull it. He pulled and pulled. It would not move. Then he saw something. It was a spider web! The web had threadlike strands. <definition>Threadlike means very thin, like a piece of thread.</definition>

The ant had an idea. He used the threadlike web. He tied it to the crumb. Now he could pull it! He pulled the crumb all the way home. He was so happy to bring the food in.

<question>Was the ant big or small?</question>
<answer>The ant was small.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1857 id=story_qa_091496 =====
The fish lived in a big blue sea. He loved to swim all day. He saw many things. One day, he saw something new. It was very colorful! It had red, yellow, and green parts. The fish swam closer to look.

The colorful thing was a toy boat. It floated on top of the water. The current pushed the boat along. <definition>Current means the way the water is moving.</definition> The fish followed the boat. He liked the boat very much.

Far away, there was a big building. This building was part of the toy industry. <definition>Industry means a group of businesses that make things.</definition> People made toy boats in the industry. They made boats just like the one the fish saw. The fish and the boat were now best friends. They played in the sea every day.

<question>Where did the fish live?</question>
<answer>The fish lived in a big blue sea.</answer>

<question>What did the fish love to do?</question>
<answer>He loved to swim all day.</answer>

<question>What did the fish see one day?</question>
<answer>He saw something new and colorful.</answer>

<question>What colors were on the new thing?</question>
<answer>It had red, yellow, and green parts.</answer>

<question>What was the colorful thing?</question>
<answer>It was a toy boat.</answer>

<question>Where did the boat float?</question>
<answer>It floated on top of the water.</answer>

<question>What pushed the boat along?</question>
<answer>The current pushed the boat along.</answer>

<question>What does current mean?</question>
<answer>Current means the way the water is moving.</answer>

<question>Did the fish follow the boat?</question>
<answer>Yes, the fish followed the boat.</answer>

<question>Where were the toy boats made?</question>
<answer>They were made in a big building far away.</answer>

<question>What does industry mean?</question>
<answer>Industry means a group of businesses that make things.</answer>

<question>Who were best friends at the end?</question>
<answer>The fish and the boat were best friends.</answer>

<question>Where did they play every day?</question>
<answer>They played in the sea every day.</answer>
===== DOC END =====

The farmer saw the pig. He laughed. The pig was covered in mud. "You are a silly pig," he said. He gave the pig a carrot. The pig ate the carrot in one bite. <definition>Bite means to use your teeth to cut something.</definition> The pig loved carrots. The pig was happy in his pen.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1866 id=story_qa_092239 =====
The little bird was sad. He could not find any berries. He looked up high. He looked down low. No berries!

"Chirp, chirp," he cried.

A big bird landed next to him. "Why are you so sad?" she asked.

"I cannot find any food," he said.

The big bird smiled. "I am a capitonidae," she said. <definition>Capitonidae is a type of bird that likes to eat fruit.</definition> "I know where all the best berries are!"

She took him to a bush. It was full of juicy, red berries. The little bird ate and ate. He was happy.

"Thank you," he said. "I am deeply grateful." <definition>Deeply means very, very much.</definition>

The capitonidae smiled. "You are welcome," she said. "Come back any time!"

Her brother, Tom, laughed. "It's just a rock. Rocks do not matter." <definition>Matter means to be important.</definition>

Lily frowned. "It does matter! This rock is from the moon!"

Tom laughed again. "No, it isn't!"

Lily held her rock tight. She knew her rock was special. Maybe it wasn't from the moon. But it was special to her, and that did matter. She put her rock in her pocket. She smiled.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1873 id=story_qa_092642 =====
The sun was a bright light. It peeked in my window. I jumped out of bed. Today was a fun day! My class was learning about new rules. <definition>Legislation is a big word for rules that the government makes.</definition> Our teacher said some things happen outside of our control. <definition>Exogenous means that something is caused by things that happen outside of something, like outside of our class.</definition> An exogenous event was pizza for lunch! It was not planned, but it was yummy! My friend had a light up watch. It blinked in the dark. We played games all day in the sun. It was the best day ever!

<question>What was bright?</question>
<answer>The sun was bright.</answer>

<question>Where did the sun peek?</question>
<answer>It peeked in my window.</answer>

<question>What was today?</question>
<answer>Today was a fun day.</answer>

<question>What was the class learning about?</question>
<answer>The class was learning about new rules.</answer>

<question>What is legislation?</question>
<answer>Legislation is rules that the government makes.</answer>

The seed started to grow. It grew a root down. It grew a stem up. Soon, it was a tiny plant. Other seeds grew next to it. They all wanted to become big and strong. <definition>Become means to start to be something.</definition>

The plants helped each other. They shared the sun. They shared the water. They were united. <definition>United means working together as one.</definition> They made a beautiful garden.

A girl named Lily saw the garden. "This is the best garden ever!" she said. Lily was an American. <definition>American means someone who lives in America.</definition> She loved to see things grow. She watered the plants every day. The plants grew even bigger. They were happy to be in Lily's garden. The garden was a happy place for everyone.

One big truck said, "That was offensive!" <definition>Offensive means causing someone to feel hurt or upset.</definition> The truck did not like the loud beep.

The little blue car was sad. He did not want to be offensive. He just wanted to be fast. He needed to be careful. He drove away to find some smaller cars to race.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1887 id=story_qa_093301 =====
Timmy was a small snail. He lived in a garden. The garden had a system. <definition>A system is a way of doing things.</definition> All the bugs had jobs. The ants carried crumbs. The bees made honey. Timmy was in charge of shiny rocks. He had to decorate the flower pots.

One day, Timmy saw a cookie crumb. It was big! "I want that crumb," he thought. He ate it. It was yummy. But it was not his job. He left his shiny rocks.

The flower pots looked sad. They were not sparkly. The other bugs were mad. Timmy felt bad. He knew he did wrong. But no one saw him. He thought he would go unpunished. <definition>Unpunished means not getting in trouble for doing something bad.</definition>

The next day, Timmy wore his uniform. <definition>A uniform is a special set of clothes.</definition> He went back to work. He put shiny rocks on every pot. He even found extra shiny ones. The garden looked great again. Timmy learned a lesson. Doing your job is important, even if no one is watching!

<question>Who was Timmy?</question>
<answer>Timmy was a small snail.</answer>

<question>Where did Timmy live?</question>
<answer>Timmy lived in a garden.</answer>

<answer>A system is a way of doing things.</answer>

<answer>The ants carried crumbs.</answer>

<answer>The bees made honey.</answer>

The big day was here! Something fun was happening at school. <definition>Happening means something is going on.</definition> It was Pet Day!

Lily brought her cat, Patches. Tom brought his dog, Buster. There were many animals. <definition>Many means there are a lot of something.</definition>

Mrs. Davis asked, "Who wants to show their pet first?"

Lily raised her hand. "Me!"

Mrs. Davis smiled. "Okay, Lily."

Lily held up Patches. "Patches can do tricks!"

Mrs. Davis asked, "Patches, can you sit?"

Lily looked at Patches. Patches looked at Lily. Patches did not sit.

"Maybe later," Lily said. "Does everyone assent to me showing Patches later?" <definition>Assent means to agree to something.</definition>

Everyone nodded their heads. Pet Day was so fun!

===== DOC START split=test idx=1889 id=story_qa_093619 =====
The little boat was red. It was very small. Lily wanted to sail it. But the boat was resistant. <definition>Resistant means not wanting to do something.</definition> It did not want to go in the water. Lily pulled it twice. Still, the boat stayed on the grass. Lily was certain she could make it float. <definition>Certain means you know something is true.</definition> She got a bucket. She filled it with water. She poured the water on the boat. The boat began to float! Lily cheered. Now the boat was happy. It floated on the water all day.

<answer>The boat was red.</answer>

<answer>The boat was small.</answer>

<answer>Lily wanted to sail the boat.</answer>

<answer>Resistant means not wanting to do something.</answer>

<answer>No, the boat did not want to go in the water.</answer>

<answer>The boat stayed on the grass.</answer>

<question>What does certain mean?</question>
<answer>Certain means you know something is true.</answer>

<answer>Lily put water in the bucket.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1890 id=story_qa_093626 =====
The class was learning about shapes. They learned about a circle. They learned about a square. Miss Lee asked, "Can you draw a star?"

Timmy raised his hand. He said, "I can make a star in any form!" <definition>Form means the shape or way something is.</definition>

Miss Lee smiled. "Show us, Timmy!"

Timmy took out his crayons. He drew a big yellow star. Then, he drew a star made of smaller stars. He even made a star out of dots!

Suddenly, the principal came in. He gave a little sermon. <definition>A sermon is like a short speech.</definition> He said, "Be kind to each other."

Timmy drew a star with the words "Be Kind" in it. Everyone clapped. Timmy was a star!

<question>What was the class learning about?</question>
<answer>The class was learning about shapes.</answer>

<question>What shapes did they learn about?</question>
<answer>They learned about a circle and a square.</answer>

<answer>Miss Lee asked them to draw a star.</answer>

<answer>Timmy said he could make a star.</answer>

Mom Bear looked at the ball. "That is a ball for playing!" she said. "You can bounce it."

The frog lived in a big green swamp. He had a little boat. It was a flatbottom boat. <definition>Flatbottom means the bottom of the boat is flat, not rounded.</definition> He liked to sail by the lily pads. One day, he saw something strange. It was spreading on the water. <definition>Spreading means to move out and cover a bigger area.</definition> It looked like green paint.

"What is that?" asked the frog. A little fish swam by. "That is algae," said the fish. "It is not good." The frog did not like the algae spreading in his swamp. He got out his net. He started to scoop up the algae. He worked and worked all day. He got most of it! Now he could sail his flatbottom boat again. He was happy.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1905 id=story_qa_094499 =====
The big red bus goes by. A little bug sits on the bus. The bug says, "I want to fly!" <definition>To fly means to move through the air using wings.</definition>

The bus drives past a park. The bug jumps off. He opens his little wings. He tries to fly up high. But he falls in the grass.

A bird sees the bug. The bird flies down. "Hello," says the bird. "Can you teach me to fly?" asks the bug.

The bird laughs. "You already have wings! Just flap them hard."

The bug flaps his wings fast. He goes up, up, up! He flies over the trees. He flies by the pond. Flying is so much fun! Now the bug flies all day long.

<question>What color is the bus?</question>
<answer>The bus is red.</answer>

<question>Who sits on the bus?</question>
<answer>A little bug sits on the bus.</answer>

<question>What does the bug want to do?</question>
<answer>The bug wants to fly.</answer>

<question>What does fly mean?</question>
<answer>To fly means to move through the air using wings.</answer>

<question>Where does the bus drive past?</question>
<answer>The bus drives past a park.</answer>

<question>What happens when the bug tries to fly?</question>
<answer>He falls in the grass.</answer>

<question>Who sees the bug?</question>
<answer>A bird sees the bug.</answer>

<question>What does the bug ask the bird?</question>
<answer>The bug asks the bird to teach him to fly.</answer>

<question>What does the bird tell the bug to do?</question>
<answer>The bird tells him to flap his wings hard.</answer>

<question>What happens when the bug flaps his wings fast?</question>
<answer>He goes up, up, up.</answer>

<question>Where does the bug fly?</question>
<answer>He flies over the trees and by the pond.</answer>

<question>Does the bug like flying?</question>
<answer>Yes, the bug likes flying.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1919 id=story_qa_095192 =====
Lily loved bugs. She had a big interest in them. <definition>Interest means you like something and want to learn more about it.</definition> She kept them in jars. One day, she found a strange bug. It glowed!

"Wow!" said Lily. "This bug is so cool."

She showed it to her dad. "What kind of bug is this?" she asked.

Her dad looked at it closely. "Hmm, it looks like a firefly, but different. It might be radiosensitive." <definition>Radiosensitive means something is easily changed by light or energy.</definition>

"Radiosensitive?" asked Lily.

"Yes! It means light can change it," said Dad. "We should keep it in the dark."

Lily put the glowing bug in a dark box. The bug stopped glowing so bright. Lily still liked it very much. She gave it a leaf. "Goodnight, little bug," she said. She knew she would learn more about it tomorrow.

<question>What did Lily love?</question>
<answer>Lily loved bugs.</answer>

<question>What does interest mean?</question>
<answer>Interest means you like something and want to learn more about it.</answer>

<question>Where did Lily keep the bugs?</question>
<answer>She kept them in jars.</answer>

<question>What did Lily find one day?</question>
<answer>She found a strange bug.</answer>

<answer>It glowed.</answer>

<question>Who did Lily show the bug to?</question>
<answer>She showed it to her dad.</answer>

<question>What kind of bug did her dad think it was?</question>
<answer>He thought it was a firefly, but different.</answer>

<question>What does radiosensitive mean?</question>
<answer>Radiosensitive means something is easily changed by light or energy.</answer>

<question>What did Lily's dad say light could do?</question>
<answer>He said light can change it.</answer>

<question>Where did Lily put the bug?</question>

The sea is an aquatic place. <definition>Aquatic means having to do with water.</definition> The fish swam all day. He used his fins to go fast. He liked being a fish. <definition>Being means existing or living.</definition> It was very fun to swim. <definition>Very means a lot.</definition> He liked to play with his friends. One day, he saw a shiny shell. He swam to it. He used his nose to push it. The shell was very heavy. He pushed it to his home. "This will be my new toy!" said the fish. He was very happy. He loved being a fish in the sea.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1925 id=story_qa_095504 =====

Patches batted the yarn with her paws. She chased it all around the house. The yarn went under the table. It went over the chair.

Several mice watched Patches play. They thought it looked like fun. They wanted to play too!

But the mice knew playing with Patches could be tricky. She was sometimes a little... well, bossy.

The mice decided to have a judicial meeting. <definition>Judicial means like a judge, making a fair decision.</definition> They talked and talked. They decided to ask Patches nicely if they could play too.

Patches was happy to share. Now, everyone played with the yarn. It was more fun with friends!
