The school was newly incorporated. <definition>Incorporated means that it is a new building.</definition> It was so new, even strange fish came to see it!

Everyone forgot about the vote. They all looked at the regalecidae. It was longer than two classrooms! Wow!

Timmy felt better soon. He learned not to eat too many berries. Now, Timmy only eats a few berries at a time. He is a happy turtle. He still likes berries, but not too many!

Her brother saw the drawing. He was being disparaging. <definition>Disparaging means saying mean things.</definition> He said, "That is not good!"

Lila felt sad. She started revising her drawing. <definition>Revising means changing something to make it better.</definition> She added stars. She added a rainbow. Now the goddess looked even better. Lila smiled. Her brother was wrong!

One day, Pat had a big idea. A big idea is called a concept. <definition>A concept is an idea or a thought.</definition> His concept was to dig to China. He started to dig a hole in the yard. The hole got bigger and bigger.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1958 id=story_qa_097563 =====
Lily loved her cat, Patches. Patches liked to sleep. He slept in his bed. He slept on the rug. Sometimes, he slept on Lily!

One day, it was cold outside. It was winter time. <definition>Time is what we use to measure minutes, hours, and days.</definition> Lily wanted to play a game. But Patches was sleepy. He curled up by the fireplace. <definition>A fireplace is a special place in a house where you can make a fire to keep warm.</definition>

Lily tried to wake him. "Patches, play with me!" she said. Patches did not move. He just purred. Lily sighed. She knew Patches loved to sleep.

Lily decided to read a book. She sat next to Patches in front of the fireplace. The fire was warm. Soon, Lily felt sleepy too. She yawned. She closed her book.

Sometimes, it is nice to just be quiet. Lily and Patches slept by the fire. They were warm and cozy. It was a good time for a nap.

<question>What was the cat's name?</question>
<answer>The cat's name was Patches.</answer>

<answer>Patches liked to sleep.</answer>

<answer>He slept in his bed and on the rug.</answer>

<answer>Lily loved Patches.</answer>

<answer>Time is what we use to measure minutes, hours, and days.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1975 id=story_qa_098330 =====
The little bear loved honey. He loved it very much. But he loved honey from the big tree especially. <definition>Especially means more than other things.</definition> The big tree was in the woods. The woods were dark and green. One sunny day, the bear went to the woods. He wanted honey! He looked up at the tree. The honey was high up in the tree. Too high for the bear to reach! He saw a squirrel. The squirrel was his friend. "Can you help me?" asked the bear. The squirrel said, "Yes!" The squirrel climbed up, up, up. He got the honey. He gave it to the bear. The bear was so happy. He ate all the honey. It was the best honey ever!

<question>What did the little bear love?</question>
<answer>The little bear loved honey.</answer>

<question>Which honey did he love especially?</question>
<answer>He loved honey from the big tree.</answer>

<question>Where was the big tree?</question>
<answer>The big tree was in the woods.</answer>

<question>What were the woods like?</question>
<answer>The woods were dark and green.</answer>

<question>What does especially mean?</question>
<answer>Especially means more than other things.</answer>

<question>What did the bear want one sunny day?</question>
<answer>He wanted honey.</answer>

<question>Where was the honey in the tree?</question>
<answer>The honey was high up in the tree.</answer>

<question>Could the bear reach the honey?</question>
<answer>No, the bear could not reach the honey.</answer>

<question>Who did the bear see?</question>
<answer>The bear saw a squirrel.</answer>

<question>Was the squirrel the bear's friend?</question>
<answer>Yes, the squirrel was his friend.</answer>

<question>Did the squirrel help the bear?</question>
<answer>Yes, the squirrel helped the bear.</answer>

<question>What did the squirrel do?</question>
<answer>The squirrel climbed up the tree.</answer>

<question>What did the squirrel give the bear?</question>
<answer>The squirrel gave him honey.</answer>

<question>How did the bear feel?</question>
<answer>The bear was so happy.</answer>

<question>Did the bear eat the honey?</question>
<answer>Yes, the bear ate all the honey.</answer>

Then, Timmy saw a ladybug. The ladybug had a flower! Timmy asked, "Can I have it?" The ladybug said, "Yes!" Timmy was so happy. Now he had a flower.

===== DOC START split=test idx=1981 id=story_qa_098926 =====
The whale was big. It was a physeteridae. <definition>Physeteridae is another name for a sperm whale. It is a very large animal that lives in the ocean.</definition> The whale liked to swim. It swam all day.

A little fish saw the whale. The fish wanted to play. "Can I play with you?" asked the fish.

The whale said, "I have to work." <definition>Work means to do something that takes effort.</definition> "I need to find food."

The fish said, "I can help!" The fish and the whale looked for food together. They found lots of yummy squid. It was fun to work together.

<question>Was the whale big or small?</question>
<answer>The whale was big.</answer>

<question>What kind of whale was it?</question>
<answer>It was a physeteridae.</answer>

<question>What is another name for physeteridae?</question>
<answer>Another name for physeteridae is sperm whale.</answer>

<question>What did the whale like to do?</question>
<answer>The whale liked to swim.</answer>

<question>Who saw the whale?</question>
<answer>A little fish saw the whale.</answer>

<question>What did the fish want to do?</question>
<answer>The fish wanted to play.</answer>

<question>What did the whale say it had to do?</question>
<answer>The whale said it had to work.</answer>

<question>What does work mean?</question>
<answer>Work means to do something that takes effort.</answer>

<question>What did the whale need to find?</question>
<answer>The whale needed to find food.</answer>

<question>Who helped the whale find food?</question>
<answer>The fish helped the whale find food.</answer>

<question>What kind of food did they find?</question>
<answer>They found lots of yummy squid.</answer>

<question>Was it fun to work together?</question>
<answer>Yes, it was fun to work together.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1982 id=story_qa_098841 =====
The little blue train loved its job. It pulled carts full of toys. It went up the hill. Then it went down. This was part of a long series of hills. <definition>Series means many things that come one after another.</definition>

One day, the train felt funny. It puffed and puffed. It tried to go faster, but it could not. "My lung hurts!" it said. <definition>Your lung is part of your body that helps you breathe.</definition>

A small bear ran up. "What's wrong?" he asked.

"I can't pull the toys," said the train. "I am showing everyone how slow I am!" <definition>Showing is when you let someone see something.</definition>

The bear smiled. "I can help!"

The bear pushed the train. Other animals helped too. Soon, the train was going fast again. It felt much better.

<question>What did the little blue train love to do?</question>
<answer>The train loved its job.</answer>

<question>What did the train pull?</question>
<answer>It pulled carts full of toys.</answer>

<question>What did the train do on the hills?</question>
<answer>It went up and down the hills.</answer>

<question>What does series mean?</question>
<answer>Series means many things that come one after another.</answer>

<question>How did the train feel one day?</question>
<answer>The train felt funny.</answer>

<question>What did the train say hurt?</question>
<answer>It said its lung hurt.</answer>

<question>What does lung mean?</question>
<answer>Your lung is part of your body that helps you breathe.</answer>

<question>Who ran up to the train?</question>
<answer>A small bear ran up.</answer>

<question>What did the bear ask?</question>
<answer>He asked what was wrong.</answer>

<question>What was the train showing everyone?</question>
<answer>The train was showing everyone how slow it was.</answer>

<question>What does showing mean?</question>
<answer>Showing is when you let someone see something.</answer>

<question>Who helped the train?</question>
<answer>The bear and other animals helped.</answer>

<question>How did the train feel at the end?</question>
<answer>It felt much better.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1983 id=story_qa_098966 =====
The little bugs wanted to play. They made a group. <definition>A group is when you have many things or people together.</definition> "Let's play hide and seek!" said Dot. The bugs all cheered.

Zoom the beetle went to hide first. He went behind a big leaf. He was easy to see.

Next, Pat the ant went to hide. She went in a flower. <definition>In means to be inside of something.</definition> It was a red flower. Pat was red too, so no one could see her!

Then, Skip the grasshopper went to hide. He jumped in a pile of sand. He was green, and the sand was yellow. Everyone could see him!

The bugs laughed and laughed. Hiding was hard! They decided to play tag instead. It was more fun to run in their group. They ran and ran until it was time for lunch.

<question>What did the little bugs want to do?</question>
<answer>The little bugs wanted to play.</answer>

<answer>Dot wanted to play hide and seek.</answer>

<answer>A group is when you have many things or people together.</answer>

<answer>Zoom the beetle hid behind a big leaf.</answer>

<question>Was Zoom easy to see?</question>
<answer>Yes, Zoom was easy to see.</answer>

<answer>Pat the ant hid in a flower.</answer>

<question>What color was the flower?</question>
<answer>The flower was red.</answer>

<question>What does in mean?</question>
<answer>In means to be inside of something.</answer>

<question>Could anyone see Pat in the flower?</question>
<answer>No, no one could see Pat.</answer>

<question>Where did Skip the grasshopper hide?</question>
<answer>Skip hid in a pile of sand.</answer>

<answer>The sand was yellow.</answer>

<answer>Yes, everyone could see Skip.</answer>

Timmy felt bad. He gave the plant some water. He said, "I am sorry, little plant." The plant seemed happy. Timmy learned to be nice to everyone, even plants!

===== DOC START split=test idx=1990 id=story_qa_099258 =====
Lily loved to draw. She drew cats. She drew dogs. She drew funny bugs. One day, her school made a new rule. It was a new policy. <definition>A policy is like a rule that everyone has to follow.</definition> The policy said everyone had to draw flowers.

Lily did not like to draw flowers. She liked bugs! But she had to follow the policy. So, she drew a flower. Then, she drew a bug ON the flower.

Her teacher smiled. "That is a good composition," she said. <definition>A composition is how all the parts of something, like a picture, are arranged.</definition> Lily smiled too. She made a flower, and a bug! The policy was not so bad after all. She drew more flowers with bugs on them. Now she liked drawing flowers a little bit.

<question>What did Lily love to do?</question>
<answer>Lily loved to draw.</answer>

<question>What animals did Lily draw?</question>
<answer>She drew cats and dogs.</answer>

<question>What else did Lily draw?</question>
<answer>She drew funny bugs.</answer>

<question>What new rule did the school make?</question>
<answer>Everyone had to draw flowers.</answer>

<question>What is a policy?</question>
<answer>A policy is like a rule that everyone has to follow.</answer>

<question>Did Lily like to draw flowers?</question>
<answer>No, Lily did not like to draw flowers.</answer>

<question>What did Lily draw on the flower?</question>
<answer>She drew a bug on the flower.</answer>

<question>What did her teacher say?</question>
<answer>Her teacher said it was a good composition.</answer>

<question>What is a composition?</question>
<answer>A composition is how all the parts of something are arranged.</answer>

<question>Did Lily like the policy after she drew the bug?</question>
<answer>Yes, she liked the policy a little bit.</answer>

<question>What did Lily draw more of?</question>
<answer>She drew more flowers with bugs on them.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=1991 id=story_qa_099265 =====
The little bug was in a big room. The room had a green plant. It was a very long plant. The bug wanted to climb the plant. He used his whole body to climb. <definition>Your body is all of you!</definition> Up, up, up he went. It took a long time. He got to the top. "Wow!" he said. He could see everything from up there. He saw a cat. He saw a dog. He saw a bird. He was happy he climbed the long plant. He loved being in the room.

<question>Where was the little bug?</question>
<answer>The little bug was in a big room.</answer>

<question>What was in the room?</question>
<answer>There was a green plant in the room.</answer>

<question>Was the plant short or long?</question>
<answer>The plant was long.</answer>

<question>What did the bug want to do?</question>

Timmy had a toy robot. The robot was named Bolt. Bolt needed batteries to work. It was part of his power system. <definition>A system is a set of things working together as a whole.</definition> Timmy put the batteries in. Bolt woke up!

"Beep boop!" said Bolt. "I want to play."

Timmy smiled. "Let's play catch!"

Timmy threw the ball. Bolt tried to catch it. But he missed. The ball bounced away.

"Oh no!" said Timmy.

Bolt felt sad. He wanted to be good at catch. He asked, "Can I try again?"

Timmy said, "Of course!"

Bolt tried again. This time, he caught the ball! Timmy cheered. Bolt felt happy. He was accepted. <definition>Accepted means to be liked and included.</definition> He was part of Timmy's team! Now they could play all day.

Then, he heard a noise. It was his mom! She had come to find him. The little bear jumped up. He ran to his mom. He was not feeling sad anymore. He was feeling happy! Being with his mom was the best feeling ever. They walked home together, hand in paw.

Lily went to the swings. She swung up high. She could see all the people. She saw her friend Tom. "Tom, over here!" she yelled. Tom ran to her. They played all day by the lake. It was a fun day for any kid at the park.

===== DOC START split=test idx=2026 id=story_qa_100897 =====
Timmy was a member of the Cub Scouts. <definition>A member is someone who belongs to a group.</definition> He loved being in the scouts. They did fun things! One day, the scouts went on a hike in the woods. Timmy looked for cool bugs. He looked under rocks. He looked up in the trees.

Suddenly, Timmy found something shiny. It was a small, metal star. An adult scout leader walked over. "That's cool! That looks like an American flag pin!" he said. <definition>American means something from the United States of America.</definition>

Timmy was so happy. He put the pin on his shirt. He was proud to be a scout. He felt like a real adventurer. Being a member of the scouts was the best! He kept the pin safe in his pocket. He could not wait to tell his Mom.

<question>
Who was Timmy?
</question>
<answer>
Timmy was a member of the Cub Scouts.
</answer>

<question>

</question>

A member is someone who belongs to a group.

===== DOC START split=test idx=2029 id=story_qa_101129 =====
The little blue truck liked to drive. He liked to drive in the sun. He liked to drive in the rain. One day, he saw a turtle. The turtle was walking very slowly. He was being very deliberative. <definition>Deliberative means thinking very carefully about every step.</definition>

"Hello!" said the truck. "Do you need any help?"

The turtle looked up. "Yes, please!" he said. "I need to get by the big hill. It is too far for me to walk today."

The truck smiled. "Hop in!" he said.

The turtle climbed in the back. The truck drove slowly and carefully. He did not want to bump the turtle. They went up the hill. They went down the hill. Soon, they were by the turtle's house.

"Thank you!" said the turtle. "You are a good friend."

The truck smiled. He was happy to help. He drove off into the sunset.

<question>What color was the truck?</question>
<answer>The truck was blue.</answer>

<question>What did the truck like to do?</question>
<answer>The truck liked to drive.</answer>

<question>Who did the truck see one day?</question>
<answer>The truck saw a turtle.</answer>

<question>How was the turtle walking?</question>
<answer>The turtle was walking very slowly.</answer>

<question>What does deliberative mean?</question>
<answer>Deliberative means thinking very carefully about every step.</answer>

<question>What did the truck say to the turtle?</question>
<answer>Hello, do you need any help?</answer>

<question>Where did the turtle need to go?</question>
<answer>The turtle needed to get by the big hill.</answer>

<question>Did the turtle walk or ride in the truck?</question>
<answer>The turtle rode in the truck.</answer>

<question>How did the truck drive with the turtle?</question>
<answer>The truck drove slowly and carefully.</answer>

<question>What did the turtle say to the truck?</question>
<answer>Thank you, you are a good friend.</answer>

<question>Was the truck happy to help?</question>
<answer>Yes, the truck was happy to help.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=2030 id=story_qa_101080 =====
Pip was a little robot. He had one big job. He had to transmit messages. <definition>Transmit means to send something.</definition> Pip loved his job. He would stand very still. Then, beep boop! He would send a message.

One day, Pip had a new idea. He had an intent to make new friends. <definition>Intent means what you plan to do.</definition> He wanted to make them laugh. He thought, "I will transmit funny sounds!"

Pip tried to make a funny noise. But it did not work. Beep boop burp! It was not funny. He tried again. Squeak honk splat! It was still not funny.

Pip felt sad. He was not good at making funny sounds. Then, a little girl giggled. She said, "Those are silly sounds, Pip!" Pip smiled. He did make her laugh!

<question>What was Pip?</question>
<answer>Pip was a little robot.</answer>

<question>What was Pip's job?</question>
<answer>His job was to transmit messages.</answer>

<question>What does transmit mean?</question>
<answer>Transmit means to send something.</answer>

<question>Did Pip like his job?</question>
<answer>Yes, Pip loved his job.</answer>

<answer>He wanted to make new friends.</answer>

<answer>Intent means what you plan to do.</answer>

<question>What did Pip want to make his friends do?</question>
<answer>He wanted to make them laugh.</answer>

<question>What did Pip try to transmit?</question>
<answer>He tried to transmit funny sounds.</answer>

<answer>Beep boop burp.</answer>

<answer>No, he did not.</answer>

One day, Timmy was in the park. He saw a big, green ball. He wanted to play with it. The ball was near a tree. Timmy ran to the ball. He started to chase his tail again. Round and round he went. He got dizzy.

Timmy bumped in to the tree. The ball rolled away. Timmy stopped chasing his tail. He went to get the ball. Playing with the ball was more fun than chasing his tail today.

One day, Tom and Amy went to school. Everything was the same. <definition>Same means not different.</definition> The desks were the same. The teacher was the same. But something was different outside! The wind was blowing all the leaves off the trees!

"Wow!" said Tom. "Winter is coming!" Amy nodded. She liked winter too. Winter was a fun season. They both liked every season.

He drove to the park. He saw his friend, the red truck. Honk! The truck beeped back.

“Want to race?” asked the blue car.

“Yes!” said the red truck.

They raced around the park. The blue car went very fast. The other cars watched them. <definition>Other means not the one you are talking about.</definition> A yellow taxi cheered. A green bus smiled. Everyone liked to watch them race.

The blue car won! He honked his horn. Honk! Honk! He was a happy little car.

<question>Where did the Smith family go?</question>

<question>How do they feel about being there?</question>
<answer>They are so happy to be there.</answer>

<question>What are they using to swim?</question>
<answer>They are using noodles to swim.</answer>

<question>What shape are the noodles?</question>
<answer>The noodles are tubular.</answer>

<question>What does tubular mean?</question>
<answer>Tubular means shaped like a tube.</answer>

<question>Who showed them how to float?</question>
<answer>Dad showed them how to float.</answer>

<question>Who showed them how to kick?</question>
<answer>Mom showed them how to kick.</answer>

<question>Are the kids having fun?</question>
<answer>Yes, the kids are having fun.</answer>

<question>Who do they love swimming with?</question>
<answer>They love swimming with their family.</answer>

<question>Are they smiling?</question>
<answer>Yes, they are all smiling.</answer>

<question>Is the beach a fun place to be?</question>
<answer>Yes, the beach is a fun place to be.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=2042 id=story_qa_101704 =====
The little ghost was sad. He used to have many friends. Now, no one wanted to play. He stalked around the graveyard. <definition>Stalked means to follow quietly.</definition> He saw a funny box. It looked like a coffin. <definition>A coffin is a box people are put in when they die.</definition> He opened it. Phew! A bad gas smell came out. <definition>Gas is like air, but some gas smells bad.</definition> The gas made him float to the eastern side of the graveyard. He saw his friends! They were scared of the smell. He giggled. He closed the coffin. "Want to play?" he asked. They all said yes!

<question>How did the little ghost feel?</question>
<answer>The little ghost was sad.</answer>

<question>Did the ghost have friends?</question>
<answer>He used to have many friends.</answer>

<question>Where did the ghost walk?</question>
<answer>He walked around the graveyard.</answer>

<question>What does stalked mean?</question>
<answer>Stalked means to follow quietly.</answer>

<question>What did the ghost see in the graveyard?</question>
<answer>He saw a funny box.</answer>

<question>What did the box look like?</question>
<answer>It looked like a coffin.</answer>

<question>What is a coffin?</question>
<answer>A coffin is a box people are put in when they die.</answer>

<question>What happened when the ghost opened the box?</question>
<answer>A bad gas smell came out.</answer>

<question>What is gas?</question>
<answer>Gas is like air, but some gas smells bad.</answer>

<question>Where did the gas make him float?</question>
<answer>The gas made him float to the eastern side.</answer>

<question>Who did he see on the eastern side?</question>
<answer>He saw his friends.</answer>

<question>Why were his friends scared?</question>
<answer>They were scared of the smell.</answer>

<question>What did the ghost do when he saw his friends?</question>
<answer>He giggled.</answer>

<question>What did the ghost ask his friends?</question>
<answer>He asked want to play.</answer>

<question>Did his friends want to play?</question>
<answer>Yes, they all said yes.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=2043 id=story_qa_101723 =====
The little dog, Pat, loved to play. He liked to play by the big tree. Pat usually chased squirrels up the tree. One day, Pat saw a bug. The bug was very small. Pat did not like bugs! It hurt his sensibility. <definition>Sensibility means your feelings about things.</definition> He ran away from the bug. He went inside for a nap. Naps made Pat feel better. He dreamed of playing with his ball. The ball was not a bug.

<question>What did the little dog's name?</question>
<answer>The little dog's name was Pat.</answer>

<question>What did Pat love to do?</question>
<answer>Pat loved to play.</answer>

<question>Where did Pat like to play?</question>
<answer>Pat liked to play by the big tree.</answer>

<question>What did Pat usually chase?</question>
<answer>Pat usually chased squirrels.</answer>

<question>What did Pat see one day?</question>
<answer>Pat saw a bug one day.</answer>

<question>Was the bug big or small?</question>
<answer>The bug was very small.</answer>

<question>Did Pat like bugs?</question>
<answer>Pat did not like bugs.</answer>

<question>What does sensibility mean?</question>
<answer>Sensibility means your feelings about things.</answer>

<question>What did Pat do when he saw the bug?</question>
<answer>He ran away from the bug.</answer>

<question>Where did Pat go after he ran away?</question>
<answer>He went inside.</answer>

<question>What did Pat do inside?</question>
<answer>He took a nap.</answer>

<question>What made Pat feel better?</question>
<answer>Naps made Pat feel better.</answer>

<question>What did Pat dream about?</question>
<answer>He dreamed of playing with his ball.</answer>

<answer>The ball was not a bug.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=2044 id=story_qa_101741 =====
The ant was a member of his colony. <definition>A member is someone who belongs to a group.</definition> He methodically moved crumbs. <definition>Methodically means doing something carefully and in order.</definition>

<question>
What was the ant?
</question>
<answer>
The ant was a member of his colony.
</answer>

<question>
What is a member?
</question>
<answer>
A member is someone who belongs to a group.
</answer>

<question>
What did the ant move?
</question>
<answer>
The ant moved crumbs.
</answer>

<question>
How did the ant move the crumbs?
</question>
<answer>
The ant moved the crumbs methodically.
</answer>

<question>
What does methodically mean?
</question>
<answer>
Methodically means doing something carefully and in order.
</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=2045 id=story_qa_101728 =====
The ant was very small. He lived in a big ant hill. It was a common ant hill, like many others. <definition>Common means it is seen very often.</definition> But this ant hill had a boss ant. The boss ant was very authoritative. <definition>Authoritative means that the boss ant gives orders and everyone listens to him.</definition>

"Work, work!" he would shout.

All the small ants would carry food. They would carry leaves. The boss ant would watch them. He wanted them to work hard. One small ant found a big crumb. It was bigger than him!

He tried to lift it. He wiggled and pulled. He asked another ant for help. Together, they carried the crumb to the ant hill. The boss ant nodded. "Good work," he said.

<question>Was the ant big or small?</question>
<answer>The ant was small.</answer>

<question>Where did the ant live?</question>
<answer>The ant lived in a big ant hill.</answer>

<question>What does common mean?</question>
<answer>Common means it is seen very often.</answer>

<question>Who was in charge of the ant hill?</question>
<answer>The boss ant was in charge.</answer>

<question>What does authoritative mean?</question>
<answer>Authoritative means that the boss ant gives orders and everyone listens to him.</answer>

<question>What did the boss ant shout?</question>
<answer>He would shout "Work, work!"</answer>

<question>What did the small ants carry?</question>
<answer>They carried food and leaves.</answer>

<question>What did one small ant find?</question>
<answer>He found a big crumb.</answer>

<question>Was the crumb bigger or smaller than the ant?</question>
<answer>The crumb was bigger than the ant.</answer>

<question>Who helped the ant carry the crumb?</question>
<answer>Another ant helped him.</answer>

<question>What did the boss ant say?</question>
<answer>He said "Good work."</answer>
===== DOC END =====

===== DOC START split=test idx=2046 id=story_qa_101799 =====
The forest is green. Many animals are in the forest. Deer are in the forest. Some deer are small. Some deer are large. <definition>Large means big.</definition> A mazama is also in the forest. <definition>A mazama is a small type of deer.</definition> It is brown as a leaf. The mazama eats berries. The berries are yummy. The mazama runs fast. It runs and plays all day.

<question>What color is the forest?</question>
<answer>The forest is green.</answer>

<question>Are there animals in the forest?</question>
<answer>Yes, there are animals in the forest.</answer>

<question>Are deer in the forest?</question>
<answer>Yes, deer are in the forest.</answer>

<question>Are all the deer the same size?</question>
<answer>No, some deer are small and some are large.</answer>

<answer>Large means big.</answer>

<question>Is a mazama in the forest?</question>
<answer>Yes, a mazama is in the forest.</answer>

<question>What is a mazama?</question>
<answer>A mazama is a small type of deer.</answer>

<question>What color is the mazama?</question>
<answer>The mazama is brown.</answer>

<question>What does the mazama eat?</question>
<answer>The mazama eats berries.</answer>

<answer>Yes, the berries are yummy.</answer>

===== DOC START split=test idx=2047 id=story_qa_101835 =====
The pig had a problem. He had too many apples! He was dealing with a big pile. <definition>Dealing means taking action to solve a problem.</definition> What could he do?

He asked the cow, "Do you want any apples?" The cow shook her head. "No, thank you," she said.

He asked the horse, "Do you want any apples?" The horse said, "I like hay more."

The pig was sad. Then he had an idea! He made applesauce. He gave applesauce to all his friends. They loved it! Now the pig did not have any extra apples. He was very happy. He liked dealing with his apple problem. It was fun!

<question>What problem did the pig have?</question>
<answer>He had too many apples.</answer>

<question>What does dealing mean?</question>
<answer>Dealing means taking action to solve a problem.</answer>

<question>Who did the pig ask first?</question>
<answer>He asked the cow first.</answer>

<question>Did the cow want any apples?</question>
<answer>No, the cow did not want any apples.</answer>

<question>Who did the pig ask second?</question>
<answer>He asked the horse second.</answer>

<question>What did the horse like more than apples?</question>
<answer>The horse liked hay more.</answer>

<question>What did the pig make?</question>
<answer>He made applesauce.</answer>

<question>Who did the pig give the applesauce to?</question>
<answer>He gave applesauce to all his friends.</answer>

<question>Did his friends like the applesauce?</question>
<answer>Yes, they loved it.</answer>

<question>Was the pig happy at the end?</question>
<answer>Yes, the pig was very happy.</answer>
===== DOC END =====
